text.NRSV: The vision of Isaiah son of Amoz, which he saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah.
text.Rotherham: The vision of Isaiah son of Amoz, which he saw, concerning Judah and Jerusalem,—in the days of Uzziah Jotham Ahaz Hezekiah—kings of Judah.
text.DRB: The vision of Isaias the son of Amos, which he saw concerning Juda and Jerusalem in the days of Ozias, Joathan, Achaz, and Ezechias, kings of Juda.
text.KJV: 1:1 The Uision of Isaiah the sonne of Amoz, which hee sawe concerning Iudah and Ierusalem, in the dayes of Uzziah, Iotham, Ahaz, & Hezekiah kings of Iudah.
text.NABRE: I. Isaiah 1—39 A. Indictment of Israel and Judah Chapter 1 The vision which Isaiah, son of Amoz, saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz and Hezekiah, kings of Judah.

text.NRSV: Hear, O heavens, and listen, O earth; for the Lord has spoken: I reared children and brought them up, but they have rebelled against me.
text.Rotherham: Hear O heavens, And give ear, O earth, for Yahweh, hath spoken:—Sons, have I brought up, and advanced, And, they, have rebelled against me.
text.DRB: Hear, O ye heavens, and give ear, O earth, for the Lord hath spoken. I have brought up children, and exalted them: but they have despised me.
text.KJV: 1:2 Heare, O heauens, and giue eare, O earth: for the Lord hath spoken; I haue nourished and brought vp children, and they haue rebelled against me.
text.NABRE: Accusation and Appeal Hear, O heavens, and listen, O earth, for the Lord speaks: Sons have I raised and reared, but they have rebelled against me!

text.NRSV: The ox knows its owner, and the donkey its master’s crib; but Israel does not know, my people do not understand.
text.Rotherham: An ox knoweth, his owner, And an ass his masters crib,—Israel, doth not know, My people doth not consider.
text.DRB: The ox knoweth his owner, and the ass his master's crib: but Israel hath not known me, and my people hath not understood.
text.KJV: 1:3 The oxe knoweth his owner, and the asse his masters cribbe: but Israel doeth not know, my people doeth not consider.
text.NABRE: An ox knows its owner, and an ass, its master’s manger; But Israel does not know, my people has not understood.

text.NRSV: Ah, sinful nation, people laden with iniquity, offspring who do evil, children who deal corruptly, who have forsaken the Lord, who have despised the Holy One of Israel, who are utterly estranged!
text.Rotherham: Alas! a nation—committing sin, a people—burdened with iniquity, a seed—practising wickedness, sons—acting corruptly. They have forsaken Yahweh despised the Holy One of Israel,—Are estranged and gone back.
text.DRB: Woe to the sinful nation, a people laden with iniquity, a wicked seed, ungracious children: they have forsaken the Lord, they have blasphemed the Holy One of Israel, they are gone away backwards.
text.KJV: 1:4 Ah sinnefull nation, a people laden with iniquitie, a seede of euill doers, children that are corrupters: they haue forsaken the Lord, they haue prouoked the Holy one of Israel vnto anger, they are gone away backward.
text.NABRE: Ah! Sinful nation, people laden with wickedness, evil offspring, corrupt children! They have forsaken the Lord, spurned the Holy One of Israel, apostatized,

text.NRSV: Why do you seek further beatings? Why do you continue to rebel? The whole head is sick, and the whole heart faint.
text.Rotherham: Why should ye be smitten any more? Ye would again turn aside! The whole head is sick, And the whole heart faint:
text.DRB: For what shall I strike you any more, you that increase transgression? the whole head is sick, and the whole heart is sad.
text.KJV: 1:5 ¶ Why should yee be stricken any more? yee will reuolt more and more: the whole head is sicke, and the whole heart faint.
text.NABRE: Why would you yet be struck, that you continue to rebel? The whole head is sick, the whole heart faint.

text.NRSV: From the sole of the foot even to the head, there is no soundness in it, but bruises and sores and bleeding wounds; they have not been drained, or bound up, or softened with oil.
text.Rotherham: From the sole of the foot even unto the head, there is in it no soundness, Bruise and stripe and newly-made wound,—They have not been pressed out, nor bound up, nor soothed with oil.
text.DRB: From the sole of the foot unto the top of the head, there is no soundness therein: wounds and bruises and swelling sores: they are not bound up, nor dressed, nor fomented with oil.
text.KJV: 1:6 From the sole of the foote, euen vnto the head, there is no soundnesse in it; but wounds, and bruises, and putrifying sores: they haue not beene closed, neither bound vp, neither mollified with oyntment.
text.NABRE: From the sole of the foot to the head there is no sound spot in it; Just bruise and welt and oozing wound, not drained, or bandaged, or eased with salve.

text.NRSV: Your country lies desolate, your cities are burned with fire; in your very presence aliens devour your land; it is desolate, as overthrown by foreigners.
text.Rotherham: Your country—is a desolation, Your cities—are consumed with fire,—Your soil—right before your eyes, foreigners are devouring it, And it is a desolation a very overthrow by foreigners;
text.DRB: Your land is desolate, your cities are burnt with fire: your country strangers devour before your face, and it shall be desolate as when wasted by enemies.
text.KJV: 1:7 Your countrey is desolate, your cities are burnt with fire: your land, strangers deuoure it in your presence, and it is desolate as ouerthrowen by strangers.
text.NABRE: Your country is waste, your cities burnt with fire; Your land—before your eyes strangers devour it, a waste, like the devastation of Sodom.

text.NRSV: And daughter Zion is left like a booth in a vineyard, like a shelter in a cucumber field, like a besieged city.
text.Rotherham: And left is the Daughter of Zion, Like a hut in a vineyard,—Like a lodge in a gourd-plot Like a city besieged.
text.DRB: And the daughter of Sion shall be left as a covert in a vineyard, and as a lodge in a garden of cucumbers, and as a city that is laid waste.
text.KJV: 1:8 And the daughter of Zion is left as a cottage in a vineyard, as a lodge in a garden of cucumbers, as a besieged citie.
text.NABRE: And daughter Zion is left like a hut in a vineyard, Like a shed in a melon patch, like a city blockaded.

text.NRSV: If the Lord of hosts had not left us a few survivors, we would have been like Sodom, and become like Gomorrah.
text.Rotherham: If, Yahweh of hosts, had not left us a very small remnant, Like Sodom, had we become, Gomorrah, had we resembled.
text.DRB: Except the Lord of hosts had left us seed, we had been as Sodom, and we should have been like to Gomorrha.
text.KJV: 1:9 Except the Lord of hostes had left vnto vs a very small remnant, we should haue beene as Sodom, and we should haue bene like vnto Gomorrah.
text.NABRE: If the Lord of hosts had not left us a small remnant, We would have become as Sodom, would have resembled Gomorrah.

text.NRSV: Hear the word of the Lord, you rulers of Sodom! Listen to the teaching of our God, you people of Gomorrah!
text.Rotherham: Hear the word of Yahweh, ye rulers of Sodom,—Give ear to the instruction of our God, ye people of Gomorrah:—
text.DRB: Hear the word of the Lord, ye rulers of Sodom, give ear to the law of our God, ye people of Gomorrha.
text.KJV: 1:10 ¶ Heare the word of the Lord, ye rulers of Sodom, giue eare vnto the Law of our God, yee people of Gomorrah.
text.NABRE: Hear the word of the Lord, princes of Sodom! Listen to the instruction of our God, people of Gomorrah!

text.NRSV: What to me is the multitude of your sacrifices? says the Lord; I have had enough of burnt offerings of rams and the fat of fed beasts; I do not delight in the blood of bulls, or of lambs, or of goats.
text.Rotherham: Of what use to me, is your multitude of sacrifices: Saith Yahweh: I am sated with ascending-offerings of rams and the fat of fed beasts,—In the blood of bulls and young rams and he-goats, have I no pleasure.
text.DRB: To what purpose do you offer me the multitude of your victims, saith the Lord? I am full, I desire not holocausts of rams, and fat of fatlings, and blood of calves, and lambs, and buck goats.
text.KJV: 1:11 To what purpose is the multitude of your sacrifices vnto me, sayth the Lord? I am full of the burnt offerings of rammes, and the fat of fedde beasts, and I delight not in the blood of bullockes, or of lambes, or of hee goates.
text.NABRE: What do I care for the multitude of your sacrifices? says the Lord. I have had enough of whole-burnt rams and fat of fatlings; In the blood of calves, lambs, and goats I find no pleasure.

text.NRSV: When you come to appear before me, who asked this from you? Trample my courts no more;
text.Rotherham: When ye enter to see my face, Who hath required this at your hand trampling my courts?
text.DRB: When you came to appear before me, who required these things at your hands, that you should walk in my courts?
text.KJV: 1:12 When ye come to appeare before mee, who hath required this at your hand, to tread my courts?
text.NABRE: When you come to appear before me, who asks these things of you?

text.NRSV: bringing offerings is futile; incense is an abomination to me. New moon and sabbath and calling of convocation— I cannot endure solemn assemblies with iniquity.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not again bring in an empty, present, Incense, an abomination, is that unto me! New moon, and sabbath calling an assembly—I cannot endure, iniquity and sacred festival!
text.DRB: Offer sacrifice no more in vain: incense is an abomination to me. The new moons, and the sabbaths, and other festivals I will not abide, your assemblies are wicked.
text.KJV: 1:13 Bring no more vaine oblations, incense is an abomination vnto me: the new Moones, and Sabbaths, the calling of assemblies I cannot away with; it is iniquitie, euen the solemne meeting.
text.NABRE: Trample my courts no more! To bring offerings is useless; incense is an abomination to me. New moon and sabbath, calling assemblies— festive convocations with wickedness— these I cannot bear.

text.NRSV: Your new moons and your appointed festivals my soul hates; they have become a burden to me, I am weary of bearing them.
text.Rotherham: Your new moons and your appointed feasts, my soul, hateth,—They have become unto me a burden I am too weary to bear:
text.DRB: My soul hateth your new moons, and your solemnities: they are become troublesome to me, I am weary of bearing them.
text.KJV: 1:14 Your new Moones, and your appointed Feasts my soule hateth: they are a trouble vnto me, I am weary to beare them.
text.NABRE: Your new moons and festivals I detest; they weigh me down, I tire of the load.

text.NRSV: When you stretch out your hands, I will hide my eyes from you; even though you make many prayers, I will not listen; your hands are full of blood.
text.Rotherham: Even when ye spread forth your open palms, I hide mine eyes from you; Yea, though ye multiply prayers, I am not hearkening,—Your hands, with deeds of blood, are filled.
text.DRB: And when you stretch forth your hands, I will turn away my eyes from you: and when you multiply prayer, I will not hear: for your hands are full of blood.
text.KJV: 1:15 And when ye spread foorth your handes, I will hide mine eyes from you; yea, when yee make many prayers I will not heare: your hands are full of blood.
text.NABRE: When you spread out your hands, I will close my eyes to you; Though you pray the more, I will not listen. Your hands are full of blood!

text.NRSV: Wash yourselves; make yourselves clean; remove the evil of your doings from before my eyes; cease to do evil,
text.Rotherham: Wash you make you clean, Put away the wickedness of your doings from before mine eyes,—Cease to do evil.
text.DRB: Wash yourselves, be clean, take away the evil of your devices from my eyes: cease to do perversely,
text.KJV: 1:16 ¶ Wash yee, make you cleane, put away the euill of your doings from before mine eyes, cease to doe euill,
text.NABRE: Wash yourselves clean! Put away your misdeeds from before my eyes; cease doing evil;

text.NRSV: learn to do good; seek justice, rescue the oppressed, defend the orphan, plead for the widow.
text.Rotherham: Learn to do well—Seek justice, Correct the oppressor,—Vindicate the fatherless, Plead the cause of the widow,
text.DRB: Learn to do well: seek judgment, relieve the oppressed, judge for the fatherless, defend the widow.
text.KJV: 1:17 Learne to doe well, seeke iudgement, relieue the oppressed, iudge the fatherlesse, plead for the widow.
text.NABRE: learn to do good. Make justice your aim: redress the wronged, hear the orphan’s plea, defend the widow.

text.NRSV: Come now, let us argue it out, says the Lord: though your sins are like scarlet, they shall be like snow; though they are red like crimson, they shall become like wool.
text.Rotherham: Come, I pray you, and let us settle the dispute Saith Yahweh,—Though your sins be found like scarlet, As snow, shall they be made white, Though they appear red like crimson, As wool, shall they become.
text.DRB: And then come, and accuse me, saith the Lord: if your sins be as scarlet, they shall be made as white as snow: and if they be red as crimson, they shall be white as wool.
text.KJV: 1:18 Come now and let vs reason together, saith the Lord: though your sinnes be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow; though they be red like crimsin, they shall be as wooll.
text.NABRE: Come now, let us set things right, says the Lord: Though your sins be like scarlet, they may become white as snow; Though they be red like crimson, they may become white as wool.

text.NRSV: If you are willing and obedient, you shall eat the good of the land;
text.Rotherham: If ye be willing—and hearken Of the good of the land, shall ye eat;
text.DRB: If you be willing, and will hearken to me, you shall eat the good things of the land.
text.KJV: 1:19 If yee be willing and obedient, yee shall eate the good of the land.
text.NABRE: If you are willing, and obey, you shall eat the good things of the land;

text.NRSV: but if you refuse and rebel, you shall be devoured by the sword; for the mouth of the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: But, if ye refuse and rebel, With the sword, shall ye be devoured, For, the mouth of Yahweh, hath spoken it.
text.DRB: But if you will not, and will provoke me to wrath: the sword shall devour you because the mouth of the Lord hath spoken it.
text.KJV: 1:20 But if yee refuse and rebell, yee shalbe deuoured with the sword: for the mouth of the Lord hath spoken it.
text.NABRE: But if you refuse and resist, you shall be eaten by the sword: for the mouth of the Lord has spoken!

text.NRSV: How the faithful city has become a whore! She that was full of justice, righteousness lodged in her— but now murderers!
text.Rotherham: How hath she become unchaste!—The city that was Faithful,—Full of justice, Righteousness lodged in her, But, now, murderers!
text.DRB: How is the faithful city, that was full of judgment, become a harlot? justice dwelt in it, but now murderers.
text.KJV: 1:21 ¶ Howe is the faithfull citie become an harlot? it was full of iudgement, righteousnesse lodged in it; but now murtherers.
text.NABRE: The Purification of Jerusalem How she has become a prostitute, the faithful city, so upright! Justice used to lodge within her, but now, murderers.

text.NRSV: Your silver has become dross, your wine is mixed with water.
text.Rotherham: Thy silver, hath become dross,—Thy wine, weakened with water;
text.DRB: Thy silver is turned into dross: thy wine is mingled with water.
text.KJV: 1:22 Thy siluer is become drosse, thy wine mixt with water.
text.NABRE: Your silver is turned to dross, your wine is mixed with water.

text.NRSV: Your princes are rebels and companions of thieves. Everyone loves a bribe and runs after gifts. They do not defend the orphan, and the widow’s cause does not come before them.
text.Rotherham: Thy rulers, are unruly, and companions of thieves, Every one of the people, loveth a bribe, and runneth after rewards,—The fatherless, they do not vindicate, And, the plea of the widow, reacheth them not.
text.DRB: Thy princes are faithless, companions of thieves: they all love bribes, they run after rewards. They judge not for the fatherless: and the widow's cause cometh not in to them.
text.KJV: 1:23 Thy princes are rebellious and companions of theeues: euery one loueth gifts, and followeth after rewards: they iudge not the fatherlesse, neither doth the cause of the widowe come vnto them.
text.NABRE: Your princes are rebels and comrades of thieves; Each one of them loves a bribe and looks for gifts. The fatherless they do not defend, the widow’s plea does not reach them.

text.NRSV: Therefore says the Lord, the Lord of hosts, the Mighty One of Israel: Ah, I will pour out my wrath on my enemies, and avenge myself on my foes!
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Declareth the Lord Yahweh of hosts, The Mighty One of Israel,—Alas! I must appease me on mine adversaries, I must avenge me on mine enemies
text.DRB: Therefore saith the Lord the God of hosts, the mighty one of Israel: Ah! I will comfort myself over my adversaries: and I will be revenged of my enemies.
text.KJV: 1:24 Therefore, saith the Lord, the Lord of hostes, the mighty one of Israel; Ah, I will ease me of mine aduersaries, and auenge me of mine enemies.
text.NABRE: Now, therefore, says the Lord, the Lord of hosts, the Mighty One of Israel: Ah! I will take vengeance on my foes and fully repay my enemies!

text.NRSV: I will turn my hand against you; I will smelt away your dross as with lye and remove all your alloy.
text.Rotherham: That I may turn my hand against thee, And smelt away, as with potash, thy dross, And remove all thine alloy;
text.DRB: And I will turn my hand to thee, and I will clean purge away thy dross, and I will take away all thy tin.
text.KJV: 1:25 ¶ And I will turne my hand vpon thee, and purely purge away thy drosse, and take away all thy tinne.
text.NABRE: I will turn my hand against you, and refine your dross in the furnace, removing all your alloy.

text.NRSV: And I will restore your judges as at the first, and your counselors as at the beginning. Afterward you shall be called the city of righteousness, the faithful city.
text.Rotherham: That I may restore thy Judges as at the first, and thy Counsellors as at the beginning,—After that, shalt thou be called Righteous citadel, Trusty city,—
text.DRB: And I will restore thy judges as they were before, and thy counsellors as of old. After this thou shalt be called the city of the just, a faithful city.
text.KJV: 1:26 And I will restore thy iudges as at the first, and thy counsellers as at the beginning: afterward thou shalt be called the citie of righteousnesse, the faithfull citie.
text.NABRE: I will restore your judges as at first, and your counselors as in the beginning; After that you shall be called city of justice, faithful city.

text.NRSV: Zion shall be redeemed by justice, and those in her who repent, by righteousness.
text.Rotherham: Zion, with justice shall be redeemed,—And her returning ones, with righteousness;
text.DRB: Sion shall be redeemed in judgment, and they shall bring her back in justice.
text.KJV: 1:27 Zion shall be redeemed with iudgement, and her conuerts with righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: Zion shall be redeemed by justice, and her repentant ones by righteousness.

text.NRSV: But rebels and sinners shall be destroyed together, and those who forsake the Lord shall be consumed.
text.Rotherham: And the downfall of transgressors and sinners, shall be, together,—And, they who forsake Yahweh, shall be brought to an end;
text.DRB: And he shall destroy the wicked, and the sinners together: and they that have forsaken the Lord, shall be consumed.
text.KJV: 1:28 ¶ And the destruction of the transgressours and of the sinners shall be together: and they that forsake the Lord shall be consumed.
text.NABRE: Rebels and sinners together shall be crushed, those who desert the Lord shall be consumed.

text.NRSV: For you shall be ashamed of the oaks in which you delighted; and you shall blush for the gardens that you have chosen.
text.Rotherham: For they shall turn pale on account of the oaks which ye desired,—And ye shall blush on account of the gardens which ye had chosen;
text.DRB: For they shall be confounded for the idols, to which they have sacrificed: and you shall be ashamed of the gardens which you have chosen.
text.KJV: 1:29 For they shall be ashamed of the okes which yee haue desired, and yee shalbe confounded for the gardens that yee haue chosen.
text.NABRE: Judgment on the Sacred Groves You shall be ashamed of the terebinths which you desired, and blush on account of the gardens which you chose.

text.NRSV: For you shall be like an oak whose leaf withers, and like a garden without water.
text.Rotherham: For ye shall be as an oak with its leaf faded, And as a garden that hath no, water;
text.DRB: When you shall be as an oak with the leaves falling off, and as a garden without water.
text.KJV: 1:30 For yee shall be as an oke whose leafe fadeth, and as a garden that hath no water.
text.NABRE: You shall become like a terebinth whose leaves wither, like a garden that has no water.

text.NRSV: The strong shall become like tinder, and their work like a spark; they and their work shall burn together, with no one to quench them.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the strong one become tow, And his work a spark,—And they shall both blaze together And there be none to quench the fire.
text.DRB: And your strength shall be as the ashes of tow, and your work as a spark: and both shall burn together, and there shall be none to quench it.
text.KJV: 1:31 And the strong shall be as towe, and the maker of it as a sparke, and they shall both burne together, and none shall quench them.
text.NABRE: The strong tree shall turn to tinder, and the one who tends it shall become a spark; Both of them shall burn together, and there shall be none to quench them.

text.NRSV: The word that Isaiah son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: That which Isaiah, son of Amoz saw in vision,—concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
text.DRB: The word that Isaias the son of Amos saw, concerning Juda and Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 2:1 The word that Isaiah, the sonne of Amoz, sawe concerning Iudah and Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 This is what Isaiah, son of Amoz, saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: In days to come the mountain of the Lord’s house shall be established as the highest of the mountains, and shall be raised above the hills; all the nations shall stream to it.
text.Rotherham: But it shall come to pass, in the afterpart of the days, That the mountain of the house of Yahweh Shall be, set up, as the head of the mountains, And be exalted above the hills,—And all the nations, shall stream thereunto;
text.DRB: And in the last days the mountain of the house of the Lord shall be prepared on the top of mountains , and it shall be exalted above the hills, and all nations shall flow unto it.
text.KJV: 2:2 And it shall come to passe in the last dayes, that the mountaine of the Lords house shall be established in the top of the mountaines, and shall be exalted aboue the hilles; and all nations shall flow vnto it.
text.NABRE: Zion, the Royal City of God In days to come, The mountain of the Lord ’s house shall be established as the highest mountain and raised above the hills. All nations shall stream toward it.

text.NRSV: Many peoples shall come and say, “Come, let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, to the house of the God of Jacob; that he may teach us his ways and that we may walk in his paths.” For out of Zion shall go forth instruction, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And many peoples shall go and say—Come ye, and let us ascend Unto the mountain of Yahweh Unto the house of the God of Jacob, That he may teach us of his ways, And we may walk in his paths,—For, out of Zion, shall go forth a law, And the word of Yahweh out of Jerusalem;
text.DRB: And many people shall go, and say: Come and let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, and to the house of the God of Jacob, and he will teach us his ways, and we will walk in his paths: for the law shall come forth from Sion, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 2:3 And many people shall goe & say; Come yee and let vs go vp to the mountaine of the Lord, to the house of the God of Iacob, and he will teach vs of his wayes, and we will walke in his pathes: for out of Zion shall goe forth the lawe, and the word of the Lord from Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: Many peoples shall come and say: “Come, let us go up to the Lord ’s mountain, to the house of the God of Jacob, That he may instruct us in his ways, and we may walk in his paths.” For from Zion shall go forth instruction, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: He shall judge between the nations, and shall arbitrate for many peoples; they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning hooks; nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.
text.Rotherham: And he will judge between the nations, And be umpire to many peoples,—And they will beat their swords into ploughshares and their spears into pruning-hooks, Nation—against nation, shall not lift up sword, Neither shall they learn any more to make war,
text.DRB: And he shall judge the Gentiles, and rebuke many people: and they shall turn their swords into ploughshares, and their spears into sickles: nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they be exercised any more to war.
text.KJV: 2:4 And hee shall iudge among the nations, and shall rebuke many people: and they shall beate their swords into plow-shares, and their speares into pruning hookes: nation shall not lift vp sword against nation, neither shall they learne warre any more.
text.NABRE: He shall judge between the nations, and set terms for many peoples. They shall beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning hooks; One nation shall not raise the sword against another, nor shall they train for war again.

text.NRSV: O house of Jacob, come, let us walk in the light of the Lord!
text.Rotherham: O house of Jacob! come ye and let us walk in the light of Yahweh.
text.DRB: O house of Jacob, come ye, and let us walk in the light of the Lord.
text.KJV: 2:5 O house of Iacob, come yee, and let vs walke in the light of the Lord.
text.NABRE: House of Jacob, come, let us walk in the light of the Lord!

text.NRSV: For you have forsaken the ways of your people, the house of Jacob. For they are full of diviners from the east and of soothsayers like the Philistines, and they clasp hands with foreigners.
text.Rotherham: Therefore hast thou abandoned thy people the house of Jacob, Because they have become full of the East, And use hidden arts like the Philistines,—And with the children of foreigners, strike hands;—
text.DRB: For thou hast cast off thy people, the house of Jacob: because they are filled as in times past, and have had soothsayers as the Philistines, and have adhered to strange children.
text.KJV: 2:6 ¶ Therefore thou hast forsaken thy people the house of Iacob; because they be replenished from the East, and are soothsayers like the Philistines, and they please themselues in the children of strangers.
text.NABRE: The Lord’s Day of Judgment on Pride You have abandoned your people, the house of Jacob! Because they are filled with diviners, and soothsayers, like the Philistines; with foreigners they clasp hands.

text.NRSV: Their land is filled with silver and gold, and there is no end to their treasures; their land is filled with horses, and there is no end to their chariots.
text.Rotherham: So that filled is their land with silver and gold, And there is no end to their treasures,—And filled is their land with horses, And there is no end to their chariots;
text.DRB: Their land is filled with silver and gold: and there is no end of their treasures.
text.KJV: 2:7 Their land also is full of siluer and gold, neither is there any end of their treasures: their land is also full of horses; neither is there any end of their charets.
text.NABRE: Their land is full of silver and gold, there is no end to their treasures; Their land is full of horses, there is no end to their chariots.

text.NRSV: Their land is filled with idols; they bow down to the work of their hands, to what their own fingers have made.
text.Rotherham: And filled is their land with idols,—To the work of their own hands, do they bow themselves down, To that which they made with their own fingers.
text.DRB: And their land is filled with horses: and their chariots are innumerable. Their land also is full of idols: they have adored the work of their own hands, which their own fingers have made.
text.KJV: 2:8 Their land also is full of idoles: they worship the worke of their owne hands, that which their owne fingers haue made.
text.NABRE: Their land is full of idols; they bow down to the works of their hands, what their fingers have made.

text.NRSV: And so people are humbled, and everyone is brought low— do not forgive them!
text.Rotherham: So the mean man boweth down And the great man stoopeth low,—Therefore do not thou forgive them!
text.DRB: And man hath bowed himself down, and man hath been debased: therefore forgive them not.
text.KJV: 2:9 And the meane man boweth downe, and the great man humbleth himselfe; therefore forgiue them not.
text.NABRE: So all shall be abased, each one brought low. Do not pardon them!

text.NRSV: Enter into the rock, and hide in the dust from the terror of the Lord, and from the glory of his majesty.
text.Rotherham: Enter into the rock, Or hide thee in the dust,—Because of the terribleness of Yahweh, And for his majestic splendour.
text.DRB: Enter thou into the rock, and hide thee in the pit from the face of the fear of the Lord, and from the glory of his majesty.
text.KJV: 2:10 ¶ Enter into the rocke, and hide thee in the dust, for feare of the Lord, and for the glory of his Maiestie.
text.NABRE: Get behind the rocks, hide in the dust, From the terror of the Lord and the splendor of his majesty!

text.NRSV: The haughty eyes of people shall be brought low, and the pride of everyone shall be humbled; and the Lord alone will be exalted in that day.
text.Rotherham: The lofty looks of mean men, shall be humbled, And, the haughtiness of great men, shall be bowed down,—And Yahweh alone shall be exalted in that day.
text.DRB: The lofty eyes of man are humbled, and the haughtiness of men shall be made to stoop: and the Lord alone shall be exalted in that day.
text.KJV: 2:11 The loftie lookes of man shalbe humbled, and the hautines of men shalbe bowed downe: and the Lord alone shalbe exalted in that day.
text.NABRE: The eyes of human pride shall be lowered, the arrogance of mortals shall be abased, and the Lord alone will be exalted, on that day.

text.NRSV: For the Lord of hosts has a day against all that is proud and lofty, against all that is lifted up and high;
text.Rotherham: For, a day of Yahweh of hosts, [shall be] —Upon every one who is high and lofty,—And upon every one who is lifted up, And he shall be brought low;
text.DRB: Because the day of the Lord of hosts shall be upon every one that is proud and highminded, and upon every one that is arrogant, and he shall be humbled.
text.KJV: 2:12 For the day of the Lord of hostes shall bee vpon euery one that is proud and loftie, and vpon euery one that is lifted vp, and he shalbe brought low;
text.NABRE: For the Lord of hosts will have his day against all that is proud and arrogant, against all that is high, and it will be brought low;

text.NRSV: against all the cedars of Lebanon, lofty and lifted up; and against all the oaks of Bashan;
text.Rotherham: And upon all cedars of Lebanon, that are high and lifted up,—And upon all the oaks of Bashan;
text.DRB: And upon all the tall and lofty cedars of Libanus, and upon all the oaks of Basan.
text.KJV: 2:13 And vpon all the Cedars of Lebanon, that are high and lifted vp; and vpon all the okes of Bashan,
text.NABRE: Yes, against all the cedars of Lebanon and against all the oaks of Bashan,

text.NRSV: against all the high mountains, and against all the lofty hills;
text.Rotherham: And upon all the lofty mountains,—And upon all the uplifted hills;
text.DRB: And upon all the high mountains, and upon all the elevated hills.
text.KJV: 2:14 And vpon all the high mountaines, and vpon all the hilles that are lifted vp,
text.NABRE: Against all the lofty mountains and all the high hills,

text.NRSV: against every high tower, and against every fortified wall;
text.Rotherham: And upon every high tower,—And upon every fortress wall;
text.DRB: And upon every high tower, and every fenced wall.
text.KJV: 2:15 And vpon euery high tower, and vpon euery fenced wall,
text.NABRE: Against every lofty tower and every fortified wall,

text.NRSV: against all the ships of Tarshish, and against all the beautiful craft.
text.Rotherham: And upon all the ships of Tarshish,—And upon all desirable banners.
text.DRB: And upon all the ships of Tharsis, and upon all that is fair to behold.
text.KJV: 2:16 And vpon all the ships of Tarshish, and vpon all pleasant pictures.
text.NABRE: Against all the ships of Tarshish and all stately vessels.

text.NRSV: The haughtiness of people shall be humbled, and the pride of everyone shall be brought low; and the Lord alone will be exalted on that day.
text.Rotherham: And the haughtiness of mean men, shall be humbled,—And the loftiness of great men, shall be laid low,—And Yahweh alone shall be exalted in that day.
text.DRB: And the loftiness of men shall be bowed down, and the haughtiness of men shall be humbled, and the Lord alone shall be exalted in that day.
text.KJV: 2:17 And the loftinesse of man shall be bowed downe, and the hautinesse of men shalbe made low: and the Lord alone shalbe exalted in that day.
text.NABRE: Then human pride shall be abased, the arrogance of mortals brought low, And the Lord alone will be exalted on that day.

text.NRSV: The idols shall utterly pass away.
text.Rotherham: And, the idols, shall wholly pass away;
text.DRB: And idols shall be utterly destroyed .
text.KJV: 2:18 And the idoles hee shall vtterly abolish.
text.NABRE: The idols will vanish completely.

text.NRSV: Enter into the caves of the rocks and the holes of the ground, from the terror of the Lord, and from the glory of his majesty, when he rises to terrify the earth.
text.Rotherham: And they shall enter into the holes of the rocks, and into the caves of clay,—Because of the terribleness of Yahweh, And for his majestic splendour, When he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
text.DRB: And they shall go into the holes of rocks, and into the caves of the earth from the face of the fear of the Lord, and from the glory of his majesty, when he shall rise up to strike the earth.
text.KJV: 2:19 And they shall goe into the holes of the rocks, and into the caues of the earth for feare of the Lord, and for the glory of his Maiestie; when hee ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
text.NABRE: People will go into caves in the rocks and into holes in the earth, At the terror of the Lord and the splendor of his majesty, as he rises to overawe the earth.

text.NRSV: On that day people shall throw out to the moles and to the bats their idols of silver and their idols of gold, which they made for themselves to worship,
text.Rotherham: In that day shall the son of earth cast his idols of silver, and his idols of gold,—which had been made for him to worship, into the hole of the mice, and to the bats;
text.DRB: In that day a man shall cast away his idols of silver, and his idols of gold, which he had made for himself to adore, moles and bats.
text.KJV: 2:20 In that day a man shall cast his idoles of siluer, and his idoles of golde which they made each one for himselfe to worship, to the moules and to the battes:
text.NABRE: On that day people shall throw to moles and bats their idols of silver and their idols of gold which they made for themselves to worship.

text.NRSV: to enter the caverns of the rocks and the clefts in the cliffs, from the terror of the Lord, and from the glory of his majesty, when he rises to terrify the earth.
text.Rotherham: That he may enter into the clefts of the rocks, and into the fissures of the crags,—Because of the terribleness of Yahweh, And for his majestic splendour, When he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
text.DRB: And he shall go into the clefts of rocks, and into the holes of stones from the face of the fear of the Lord, and from the glory of his majesty, when he shall rise up to strike the earth.
text.KJV: 2:21 To go into the clefts of the rocks, and into the tops of the ragged rockes, for feare of the Lord, and for the glorie of his Maiestie; when hee ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
text.NABRE: And they shall go into caverns in the rocks and into crevices in the cliffs, At the terror of the Lord and the splendor of his majesty, as he rises to overawe the earth.

text.NRSV: Turn away from mortals, who have only breath in their nostrils, for of what account are they?
text.Rotherham: Cease ye from the son of earth, In whose nostrils is but a breath,—For wherein to be reckoned upon, is he?
text.DRB: Cease ye therefore from the man, whose breath is in his nostrils, for he is reputed high.
text.KJV: 2:22 Cease ye from man whose breath is in his nostrels: for wherein is hee to be accounted of?
text.NABRE: As for you, stop worrying about mortals, in whose nostrils is but a breath; for of what worth are they?

text.NRSV: For now the Lord, the Lord of hosts, is taking away from Jerusalem and from Judah support and staff— all support of bread, and all support of water;
text.Rotherham: For lo! the Lord, Yahweh of hosts, removing from Jerusalem and from Judah, The stay and staff,—The whole stay of bread, And the whole stay of water:
text.DRB: For behold the sovereign the Lord of hosts shall take away from Jerusalem, and from Juda the valiant and the strong, the whole strength of bread, and the whole strength of water.
text.KJV: 3:1 For behold, the Lord, the Lord of hostes doeth take away from Ierusalem, and from Iudah, the stay and the staffe, the whole stay of bread, and the whole stay of water,
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 Judgment on Jerusalem and Judah The Lord, the Lord of hosts, will take away from Jerusalem and from Judah Support and staff— all support of bread, all support of water:

text.NRSV: mighty warrior and soldier, judge and prophet, diviner and elder,
text.Rotherham: Man of might and man of war,—Judge and prophet And diviner and elder;
text.DRB: The strong man, and the man of war, the judge, and the prophet, and the cunning man, and the ancient.
text.KJV: 3:2 The mighty man, and the man of warre; the Iudge and the Prophet, and the prudent, and the ancient,
text.NABRE: Hero and warrior, judge and prophet, diviner and elder,

text.NRSV: captain of fifty and dignitary, counselor and skillful magician and expert enchanter.
text.Rotherham: Captain of fifty, and favourite; And counsellor and skilled artificer, And master of magical formulas;
text.DRB: The captain over fifty, and the honourable in countenance, and the counsellor, and the architect, and the skillful in eloquent speech.
text.KJV: 3:3 The captaine of fiftie, and the honourable man, and the counseller, and the cunning artificer, and the eloquent oratour.
text.NABRE: The captain of fifty and the nobleman, counselor, skilled magician, and expert charmer.

text.NRSV: And I will make boys their princes, and babes shall rule over them.
text.Rotherham: And I will appoint boys to be their princes,—And, petulant children, shall rule over them,
text.DRB: And I will give children to be their princes, and the effeminate shall rule over them.
text.KJV: 3:4 And I will giue children to bee their Princes, and babes shall rule ouer them.
text.NABRE: I will place boys as their princes; the fickle will govern them,

text.NRSV: The people will be oppressed, everyone by another and everyone by a neighbor; the youth will be insolent to the elder, and the base to the honorable.
text.Rotherham: And the people will, tyrannise, Every man over his fellow-man, And every man over his friend,—And they will rage The boy against, the elder, And the despised, against, the honourable,
text.DRB: And the people shall rush one upon another, and every man against his neighbour: the child shall make it tumult against the ancient, and the base against the honourable.
text.KJV: 3:5 And the people shall be oppressed, euery one by another, and euery one by his neighbour: the childe shall behaue himselfe proudly against the ancient, and the base against the honourable.
text.NABRE: And the people will oppress one another, yes, each one the neighbor. The child will be insolent toward the elder, and the base toward the honorable.

text.NRSV: Someone will even seize a relative, a member of the clan, saying, “You have a cloak; you shall be our leader, and this heap of ruins shall be under your rule.”
text.Rotherham: When a man shall lay hold, on his brother of the house of his father, [saying] —Clothing, hast thou, Become thou our, ruler, This downfall, be under thy hand,
text.DRB: For a man shall take hold or his brother, one of the house of his father, saying: Thou hast a garment, be thou our ruler, and let this ruin be under thy hand.
text.KJV: 3:6 When a man shall take hold of his brother of the house of his father, saying, Thou hast clothing, be thou our ruler, and let this ruine bee vnder thy hand:
text.NABRE: When anyone seizes a brother in their father’s house, saying, “You have clothes! Be our ruler, and take in hand this ruin!”—

text.NRSV: But the other will cry out on that day, saying, “I will not be a healer; in my house there is neither bread nor cloak; you shall not make me leader of the people.”
text.Rotherham: He will swear, in that day, saying—I will take no control, When in mine own house, is neither food nor clothing,—Ye must not set me for a ruler of people!
text.DRB: In that day he shall answer, saying: I am no healer, and in my house there is no bread, nor clothing: make me not ruler of the people.
text.KJV: 3:7 In that day shall he sweare, saying, I will not be an healer: for in my house is neither bread nor clothing: make me not a ruler of the people.
text.NABRE: He will cry out in that day: “I cannot be a healer, when there is neither bread nor clothing in my own house! You will not make me a ruler of the people!”

text.NRSV: For Jerusalem has stumbled and Judah has fallen, because their speech and their deeds are against the Lord, defying his glorious presence.
text.Rotherham: For stumbled hath Jerusalem, and, Judah, hath fallen,—Because, their tongue and their doings, are against Yahweh, Provoking his glorious presence,
text.DRB: For Jerusalem is ruined, and Juda is fallen: because their tongue, and their devices are against the Lord, to provoke the eyes of his majesty.
text.KJV: 3:8 For Ierusalem is ruined, & Iudah is fallen: because their tongue and their doings are against the Lord, to prouoke the eyes of his glorie.
text.NABRE: Jerusalem has stumbled, Judah has fallen; for their speech and deeds affront the Lord, a provocation in the sight of his majesty.

text.NRSV: Their very look testifies against them; they proclaim their sin like Sodom, they do not hide it. Woe to them! For they have brought evil on themselves.
text.Rotherham: The show of their face, hath answered against them, And their sin—like Sodom, have they told, they have not concealed it. Alas for their souls! For they have requited to themselves, calamity.
text.DRB: The shew of their countenance hath answered them: and they have proclaimed abroad their sin as Sodom, and they have not hid it: woe to their souls, for evils are rendered to them.
text.KJV: 3:9 ¶ The shew of their countenance doeth witnesse against them, and they declare their sinne as Sodom, they hide it not: woe vnto their soule, for they haue rewarded euill vnto themselues.
text.NABRE: Their very look bears witness against them; they boast of their sin like Sodom, They do not hide it. Woe to them! They deal out evil to themselves.

text.NRSV: Tell the innocent how fortunate they are, for they shall eat the fruit of their labors.
text.Rotherham: Say ye to the righteous It is well! For the fruit of their doings, shall they eat:
text.DRB: Say to the just man that it is well, for he shall eat the fruit of his doings.
text.KJV: 3:10 Say yee to the righteous, that it shall be well with him: for they shall eate the fruit of their doings.
text.NABRE: Happy the just, for it will go well with them, the fruit of their works they will eat.

text.NRSV: Woe to the guilty! How unfortunate they are, for what their hands have done shall be done to them.
text.Rotherham: Alas! for the lawless, it is ill, For what his own hand hath matured, shall be done to him.
text.DRB: Woe to the wicked unto evil: for the reward of his hands shall be given him.
text.KJV: 3:11 Woe vnto the wicked, it shall be ill with him: for the reward of his handes shalbe giuen him.
text.NABRE: Woe to the wicked! It will go ill with them, with the work of their hands they will be repaid.

text.NRSV: My people—children are their oppressors, and women rule over them. O my people, your leaders mislead you, and confuse the course of your paths.
text.Rotherham: My people! children, are their tyrants, And, women, rule over them,—My people! they who should lead thee forward, are causing thee to stray, Thy pathways, have they destroyed,
text.DRB: As for my people, their oppressors have stripped them, and women have ruled over them. O my people, they that call thee blessed, the same deceive thee, and destroy the way of thy steps.
text.KJV: 3:12 ¶ As for my people, children are their oppressours, and women rule ouer them: O my people, they which lead thee, cause thee to erre, and destroy the way of thy paths.
text.NABRE: My people—infants oppress them, women rule over them! My people, your leaders deceive you, they confuse the paths you should follow.

text.NRSV: The Lord rises to argue his case; he stands to judge the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh hath taken his station to plead,—And is standing to judge peoples:
text.DRB: The Lord standeth up to judge, and he standeth to judge the people.
text.KJV: 3:13 The Lord standeth vp to plead, and standeth to iudge the people.
text.NABRE: The Lord rises to accuse, stands to try his people.

text.NRSV: The Lord enters into judgment with the elders and princes of his people: It is you who have devoured the vineyard; the spoil of the poor is in your houses.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, into judgment, will enter, With the elders of his people And their princes,—But ye, have consumed the vineyard, That which hath been robbed from the oppressed, is in your houses.
text.DRB: The Lord will enter into judgment with the ancients of his people, and its princes: for you have devoured the vineyard, and the spoil of the poor is in your house.
text.KJV: 3:14 The Lord will enter into iudgement with the ancients of his people, and the Princes thereof: for ye haue eaten vp the Uineyard; the spoile of the poore is in your houses.
text.NABRE: The Lord enters into judgment with the people’s elders and princes: You, you who have devoured the vineyard; the loot wrested from the poor is in your houses.

text.NRSV: What do you mean by crushing my people, by grinding the face of the poor? says the Lord God of hosts.
text.Rotherham: What right have ye to crush my people, And the faces of the oppressed, to grind? Demandeth My Lord Yahweh of hosts—And Yahweh saith—
text.DRB: Why do you consume my people, and grind the faces of the poor? saith the Lord the God of hosts.
text.KJV: 3:15 What meane yee that yee beat my people to pieces, and grinde the faces of the poore, saith the Lord God of hosts?
text.NABRE: What do you mean by crushing my people, and grinding down the faces of the poor? says the Lord, the God of hosts.

text.NRSV: The Lord said: Because the daughters of Zion are haughty and walk with outstretched necks, glancing wantonly with their eyes, mincing along as they go, tinkling with their feet;
text.Rotherham: Because haughty are the daughters of Zion, And they walk with neck thrown back, and wanton eyes,—Tripping along as they go, And with their feet, making, a tinkling sound,
text.DRB: And the Lord said: Because the daughters of Sion are haughty, and have walked with stretched out necks, and wanton glances of their eyes, and made a noise as they walked with their feet and moved in a set pace:
text.KJV: 3:16 ¶ Moreouer the Lord saith; Because the daughters of Zion are hautie, and walke with stretched forth necks, and wanton eyes, walking and mincing as they goe, and making a tinkeling with their feet:
text.NABRE: The Haughty Women of Zion The Lord said: Because the daughters of Zion are haughty, and walk with necks outstretched, Ogling and mincing as they go, their anklets tinkling with every step,

text.NRSV: the Lord will afflict with scabs the heads of the daughters of Zion, and the Lord will lay bare their secret parts.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will My Lord, smite with leprosy the crown of hair of the daughters of Zion,—And, as for Yahweh, their shame, will he lay bare!
text.DRB: The Lord will make bald the crown of the head of the daughters of Sion, and the Lord will discover their hair.
text.KJV: 3:17 Therefore the Lord will smite with a scab the crowne of the head of the daughters of Zion, and the Lord will discouer their secret parts.
text.NABRE: The Lord shall cover the scalps of Zion’s daughters with scabs, and the Lord shall lay bare their heads.

text.NRSV: In that day the Lord will take away the finery of the anklets, the headbands, and the crescents;
text.Rotherham: In that day, will My Lord remove the finery—of the anklets, and the little suns and the little moons;
text.DRB: In that day the Lord will take away the ornaments of shoes, and little moons,
text.KJV: 3:18 In that day the Lord will take away the brauery of their tinckling ornaments about their feete, and their caules, and their round tyres like the Moone.
text.NABRE: On that day the Lord will do away with the finery of the anklets, sunbursts, and crescents;

text.NRSV: the pendants, the bracelets, and the scarfs;
text.Rotherham: the pendants and the bracelets and the veils;
text.DRB: And chains and necklaces, and bracelets, and bonnets,
text.KJV: 3:19 The chaines, and the bracelets, and the mufflers,
text.NABRE: the pendants, bracelets, and veils;

text.NRSV: the headdresses, the armlets, the sashes, the perfume boxes, and the amulets;
text.Rotherham: the chaplets and the armlets and the girdles, and the scent-cases, and the amulets;
text.DRB: And bodkins, and ornaments of the legs, and tablets, and sweet balls, and earrings,
text.KJV: 3:20 The bonnets, and the ornaments of the legges, and the headbands, and the tablets, and the earerings,
text.NABRE: the headdresses, bangles, cinctures, perfume boxes, and amulets;

text.NRSV: the signet rings and nose rings;
text.Rotherham: the rings and the nose-jewels;
text.DRB: And rings, and jewels hanging on the forehead,
text.KJV: 3:21 The rings, and nose-iewels,
text.NABRE: the signet rings, and the nose rings;

text.NRSV: the festal robes, the mantles, the cloaks, and the handbags;
text.Rotherham: the robes, and the over-tunics, and the cloaks and the purses;
text.DRB: And changes of apparel, and short cloaks, and fine linen, and crisping pins,
text.KJV: 3:22 The changeable sutes of apparell, and the mantles, and the wimples, and the crisping pinnes,
text.NABRE: the court dresses, wraps, cloaks, and purses;

text.NRSV: the garments of gauze, the linen cloths, the turbans, and the veils.
text.Rotherham: and the mirrors and the linen wraps, and the tiaras and the cloaks.
text.DRB: And looking-glasses, and lawns, and headbands, and fine veils.
text.KJV: 3:23 The glasses, and the fine linnen, and the hoods, and the vailes.
text.NABRE: the lace gowns, linen tunics, turbans, and shawls.

text.NRSV: Instead of perfume there will be a stench; and instead of a sash, a rope; and instead of well-set hair, baldness; and instead of a rich robe, a binding of sackcloth; instead of beauty, shame.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass—That instead of fragrance, a putrid odour, shall be, And instead of a girdle, an encircling rope, And instead of braided hair, baldness, And instead of a festal robe, a girding of sackcloth, Branding instead of beauty:
text.DRB: And instead of a sweet smell there shall be stench, and instead of a girdle, a cord, and instead of curled hair, baldness, and instead of a stomacher, haircloth.
text.KJV: 3:24 And it shall come to passe, that in steade of sweete smell, there shall bee stinke; and in stead of a girdle, a rent; and in stead of well set haire, baldnesse; and in stead of a stomacher, a girding of sackecloth; and burning, in stead of beautie.
text.NABRE: Instead of perfume there will be stench, instead of a girdle, a rope, And instead of elaborate coiffure, baldness; instead of a rich gown, a sackcloth skirt. Then, instead of beauty, shame.

text.NRSV: Your men shall fall by the sword and your warriors in battle.
text.Rotherham: Thy males, by the sword shall fall,—And, thy mighty men, by the war;
text.DRB: Thy fairest men also shall fall by the sword, and thy valiant ones in battle.
text.KJV: 3:25 Thy men shall fall by the sword, and thy mightie in the warre.
text.NABRE: Your men will fall by the sword, and your champions, in war;

text.NRSV: And her gates shall lament and mourn; ravaged, she shall sit upon the ground.
text.Rotherham: And her gates shall mourn and lament,—And forsaken, on the ground, shall she sit.
text.DRB: And her gates shall lament and mourn, and she shall sit desolate on the ground.
text.KJV: 3:26 And her gates shall lament and mourne; and she being desolate, shall sit vpon the ground.
text.NABRE: Her gates will lament and mourn, as the city sits desolate on the ground.

text.NRSV: Seven women shall take hold of one man in that day, saying, “We will eat our own bread and wear our own clothes; just let us be called by your name; take away our disgrace.”
text.Rotherham: And seven women will take hold of one man in that day, saying, Our own bread, will we eat, And our own apparel, will we wear,—Only let us be called by thy name, to take away our reproach.
text.DRB: And in that day seven women shall take hold of one man, saying: We will eat our own bread, and wear our own apparel: only let us be called by thy name, take away our reproach.
text.KJV: 4:1 And in that day seuen women shall take hold of one man, saying, We will eate our owne bread, & weare our owne apparell: onely let vs be called by thy name, to take away our reproch.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Seven women will take hold of one man on that day, saying: “We will eat our own food and wear our own clothing; Only let your name be given us, put an end to our disgrace!”

text.NRSV: On that day the branch of the Lord shall be beautiful and glorious, and the fruit of the land shall be the pride and glory of the survivors of Israel.
text.Rotherham: In that day, Shall the Bud of Yahweh, become beautiful and glorious,—And the Fruit of the Land splendid and majestic, To the escaped of Israel.
text.DRB: In that day the bud of the Lord shall be in magnificence and glory, and the fruit of the earth shall be high, and a great joy to them that shall have escaped of Israel.
text.KJV: 4:2 In that day shall the Branch of the Lord be beautifull and glorious, and the fruit of the earth shalbe excellent and comely for them that are escaped of Israel.
text.NABRE: Jerusalem Purified On that day, The branch of the Lord will be beauty and glory, and the fruit of the land will be honor and splendor for the survivors of Israel.

text.NRSV: Whoever is left in Zion and remains in Jerusalem will be called holy, everyone who has been recorded for life in Jerusalem,
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass—He that is left in Zion! And he that remaineth in Jerusalem, Shall be called, holy,—Everyone written unto life in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass, that every one that shall be left in Sion, and that shall remain in Jerusalem, shall be called holy, every one that is written in life in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 4:3 And it shall come to passe, that hee that is left in Zion, and hee that remaineth in Ierusalem, shall be called Holy, euen euery one that is written among the liuing in Ierusalem,
text.NABRE: Everyone who remains in Zion, everyone left in Jerusalem Will be called holy: everyone inscribed for life in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: once the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion and cleansed the bloodstains of Jerusalem from its midst by a spirit of judgment and by a spirit of burning.
text.Rotherham: When My Lord shall have bathed away the filth of the daughters of Zion, And the blood-guiltiness of Jerusalem, he shall wash away out of her midst,—By the spirit of judgment, and By the spirit of thorough cleansing,
text.DRB: If the Lord shall wash away the filth of the daughters of Sion, and shall wash away the blood of Jerusalem out of the midst thereof, by the spirit of judgment, and by the spirit of burning.
text.KJV: 4:4 When the Lord shall haue washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion, and shall haue purged the blood of Ierusalem from the middest thereof, by the spirit of iudgement, and by the spirit of burning.
text.NABRE: When the Lord washes away the filth of the daughters of Zion, And purges Jerusalem’s blood from her midst with a blast of judgment, a searing blast,

text.NRSV: Then the Lord will create over the whole site of Mount Zion and over its assemblies a cloud by day and smoke and the shining of a flaming fire by night. Indeed over all the glory there will be a canopy.
text.Rotherham: Then will Yahweh, create—Over all the home of Mount Zion and Over her assembly, A cloud by day and a smoke, And the shining of a fire-flame, by night,—For over all the glory, shall be a canopy;
text.DRB: And the Lord will create upon every place of mount Sion, and where he is called upon, a cloud by day, and a smoke and the brightness of a flaming fire in the night: for over all the glory shall be a protection.
text.KJV: 4:5 And the Lord will create vpon euery dwelling place of mount Zion, and vpon her assemblies a cloude, and smoke by day, and the shining of a flaming fire by night; for vpon all the glory shall be a defence.
text.NABRE: Then will the Lord create, over the whole site of Mount Zion and over her place of assembly, A smoking cloud by day and a light of flaming fire by night.

text.NRSV: It will serve as a pavilion, a shade by day from the heat, and a refuge and a shelter from the storm and rain.
text.Rotherham: And a pavilion, shall there be For a shade by day, from the heat,—and For a refuge and for a shelter, from storm and from rain.
text.DRB: And there shall be a tabernacle for a shade in the daytime from the heat, and for a security and covert from the whirlwind, and from rain.
text.KJV: 4:6 And there shalbe a tabernacle for a shadow in the day time from the heat, and for a place of refuge, and for a couert from storme and from raine.
text.NABRE: For over all, his glory will be shelter and protection: shade from the parching heat of day, refuge and cover from storm and rain.

text.NRSV: Let me sing for my beloved my love-song concerning his vineyard: My beloved had a vineyard on a very fertile hill.
text.Rotherham: Let me sing, I pray you, for a well-beloved of mine, The song of my beloved concerning his vineyard:—A vineyard, had my well-beloved on a very fruitful hill;
text.DRB: I will sing to my beloved the canticle of my cousin concerning his vineyard. My beloved had a vineyard on a hill in a fruitful place.
text.KJV: 5:1 Now will I sing to my welbeloued, a song of my beloued touching his vineyard: my wellbeloued hath a vineyard in a very fruitfull hill.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 The Song of the Vineyard Now let me sing of my friend, my beloved’s song about his vineyard. My friend had a vineyard on a fertile hillside;

text.NRSV: He dug it and cleared it of stones, and planted it with choice vines; he built a watchtower in the midst of it, and hewed out a wine vat in it; he expected it to yield grapes, but it yielded wild grapes.
text.Rotherham: And he thoroughly digged it, And gathered out the stones thereof, And planted it with a precious vine, And built a tower in the midst thereof, Moreover also a wine-press, hewed he therein,—Then waited he that it should bring forth grapes. And it brought forth wild grapes:
text.DRB: And he fenced it in, and picked the stones out of it, and planted it with the choicest vines, and built a tower in the midst thereof, and set up a winepress therein: and he looked that it should bring forth grapes, and it broutht forth wild grapes.
text.KJV: 5:2 And hee fenced it, and gathered out the stones thereof, and planted it with the choicest vine, and built a towre in the middest of it, and also made a winepresse therein: and he looked that it should bring foorth grapes, and it brought foorth wilde grapes.
text.NABRE: He spaded it, cleared it of stones, and planted the choicest vines; Within it he built a watchtower, and hewed out a wine press. Then he waited for the crop of grapes, but it yielded rotten grapes.

text.NRSV: And now, inhabitants of Jerusalem and people of Judah, judge between me and my vineyard.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O inhabitant of Jerusalem, And men of Judah,—Judge, I pray you, betwixt me, and my vineyard:—
text.DRB: And now, O ye inhabitants of Jerusalem, and ye men of Juda, judge between me and my vineyard.
text.KJV: 5:3 And now, O inhabitants of Ierusalem, and men of Iudah, Iudge, I pray you, betwixt me and my Uineyard.
text.NABRE: Now, inhabitants of Jerusalem, people of Judah, judge between me and my vineyard:

text.NRSV: What more was there to do for my vineyard that I have not done in it? When I expected it to yield grapes, why did it yield wild grapes?
text.Rotherham: What could have been done further to my vineyard, That I had not done in it? Why then—When I had waited that it should bring forth grapes, Brought it forth, wild grapes?
text.DRB: What is there that I ought to do more to my vineyard, that I have not done to it?
text.KJV: 5:4 What could haue beene done more to my Uineyard, that I haue not done in it? wherefore when I looked that it should bring foorth grapes, brought it foorth wilde grapes?
text.NABRE: What more could be done for my vineyard that I did not do? Why, when I waited for the crop of grapes, did it yield rotten grapes?

text.NRSV: And now I will tell you what I will do to my vineyard. I will remove its hedge, and it shall be devoured; I will break down its wall, and it shall be trampled down.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I pray you, let me tell, you, what I am about to do to my vineyard,—To take away the fence thereof And it shall be eaten up, To destroy the wall thereof And it shall be trodden down;
text.DRB: And now I will shew you what I will do to my vineyard. I will take away the hedge thereof, and it shall be wasted: I will break down the wall thereof, and it shall be trodden down.
text.KJV: 5:5 And now goe to; I will tell you what I will doe to my Uineyard, I will take away the hedge thereof, and it shall be eaten vp; and breake downe the wall thereof, and it shall be troden downe.
text.NABRE: Now, I will let you know what I am going to do to my vineyard: Take away its hedge, give it to grazing, break through its wall, let it be trampled!

text.NRSV: I will make it a waste; it shall not be pruned or hoed, and it shall be overgrown with briers and thorns; I will also command the clouds that they rain no rain upon it.
text.Rotherham: And I will make it a waste;—It shall be neither pruned nor hoed, But there shall come up briars and thorns,—And upon the clouds, will I lay a charge, That they rain thereon no rain.
text.DRB: And I will make it desolate: it shall not be pruned, and it shall not be digged: but briers and thorns shall come up: and I will command the clouds to rain no rain upon it.
text.KJV: 5:6 And I will lay it waste; it shall not be pruned, nor digged, but there shall come vp briars and thornes: I will also command the cloudes, that they raine no raine vpon it.
text.NABRE: Yes, I will make it a ruin: it shall not be pruned or hoed, but will be overgrown with thorns and briers; I will command the clouds not to rain upon it.

text.NRSV: For the vineyard of the Lord of hosts is the house of Israel, and the people of Judah are his pleasant planting; he expected justice, but saw bloodshed; righteousness, but heard a cry!
text.Rotherham: Surely the vineyard of Yahweh of hosts, is the house of Israel, And, the men of Judah, are the plantation in which he dearly delighted,—And he waited, For equity but lo! murderous iniquity, For the rule of right but lo the cry of the wronged.
text.DRB: For the vineyard of the Lord of hosts is the house of Israel: and the man of Juda, his pleasant plant: and I looked that he should do judgment, and behold iniquity: and do justice, and behold a cry.
text.KJV: 5:7 For the Uineyard of the Lord of hostes is the house of Israel, and the men of Iudah his pleasant plant: and he looked for iudgement, but beholde oppression; for righteousnesse, but behold a crie.
text.NABRE: The vineyard of the Lord of hosts is the house of Israel, the people of Judah, his cherished plant; He waited for judgment, but see, bloodshed! for justice, but hark, the outcry!

text.NRSV: Ah, you who join house to house, who add field to field, until there is room for no one but you, and you are left to live alone in the midst of the land!
text.Rotherham: Alas for them who join house to house, Field to field, bring they near,—Until there is no room, But ye are left to dwell alone in the midst of the land.
text.DRB: Woe to you that join house to house and lay field to field, even to the end of the place: shall you alone dwell in the midst of the earth?
text.KJV: 5:8 ¶ Woe vnto them that ioyne house to house, that lay field to field, till there be no place, that they may be placed alone in the midst of the earth.
text.NABRE: Oracles of Reproach Ah! Those who join house to house, who connect field with field, Until no space remains, and you alone dwell in the midst of the land!

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts has sworn in my hearing: Surely many houses shall be desolate, large and beautiful houses, without inhabitant.
text.Rotherham: In mine ears, [said] Yahweh of hosts,—Verily, houses in abundance, shall become, a desolation, Large and fair, without inhabitant;
text.DRB: These things are in my ears, saith the Lord of hosts: unless many great and fair houses shall become desolate, without an inhabitant.
text.KJV: 5:9 In mine eares said the Lord of hostes, Of a trueth many houses shall be desolate, euen great and faire without inhabitant.
text.NABRE: In my hearing the Lord of hosts has sworn: Many houses shall be in ruins, houses large and fine, with nobody living there.

text.NRSV: For ten acres of vineyard shall yield but one bath, and a homer of seed shall yield but an ephah.
text.Rotherham: For, ten yokes of vineyard, shall yield one bath,—And the seed of a homer, shall yield an ephah,
text.DRB: For ten acres of vineyard shall yield one little measure, and thirty bushels of seed shall yield three bushels.
text.KJV: 5:10 Yea ten acres of vineyard shall yeeld one Bath, and the seed of an Homer shall yeeld an Ephah.
text.NABRE: Ten acres of vineyard shall yield but one bath, And a homer of seed shall yield but an ephah.

text.NRSV: Ah, you who rise early in the morning in pursuit of strong drink, who linger in the evening to be inflamed by wine,
text.Rotherham: Alas, for them who rise early in the morning that strong drink, they may pursue,—Who follow on in the evening cool [until] with wine, they are heated;
text.DRB: Woe to you that rise up early in the morning to follow drunkenness, and to drink till the evening, to be inflamed with wine.
text.KJV: 5:11 ¶ Woe vnto them that rise vp earely in the morning, that they may follow strong drink, that continue vntill night, till wine enflame them.
text.NABRE: Ah! Those who rise early in the morning in pursuit of strong drink, lingering late inflamed by wine,

text.NRSV: whose feasts consist of lyre and harp, tambourine and flute and wine, but who do not regard the deeds of the Lord, or see the work of his hands!
text.Rotherham: And it cometh to pass—that lyre and harp, timbrel and flute and wine are in their banquets,—But, the doing of Yahweh, they do not discern, And the work of his hands, have they not seen.
text.DRB: The harp, and the lyre, and the timbrel, and the pipe, and wine are in your feasts: and the work of the Lord you regard not, nor do you consider the works of his hands.
text.KJV: 5:12 And the harpe and the viole, the tabret and pipe, and wine are in their feasts: but they regard not the worke of the Lord, neither consider the operation of his hands.
text.NABRE: Banqueting on wine with harp and lyre, timbrel and flute, But the deed of the Lord they do not regard, the work of his hands they do not see!

text.NRSV: Therefore my people go into exile without knowledge; their nobles are dying of hunger, and their multitude is parched with thirst.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, are my people taken away captive before they know it,—And their honourable men are famished with hunger, And, their multitude, do gape for thirst.
text.DRB: Therefore is my people led away captive, because they had not knowledge, and their nobles have perished with famine, and their multitude were dried up with thirst.
text.KJV: 5:13 ¶ Therefore my people are gone into captiuitie, because they haue no knowledge: and their honourable men are famished, aud their multitude dried vp with thirst.
text.NABRE: Therefore my people go into exile for lack of understanding, Its nobles starving, its masses parched with thirst.

text.NRSV: Therefore Sheol has enlarged its appetite and opened its mouth beyond measure; the nobility of Jerusalem and her multitude go down, her throng and all who exult in her.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, hath hades enlarged her desire, And opened her mouth to its widest,—And their glory, and their multitude and their pomp. and he that is uproarious shall descend thereinto.
text.DRB: Therefore hath hell enlarged her soul, and opened her mouth without any bounds, and their strong ones, and their people, and their high and glorious ones shall go down into it.
text.KJV: 5:14 Therefore hell hath enlarged her selfe, and opened her mouth without measure: and their glory, and their multitude, and their pompe, and hee that reioyceth, shall descend into it.
text.NABRE: Therefore Sheol enlarges its throat and opens its mouth beyond measure; Down into it go nobility and masses, tumult and revelry.

text.NRSV: People are bowed down, everyone is brought low, and the eyes of the haughty are humbled.
text.Rotherham: And the mean man hath been bowed down And the mighty man hath been humbled,—Yea the looks of the haughty shall be humbled.
text.DRB: And man shall be brought down, and man shall be humbled, and the eyes of the lofty shall be brought low.
text.KJV: 5:15 And the meane man shall bee brought downe, and the mightie man shall be humbled, and the eyes of the loftie shall be humbled.
text.NABRE: All shall be abased, each one brought low, and the eyes of the haughty lowered,

text.NRSV: But the Lord of hosts is exalted by justice, and the Holy God shows himself holy by righteousness.
text.Rotherham: But Yahweh of hosts, hath been exalted, in justice,—And the GOD that is holy hath been hallowed in righteousness;
text.DRB: And the Lord of hosts shall be exalted in judgment, and the holy God shall be sanctified in justice.
text.KJV: 5:16 But the Lord of hosts shalbe exalted in iudgement, and God that is holy, shall bee sanctified in righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: But the Lord of hosts shall be exalted by judgment, by justice the Holy God shown holy.

text.NRSV: Then the lambs shall graze as in their pasture, and fatlings shall feed among the ruins.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the young rams feed where they please,—And, the wastes of the wealthy, shall strangers consume.
text.DRB: And the lambs shall feed according to their order, and strangers shall eat the deserts turned into fruitfulness.
text.KJV: 5:17 Then shall the lambes feed after their maner, and the waste places of the fat ones shall strangers eate.
text.NABRE: Lambs shall graze as at pasture, young goats shall eat in the ruins of the rich.

text.NRSV: Ah, you who drag iniquity along with cords of falsehood, who drag sin along as with cart ropes,
text.Rotherham: Alas! for them Who draw on themselves punishment with cords of falsehood,—And as with waggon-bands, penalty:
text.DRB: Woe to you that draw iniquity with cords of vanity, and sin as the rope of a cart.
text.KJV: 5:18 Woe vnto them that draw iniquitie with cords of vanitie, and sinne, as it were with a cart rope:
text.NABRE: Ah! Those who tug at guilt with cords of perversity, and at sin as if with cart ropes!

text.NRSV: who say, “Let him make haste, let him speed his work that we may see it; let the plan of the Holy One of Israel draw near and be fulfilled, that we may know it!”
text.Rotherham: Who say—Let his work quicken—let it hasten, That we may see,—And let the purpose of Israel’s Holy One, draw near and come. That we may know!
text.DRB: That say: Let him make haste, and let his work come quickly, that we may see it: and let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel come, that we may know it.
text.KJV: 5:19 That say, Let him make speede, and hasten his worke, that we may see it: and let the counsell of the holy one of Israel draw nigh and come, that wee may know it.
text.NABRE: Who say, “Let him make haste, let him speed his work, that we may see it; On with the plan of the Holy One of Israel! let it come to pass, that we may know it!”

text.NRSV: Ah, you who call evil good and good evil, who put darkness for light and light for darkness, who put bitter for sweet and sweet for bitter!
text.Rotherham: Alas! for them Who call evil good and good evil,—Who put darkness for light and light for darkness, Who put bitter for sweet and sweet for bitter.
text.DRB: Woe to you that call evil good, and good evil: that put darkness for light, and light for darkness: that put bitter for sweet, and sweet for bitter.
text.KJV: 5:20 ¶ Woe vnto them that call euill good, and good euill, that put darkenes for light, and light for darkenesse, that put bitter for sweete, and sweete for bitter.
text.NABRE: Ah! Those who call evil good, and good evil, who change darkness to light, and light into darkness, who change bitter to sweet, and sweet into bitter!

text.NRSV: Ah, you who are wise in your own eyes, and shrewd in your own sight!
text.Rotherham: Alas for them Who are wise in their own eyes,—And in their own sight, are prudent.
text.DRB: Woe to you that are wise in your own eyes, and prudent in your own conceits.
text.KJV: 5:21 Woe vnto them that are wise in their owne eyes, and prudent in their owne sight.
text.NABRE: Ah! Those who are wise in their own eyes, prudent in their own view!

text.NRSV: Ah, you who are heroes in drinking wine and valiant at mixing strong drink,
text.Rotherham: Alas for them Who are heroes to drink wine,—And men of valour to mingle strong drink,
text.DRB: Woe to you that are mighty to drink wine, and stout men at drunkenness.
text.KJV: 5:22 Woe vnto them that are mightie to drinke wine, and men of strength to mingle strong drinke.
text.NABRE: Ah! Those who are champions at drinking wine, masters at mixing drink!

text.NRSV: who acquit the guilty for a bribe, and deprive the innocent of their rights!
text.Rotherham: Who justify the lawless, for a bribe,—Whereas the righteousness of the righteous, they take from him.
text.DRB: That justify the wicked for gifts, and take away the justice of the just from him.
text.KJV: 5:23 Which iustifie the wicked for reward, and take away the righteousnes of the righteous from him.
text.NABRE: Those who acquit the guilty for bribes, and deprive the innocent of justice!

text.NRSV: Therefore, as the tongue of fire devours the stubble, and as dry grass sinks down in the flame, so their root will become rotten, and their blossom go up like dust; for they have rejected the instruction of the Lord of hosts, and have despised the word of the Holy One of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, As a tongue of fire, eateth up straw, And a flame reduceth, dry grass, to powder, Their root, like rottenness, shall become, And their blossom, like dust shall ascend,—Because they refused the law of Yahweh of hosts, And the utterance of the Holy One of Israel, they despised.
text.DRB: Therefore as the tongue of the fire devoureth the stubble, and the heat of the flame consumeth it: so shall their root be as ashes, and their bud shall go up as dust: for they have cast away the law of the Lord of hosts, and have blasphemed the word of the Holy One of Israel.
text.KJV: 5:24 Therfore as the fire deuoureth the stubble, and the flame consumeth the chaffe, so their root shall be rottennes, and their blossome shall goe vp as dust: because they haue cast away the Lawe of the Lord of hosts, and despised the worde of the Holy One of Israel.
text.NABRE: Therefore, as the tongue of fire licks up stubble, as dry grass shrivels in the flame, Their root shall rot and their blossom scatter like dust; For they have rejected the instruction of the Lord of hosts, and scorned the word of the Holy One of Israel.

text.NRSV: Therefore the anger of the Lord was kindled against his people, and he stretched out his hand against them and struck them; the mountains quaked, and their corpses were like refuse in the streets. For all this his anger has not turned away, and his hand is stretched out still.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, did the anger of Yahweh kindle upon his own people, And he stretched out his hand against them and smote them—So that the mountains, trembled, And, their dead bodies, served, for fuel in the midst of the streets. For all this, hath his anger, not turned back, But still, is his hand outstretched,
text.DRB: Therefore is the wrath of the Lord kindled against his people, and he hath stretched out his hand upon them, and struck them: and the mountains were troubled, and their carcasses became as dung in the midst of the streets. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
text.KJV: 5:25 Therefore is the anger of the Lord kindled against his people, and he hath stretched foorth his hande against them, and hath smitten them: and the hilles did tremble, and their carkeises were torne in the midst of the streets: for all this, his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
text.NABRE: Therefore the wrath of the Lord blazes against his people, he stretches out his hand to strike them; The mountains quake, their corpses shall be like refuse in the streets. For all this, his wrath is not turned back, his hand is still outstretched.

text.NRSV: He will raise a signal for a nation far away, and whistle for a people at the ends of the earth; Here they come, swiftly, speedily!
text.Rotherham: Therefore will he lift up an ensign to the nations afar off, And signal for him from the end of the earth,—And lo! with hot haste, will he come:
text.DRB: And he will lift up a sign to the nations afar off, and will whistle to them from the ends of the earth: and behold they shall come with speed swiftly.
