text.KJV: 5:26 ¶ And he will lift vp an ensigne to the nations from farre, and wil hisse vnto them from the end of the earth: and behold, they shall come with speed swiftly.
text.NABRE: Invasion He will raise a signal to a far-off nation, and whistle for it from the ends of the earth. Then speedily and promptly they will come.

text.NRSV: None of them is weary, none stumbles, none slumbers or sleeps, not a loincloth is loose, not a sandal-thong broken;
text.Rotherham: None shall be weary, and none shall stumble in his midst, He shall neither slumber nor sleep,—Nor shall be loosened, the girdle of his loins, Nor snapped, the thong of his sandals:
text.DRB: There is none that shall faint, nor labour among them: they shall not slumber nor sleep, neither shall the girdle of their loins be loosed, nor the latchet of their shoes be broken.
text.KJV: 5:27 None shalbe weary, nor stumble amongst them: none shall slumber nor sleepe, neither shall the girdle of their loynes be loosed, nor the latchet of their shooes be broken.
text.NABRE: None among them is weary, none stumbles, none will slumber, none will sleep. None with waist belt loose, none with sandal thong broken.

text.NRSV: their arrows are sharp, all their bows bent, their horses’ hoofs seem like flint, and their wheels like the whirlwind.
text.Rotherham: Whose arrows, are sharpened, and all his bows, bent,—The hoofs of his horses, like flint, are accounted, And, his wheels, [are] like a storm-wind:
text.DRB: Their arrows are sharp, and all their bows are bent. The hoofs of their horses shall be like the flint, and their wheels like the violence of a tempest.
text.KJV: 5:28 Whose arrowes are sharpe, and all their bowes bent, their horses hoofs shall bee counted like flint, and their wheeles like a whirlewind.
text.NABRE: Their arrows are sharp, and all their bows are bent, The hooves of their horses like flint, and their chariot wheels like the whirlwind.

text.NRSV: Their roaring is like a lion, like young lions they roar; they growl and seize their prey, they carry it off, and no one can rescue.
text.Rotherham: A roar, hath he, like a lioness,—He will roar like wild lions—And will growl, and lay hold on prey, and carry into safety, and there be none to deliver.
text.DRB: Their roaring like that of a lion, they shall roar like young lions: yea they shall roar, and take hold of the prey, and they shall keep fast hold of it, and there shall be none to deliver it.
text.KJV: 5:29 Their roaring shalbe like a lyon, they shall roare like yong lions: yea they shal roare and lay hold of the pray, and shall carie it away safe, and none shall deliuer it.
text.NABRE: They roar like the lion, like young lions, they roar; They growl and seize the prey, they carry it off and none can rescue.

text.NRSV: They will roar over it on that day, like the roaring of the sea. And if one look to the land— only darkness and distress; and the light is darkened by its clouds.
text.Rotherham: Yea he will growl at him in that day, like the growling of the sea,—Though he look hard for the land, lo! the darkness of distress, Yea the light, hath grown dark in its clouds!
text.DRB: And they shall make a noise against them that day, like the roaring of the sea; we shall look towards the land, and behold darkness of tribulation, and the light is darkened with the mist thereof.
text.KJV: 5:30 And in that day they shall roare against them, like the roaring of the sea: and if one looke vnto the land, behold darkenesse and sorrow, and the light is darkened in the heauens therof.
text.NABRE: They will growl over it, on that day, like the growling of the sea, Look to the land— darkness closing in, the light dark with clouds!

text.NRSV: In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord sitting on a throne, high and lofty; and the hem of his robe filled the temple.
text.Rotherham: In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw My Lord, sitting upon a throne high and lifted up, and, his skirts, did fill the temple,
text.DRB: In the year that king Ozias died, I saw the Lord sitting upon a throne high and elevated: and his train filled the temple.
text.KJV: 6:1 In the yeere that King Uzziah died, I saw also the Lord sitting vpon a throne, high and lifted vp, and his traine filled the Temple.
text.NABRE: B. The Book of Emmanuel Chapter 6 The Sending of Isaiah. In the year King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord seated on a high and lofty throne, with the train of his garment filling the temple.

text.NRSV: Seraphs were in attendance above him; each had six wings: with two they covered their faces, and with two they covered their feet, and with two they flew.
text.Rotherham: Seraphim, were standing above him; six wings severally, had each one,—With twain, he covered his face And with twain, he covered his feet And with twain, he did fly.
text.DRB: Upon it stood the seraphims: the one had six wings, and the other had six wings: with two they covered his face, and with two they covered his feet, and with two they flew.
text.KJV: 6:2 Aboue it stood the Seraphims: each one had sixe wings, with twaine he couered his face, and with twaine hee couered his feete, and with twaine hee did flie.
text.NABRE: Seraphim were stationed above; each of them had six wings: with two they covered their faces, with two they covered their feet, and with two they hovered.

text.NRSV: And one called to another and said: “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of hosts; the whole earth is full of his glory.”
text.Rotherham: And they continued crying out one to another, and said, Holy—holy—holy, is Yahweh of hosts,—the fulness of the whole earth, is his glory.
text.DRB: And they cried one to another, and said: Holy, holy, holy, the Lord God of hosts, all the earth is full of his glory.
text.KJV: 6:3 And one cryed vnto another, and sayd; Holy, holy, holy, is the Lord of hostes, the whole earth is full of his glory.
text.NABRE: One cried out to the other: “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of hosts! All the earth is filled with his glory!”

text.NRSV: The pivots on the thresholds shook at the voices of those who called, and the house filled with smoke.
text.Rotherham: And the foundations of the porch, were moved at the voice of him that cried,—and the house, was filled with smoke.
text.DRB: And the lintels of the doors were moved at the voice of him that cried, and the house was filled with smoke.
text.KJV: 6:4 And the posts of the doore moued at the voyce of him that cryed, and the house was filled with smoke.
text.NABRE: At the sound of that cry, the frame of the door shook and the house was filled with smoke.

text.NRSV: And I said: “Woe is me! I am lost, for I am a man of unclean lips, and I live among a people of unclean lips; yet my eyes have seen the King, the Lord of hosts!”
text.Rotherham: Then said I—Woe to me!—for I am undone, Because a man of unclean lips, am, I, And in the midst of a people of unclean lips, do I dwell,—For the King. Yahweh of hosts, have mine eyes seen!
text.DRB: And I said: Woe is me, because I have held my peace; because I am a man of unclean lips, and I dwell in the midst of a people that hath unclean lips, and I have seen with my eyes the King the Lord of hosts.
text.KJV: 6:5 ¶ Then sayd I; woe is me; for I am vndone, because I am a man of vncleane lippes, and I dwell in the midst of a people of vncleane lippes: for mine eyes haue seene the king, the Lord of hostes.
text.NABRE: Then I said, “Woe is me, I am doomed! For I am a man of unclean lips, living among a people of unclean lips, and my eyes have seen the King, the Lord of hosts!”

text.NRSV: Then one of the seraphs flew to me, holding a live coal that had been taken from the altar with a pair of tongs.
text.Rotherham: Then flew unto me one of the seraphim, And in his hand, a live coal,—With tongs, had he taken it from off the altar.
text.DRB: And one of the seraphims flew to me, and in his hand was a live coal, which he had taken with the tongs off the altar.
text.KJV: 6:6 Then flew one of the Seraphims vnto mee, hauing a liue-cole in his hand, which hee had taken with the tongs from off the altar.
text.NABRE: Then one of the seraphim flew to me, holding an ember which he had taken with tongs from the altar.

text.NRSV: The seraph touched my mouth with it and said: “Now that this has touched your lips, your guilt has departed and your sin is blotted out.”
text.Rotherham: Then touched he my mouth, and said—Lo! this hath touched thy lips,—Thus shall be taken away, thine iniquity, And, thy sin, by propitiation be covered.
text.DRB: And he touched my mouth, and said: Behold this hath touched thy lips, and thy iniquities shall be taken away, and thy sin shall be cleansed.
text.KJV: 6:7 And he laide it vpon my mouth, and sayd, Loe, this hath touched thy lippes, and thine iniquitie is taken away, and thy sinne purged.
text.NABRE: He touched my mouth with it. “See,” he said, “now that this has touched your lips, your wickedness is removed, your sin purged.”

text.NRSV: Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying, “Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?” And I said, “Here am I; send me!”
text.Rotherham: Then heard I the voice of My Lord, saying, Whom shall I send? And who will go for us? And I said—Here am I—send me.
text.DRB: And I heard the voice of the Lord, saying: Whom shall I send? and who shall go for us? And I said: Lo, here am I, send me.
text.KJV: 6:8 Also I heard the voyce of the Lord, saying; Whom shall I send, and who will goe for vs? Then I saide; Heere am I, send me.
text.NABRE: Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying, “Whom shall I send? Who will go for us?” “Here I am,” I said; “send me!”

text.NRSV: And he said, “Go and say to this people: ‘Keep listening, but do not comprehend; keep looking, but do not understand.’
text.Rotherham: Then said he—Go and say unto this people,—Hear on but do not discern, See on but do not perceive:
text.DRB: And he said: Go, and thou shalt say to this people: Hearing, hear, and understand not: and see the vision, and know it not.
text.KJV: 6:9 ¶ And he sayd, Goe and tell this people; Heare yee indeede, but vnderstand not: and see yee indeed, but perceiue not.
text.NABRE: And he replied: Go and say to this people: Listen carefully, but do not understand! Look intently, but do not perceive!

text.NRSV: Make the mind of this people dull, and stop their ears, and shut their eyes, so that they may not look with their eyes, and listen with their ears, and understand with their minds, and turn and be healed.”
text.Rotherham: Stupefy thou the heart of this people, And their ears, make thou heavy, And their eyes, overspread,—Lest they see with their eyes And with their ears, should hear, And their heart should discern and come back. And they be healed.
text.DRB: Blind the heart of this people, and make their ears heavy, and shut their eyes: lest they see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and be converted and I heal them.
text.KJV: 6:10 Make the heart of this people fat, and make their eares heauy, and shut their eyes: lest they see with their eyes, and heare with their eares, and vnderstand with their heart, and conuert and be healed.
text.NABRE: Make the heart of this people sluggish, dull their ears and close their eyes; Lest they see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and their heart understand, and they turn and be healed.

text.NRSV: Then I said, “How long, O Lord?” And he said: “Until cities lie waste without inhabitant, and houses without people, and the land is a utter desolation;
text.Rotherham: Then said I—How long, My Lord? And he said—Until the time that Cities be wasted through having no inhabitant And houses—through having no men, And, the ground, be laid waste unto desolation;
text.DRB: And I said: How long, O Lord? And he said: Until the cities be wasted without inhabitant, and the houses without man, and the land shall be left desolate.
text.KJV: 6:11 Then sayd I; Lord, how long? And hee answered, Untill the cities be wasted without inhabitant, and the houses without man, and the land be vtterly desolate,
text.NABRE: “How long, O Lord?” I asked. And he replied: Until the cities are desolate, without inhabitants, Houses, without people, and the land is a desolate waste.

text.NRSV: until the Lord sends everyone far away, and vast is the emptiness in the midst of the land.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh have far removed men,—And great! be the abandonment in the midst of the land.
text.DRB: And the Lord shall remove men far away, and she shall be multiplied that was left in the midst of the earth.
text.KJV: 6:12 And the Lord haue remoued men farre away, and there be a great forsaking in the midst of the land.
text.NABRE: Until the Lord sends the people far away, and great is the desolation in the midst of the land.

text.NRSV: Even if a tenth part remain in it, it will be burned again, like a terebinth or an oak whose stump remains standing when it is felled.” The holy seed is its stump.
text.Rotherham: Yet still shall there be in it a tenth, Though it again be consumed,—Like an oak and like a terebinth Which when felled, have a stock in them, A holy seed, shall be the stock thereof.
text.DRB: And there shall be still a tithing therein, and she shall turn, and shall be made a shew as a turpentine tree, and as an oak that spreadeth its branches: that which shall stand therein, shall be a holy seed.
text.KJV: 6:13 ¶ But yet in it shalbe a tenth, and it shall returne, and shall be eaten: as a Teyle tree, and as an Oke whose substance is in them, when they cast their leaues: so the holy seede shall be the substance thereof.
text.NABRE: If there remain a tenth part in it, then this in turn shall be laid waste; As with a terebinth or an oak whose trunk remains when its leaves have fallen. Holy offspring is the trunk.

text.NRSV: In the days of Ahaz son of Jotham son of Uzziah, king of Judah, King Rezin of Aram and King Pekah son of Remaliah of Israel went up to attack Jerusalem, but could not mount an attack against it.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the days of Ahaz son of Jotham son of Uzziah king of Judah, that Rezin king of Syria, and Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel, came up, to Jerusalem, to war against it, but could not prevail against it.
text.DRB: And it came to pass in the days of Achaz the son of Joathan, the son of Ozias, king of Juda, that Rasin king of Syria, and Phacee the son of Romelia king of Israel, came up to Jerusalem, to fight against it: but they could not prevail over it.
text.KJV: 7:1 And it came to passe in the dayes of Ahaz the sonne of Iotham, the sonne of Uzziah king of Iudah, that Rezin the king of Syria, and Pekah, the sonne of Remaliah king of Israel, went vp towards Ierusalem to warre against it, but could not preuaile against it.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 The Syro-Ephraimite War Crisis in Judah. In the days of Ahaz, king of Judah, son of Jotham, son of Uzziah, Rezin, king of Aram, and Pekah, king of Israel, son of Remaliah, went up to attack Jerusalem, but they were not able to conquer it.

text.NRSV: When the house of David heard that Aram had allied itself with Ephraim, the heart of Ahaz and the heart of his people shook as the trees of the forest shake before the wind.
text.Rotherham: And it was told the house of David, saying, Syria hath settled down upon Ephraim. Then shook his heart, and the heart of his people, as the trees of a forest shake before a wind.
text.DRB: And they told the house of David, saying: Syria hath rested upon Ephraim, and his heart was moved, and the heart of his people, as the trees of the woods are moved with the wind.
text.KJV: 7:2 And it was told the house of Dauid, saying, Syria is confederate with Ephraim: and his heart was moued, and the heart of his people as the trees of the wood are mooued with the wind.
text.NABRE: When word came to the house of David that Aram had allied itself with Ephraim, the heart of the king and heart of the people trembled, as the trees of the forest tremble in the wind.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Isaiah, Go out to meet Ahaz, you and your son Shear-jashub, at the end of the conduit of the upper pool on the highway to the Fuller’s Field,
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh, unto Isaiah, Go forth, I pray thee, to meet Ahaz, thou, and Shear-jashub thy son,—unto the end of the channel of the upper pool, unto the highway of the fuller’s field;
text.DRB: And the Lord said to Isaias: Go forth to meet Achaz, thou and Jasub thy son that is left, to the conduit of the upper pool, in the way of the fuller's field.
text.KJV: 7:3 Then sayd the Lord vnto Isaiah; Goe forth now to meete Ahaz, thou, & Shear-iashub thy sonne, at the end of the conduit of the vpper poole in the high way of the fullers field.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Isaiah: Go out to meet Ahaz, you and your son Shear-jashub, at the end of the conduit of the upper pool, on the highway to the fuller’s field,

text.NRSV: and say to him, Take heed, be quiet, do not fear, and do not let your heart be faint because of these two smoldering stumps of firebrands, because of the fierce anger of Rezin and Aram and the son of Remaliah.
text.Rotherham: and say unto him—Take heed and keep thyself calm—do not fear, neither let thy heart be faint, because of these two fag-ends of smoking firebrands,—in spite of the glow of the anger of Rezin and Syria and the son of Remaliah.
text.DRB: And thou shalt say to him: See thou be quiet: fear not, and let not thy heart be afraid of the two tails of these fire brands, smoking with the wrath of the fury of Rasin king of Syria, and of the son of Romelia.
text.KJV: 7:4 And say vnto him; Take heede and be quiet: feare not, neither be faint hearted for the two tailes of these smoking firebrands, for the fierce anger of Rezin with Syria, and of the sonne of Remaliah.
text.NABRE: and say to him: Take care you remain calm and do not fear; do not let your courage fail before these two stumps of smoldering brands, the blazing anger of Rezin and the Arameans and of the son of Remaliah—

text.NRSV: Because Aram—with Ephraim and the son of Remaliah—has plotted evil against you, saying,
text.Rotherham: Because Syria, hath taken counsel against thee, for mischief,—[with] Ephraim and the son of Remaliah, saying,
text.DRB: Because Syria hath taken counsel against thee, unto the evil of Ephraim and the son of Romelia, saying:
text.KJV: 7:5 Because Syria, Ephraim, and the sonne of Remaliah haue taken euill counsell against thee, saying;
text.NABRE: because Aram, with Ephraim and the son of Remaliah, has planned evil against you. They say,

text.NRSV: Let us go up against Judah and cut off Jerusalem and conquer it for ourselves and make the son of Tabeel king in it;
text.Rotherham: Let us go up against Judah, and besiege it, and break it open, for ourselves,—and set up a king in the midst thereof, even the son of Tabeal,
text.DRB: Let us go up to Juda, and rouse it up, and draw it away to us, and make the son of Tabeel king in the midst thereof.
text.KJV: 7:6 Let vs goe vp against Iudah and vexe it, and let vs make a breach therein for vs, and set a king in the midst of it, euen the sonne of Tabeal.
text.NABRE: “Let us go up against Judah, tear it apart, make it our own by force, and appoint the son of Tabeel king there.”

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God: It shall not stand, and it shall not come to pass.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh,—It shall not stand Neither shall it come to pass!
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord God: It shall not stand, and this shall not be.
text.KJV: 7:7 Thus saith the Lord God; It shall not stand, neither shall it come to passe.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: It shall not stand, it shall not be!

text.NRSV: For the head of Aram is Damascus, and the head of Damascus is Rezin; within sixty-five years Ephraim will be shattered, no longer a people.
text.Rotherham: For, though the head of Syria is Damascus, And, the head of Damascus, is Rezin, Yet within threescore and five years more, shall Ephraim be broken that it shall not be a people;
text.DRB: But the head of Syria is Damascus, and the head of Damascus is Rasin: and within threescore and five years, Ephraim shall cease to be a people:
text.KJV: 7:8 For the head of Syria is Damascus, and the head of Damascus is Rezin, and within threescore and fiue yeeres shall Ephraim be broken, that it be not a people.
text.NABRE: The head of Aram is Damascus, and the head of Damascus is Rezin;

text.NRSV: The head of Ephraim is Samaria, and the head of Samaria is the son of Remaliah. If you do not stand firm in faith, you shall not stand at all.
text.Rotherham: Even though the head of Ephraim is Samaria, And, the head of Samaria, is the son of Remaliah. If ye trust not, Surely, ye cannot be trusted!
text.DRB: And the head of Ephraim is Samaria and the head of Samaria the son of Romelia. If you will not believe, you shall not continue.
text.KJV: 7:9 And the head of Ephraim is Samaria, and the head of Samaria is Remaliahs sonne: if yee will not beleeue, surely yee shall not be established.
text.NABRE: The head of Ephraim is Samaria, and the head of Samaria is the son of Remaliah. Within sixty-five years, Ephraim shall be crushed, no longer a nation. Unless your faith is firm, you shall not be firm!

text.NRSV: Again the Lord spoke to Ahaz, saying,
text.Rotherham: And again spake Yahweh unto Ahaz saying—
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke again to Achaz, saying:
text.KJV: 7:10 ¶ Moreouer the Lord spake againe vnto Ahaz, saying;
text.NABRE: Emmanuel. Again the Lord spoke to Ahaz:

text.NRSV: Ask a sign of the Lord your God; let it be deep as Sheol or high as heaven.
text.Rotherham: Ask thee a sign, of Yahweh thy God,—Go down deep for a request, Or ascend on high!
text.DRB: Ask thee a sign of the Lord thy God either unto the depth of hell, or unto the height above.
text.KJV: 7:11 Aske thee a signe of the Lord thy God; aske it either in the depth, or in the height aboue.
text.NABRE: Ask for a sign from the Lord, your God; let it be deep as Sheol, or high as the sky!

text.NRSV: But Ahaz said, I will not ask, and I will not put the Lord to the test.
text.Rotherham: But Ahaz said,—I will not ask Nor will I put Yahweh to the proof.
text.DRB: And Achaz said: I will not ask, and I will not tempt the Lord.
text.KJV: 7:12 But Ahaz sayd, I will not aske, neither will I tempt the Lord.
text.NABRE: But Ahaz answered, “I will not ask! I will not tempt the Lord!”

text.NRSV: Then Isaiah said: “Hear then, O house of David! Is it too little for you to weary mortals, that you weary my God also?
text.Rotherham: Then said he—Hear, I pray you, O house of David! Is it, too little, for you to weary men, that ye must weary even my God?
text.DRB: And he said: Hear ye therefore, O house of David: Is it a small thing for you to be grievous to men, that you are grievous to my God also?
text.KJV: 7:13 And he sayd; Heare yee now, O house of Dauid; Is it a small thing for you to wearie men, but will yee wearie my God also?
text.NABRE: Then he said: Listen, house of David! Is it not enough that you weary human beings? Must you also weary my God?

text.NRSV: Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign. Look, the young woman is with child and shall bear a son, and shall name him Immanuel.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore let My Lord Himself give you a sign,—Lo! a Virgin, being with child and giving birth to a son, thou wilt call his name Immanuel.
text.DRB: Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign. Behold a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and his name shall be called Emmanuel.
text.KJV: 7:14 Therefore the Lord himselfe shal giue you a signe: Behold, a Uirgine shall conceiue and beare a Sonne, and shall call his name Immanuel.
text.NABRE: Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign; the young woman, pregnant and about to bear a son, shall name him Emmanuel.

text.NRSV: He shall eat curds and honey by the time he knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good.
text.Rotherham: Curds and honey, shall he eat, by the time that he knoweth to refuse the bad and choose the good;
text.DRB: He shall eat butter and honey, that he may know to refuse the evil, and to choose the good.
text.KJV: 7:15 Butter and hony shall he eat, that hee may know to refuse the euill, and choose the good.
text.NABRE: Curds and honey he will eat so that he may learn to reject evil and choose good;

text.NRSV: For before the child knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good, the land before whose two kings you are in dread will be deserted.
text.Rotherham: for before the boy knoweth to refuse the bad and choose the good, forsaken shall be the land, at which, thou, art alarmed, of the presence of both her kings.
text.DRB: For before the child know to refuse the evil, and to choose the good, the land which thou abhorrest shall be forsaken of the face of her two kings.
text.KJV: 7:16 For before the childe shall know to refuse the euill and choose the good; the land that thou abhorrest, shalbe forsaken of both her kings.
text.NABRE: for before the child learns to reject evil and choose good, the land of those two kings whom you dread shall be deserted.

text.NRSV: The Lord will bring on you and on your people and on your ancestral house such days as have not come since the day that Ephraim departed from Judah—the king of Assyria.”
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will bring upon thee and upon thy people, and upon the house of thy father, days which have not come, from the day when Ephraim departed from Judah,—even the king of Assyria.
text.DRB: The Lord shall bring upon thee, and upon thy people, and upon the house of thy father, days that have not come since the time of the separation of Ephraim from Juda with the king of the Assyrians.
text.KJV: 7:17 ¶ The Lord shall bring vpon thee and vpon thy people, and vpon thy fathers house, dayes that haue not come, from the day that Ephraim departed from Iudah; euen the King of Assyria.
text.NABRE: The Lord shall bring upon you and your people and your father’s house such days as have not come since Ephraim seceded from Judah (the king of Assyria).

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord will whistle for the fly that is at the sources of the streams of Egypt, and for the bee that is in the land of Assyria.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day that Yahweh will give a signal—To the fly that is in the uttermost part of the Nile-canals of Egypt, And to the bee that is in the land of Assyria.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the Lord shall hiss for the fly, that is in the uttermost parts of the rivers of Egypt, and for the bee that is in the land of Assyria.
text.KJV: 7:18 And it shall come to passe in that day, that the Lord shall hisse for the flie, that is in the vttermost part of the riuers of Egypt, and for the Bee that is in the land of Assyria.
text.NABRE: On that day The Lord shall whistle for the fly in the farthest streams of Egypt, and for the bee in the land of Assyria.

text.NRSV: And they will all come and settle in the steep ravines, and in the clefts of the rocks, and on all the thornbushes, and on all the watering holes.
text.Rotherham: And they shall all of them come and settle down—In the desolate torrent-valleys, And in the rents of the crags,—And on all the thorn-bushes, And on all the pastures,
text.DRB: And they shall come, and shall all of them rest in the torrents of the valleys, and in the holes of the rocks, and upon all places set with shrubs, and in all hollow places.
text.KJV: 7:19 And they shall come, and shall rest all of them in the desolate valleys, and in the holes of the rockes, and vpon all thornes, and vpon all bushes.
text.NABRE: All of them shall come and settle in the steep ravines and in the rocky clefts, on all thornbushes and in all pastures.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord will shave with a razor hired beyond the River—with the king of Assyria—the head and the hair of the feet, and it will also take off the beard.
text.Rotherham: In that day, will My Lord shave, with a hired razor, even with them of the lands over the River Euphrates, with the king of Assyria, the head and the hair of the feet,—yea, even the beard, will it sweep off.
text.DRB: In that day the Lord shall shave with a razor that is hired by them that are beyond the river, by the king of the Assyrians, the head and the hairs of the feet, and the whole beard.
text.KJV: 7:20 In the same day shall the Lord shaue with a rasor that is hired, namely by them beyond the riuer, by the king of Assyria, the head, and the haire of the feet: and it shal also consume the beard.
text.NABRE: On that day the Lord shall shave with the razor hired from across the River (the king of Assyria) the head, and the hair of the feet; it shall also shave off the beard.

text.NRSV: On that day one will keep alive a young cow and two sheep,
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, that a man shall keep alive a young cow and two sheep.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass in that day, that a man shall nourish a young cow, and two sheep.
text.KJV: 7:21 And it shall come to passe in that day, that a man shal nourish a yong cow and two sheepe.
text.NABRE: On that day a man shall keep alive a young cow or a couple of sheep,

text.NRSV: and will eat curds because of the abundance of milk that they give; for everyone that is left in the land shall eat curds and honey.
text.Rotherham: Yea it shall come to pass for the abundance of the yield of milk, that he shall eat curds,—for, curds and honey, shall every one eat that is left in the midst of the land.
text.DRB: And for the abundance of milk he shall eat butter: for butter and honey shall every one eat that shall be left in the midst of the land.
text.KJV: 7:22 And it shall come to passe, for the abundance of milke that they shall giue, he shal eate butter: for butter and hony shall euery one eate, that is left in the land.
text.NABRE: and from their abundant yield of milk he shall eat curds; curds and honey shall be the food of all who are left in the land.

text.NRSV: On that day every place where there used to be a thousand vines, worth a thousand silver shekels, will become briers and thorns.
text.Rotherham: And it shall some to pass in that day, that, every place wherein there used to be a thousand vines at a thousand pieces of silver,—yea, even for briars and thorns, shall it be.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass in that day, that every place where there were a thousand vines, at a thousand pieces of silver, shall become thorns and briers.
text.KJV: 7:23 And it shall come to passe in that day, that euery place shalbe, where there were a thousand Uines at a thousand siluerlings, it shall euen be for briers and thornes.
text.NABRE: On that day every place where there were a thousand vines worth a thousand pieces of silver shall become briers and thorns.

text.NRSV: With bow and arrows one will go there, for all the land will be briers and thorns;
text.Rotherham: With arrows and with a bow, shall one come in thither,—for briars and thorns, shall be all the land.
text.DRB: With arrows and with bows they shall go in thither: for briars and thorns shall be in all the land.
text.KJV: 7:24 With arrowes and with bowes shall men come thither; because all the land shall become briars and thornes.
text.NABRE: One shall have to go there with bow and arrows, for all the country shall be briers and thorns.

text.NRSV: and as for all the hills that used to be hoed with a hoe, you will not go there for fear of briers and thorns; but they will become a place where cattle are let loose and where sheep tread.
text.Rotherham: But all the hills which, with the hoe, can be weeded,—there shall not come thither, the fear of briars and thorns,—but it shall be for the sending forth of oxen, and for the tread of lesser cattle.
text.DRB: And as for all the hills that shall be raked with a rake, the fear of thorns and briers shall not come thither, but they shall be for the ox to feed on, and the lesser cattle to tread upon.
text.KJV: 7:25 And on all hilles that shalbe digged with the mattocke, there shall not come thither the feare of briars and thornes: but it shall bee for the sending foorth of oxen, and for the treading of lesser cattell.
text.NABRE: But as for all the hills which were hoed with a mattock, for fear of briers and thorns you will not go there; they shall become a place for cattle to roam and sheep to trample.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to me, Take a large tablet and write on it in common characters, “Belonging to Maher-shalal-hash-baz,”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto me: Take thee a large tablet,—and write thereon in plain characters, To Maher-shalal-hash-baz. ["Speed-spoil-hurry-prey".]
text.DRB: And the Lord said to me: Take thee a great book, and write in it with a man's pen. Take away the spoils with speed, quickly take the prey.
text.KJV: 8:1 Moreouer the Lord said vnto mee, Take thee a great roule, and write in it with a mans penne, concerning Maher-shalal-hash-baz.
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 A Son of Isaiah. The Lord said to me: Take a large tablet, and inscribe on it with an ordinary stylus, “belonging to Maher-shalal-hash-baz,”

text.NRSV: and have it attested for me by reliable witnesses, the priest Uriah and Zechariah son of Jeberechiah.
text.Rotherham: That I may take in attestation, faithful witnesses,—even Uriah the priest, and Zechariah son of Jeberechiah.
text.DRB: And I took unto me faithful witnesses, Urias the priest, and Zacharias the son of Barachias.
text.KJV: 8:2 And I tooke vnto mee faithfull witnesses to record, Uriah the Priest, and Zechariah the sonne of Ieberechiah.
text.NABRE: and call reliable witnesses for me, Uriah the priest, and Zechariah, son of Jeberechiah.

text.NRSV: And I went to the prophetess, and she conceived and bore a son. Then the Lord said to me, Name him Maher-shalal-hash-baz;
text.Rotherham: Then approached I unto the prophetess, and she conceived and bare a son,—and Yahweh said unto me, Call his name Maher-shalal-hash-baz.
text.DRB: And I went to the prophetess, and she conceived, and bore a son. And the Lord said to me: Call his name, Hasten to take away the spoils: Make haste to take away the prey.
text.KJV: 8:3 And I went vnto the Prophetesse, and shee conceiued and bare a sonne, then said the Lord to mee, Call his name Maher-shalal-hash-baz.
text.NABRE: Then I went to the prophetess and she conceived and bore a son. The Lord said to me: Name him Maher-shalal-hash-baz,

text.NRSV: for before the child knows how to call “My father” or “My mother,” the wealth of Damascus and the spoil of Samaria will be carried away by the king of Assyria.
text.Rotherham: For before the boy shall have knowledge to cry, My father! and, My mother! the riches of Damascus and the spoil of Samaria shall be carried away before the king of Assyria.
text.DRB: For before the child know to call his father and his mother, the strength of Damascus, and the spoils of Samaria shall be taken away before the king of the Assyrians.
text.KJV: 8:4 For before the childe shall haue knowledge to cry, My father and my mother, the riches of Damascus, and the spoile of Samaria shalbe taken away before the king of Assyria.
text.NABRE: for before the child learns to say, “My father, my mother,” the wealth of Damascus and the spoils of Samaria shall be carried off by the king of Assyria.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to me again:
text.Rotherham: And again Yahweh spake unto me yet further saying:
text.DRB: And the Lord spoke to me again, saying:
text.KJV: 8:5 ¶ The Lord spake also vnto me againe, saying,
text.NABRE: The Choice: The Lord or Assyria. Again the Lord spoke to me:

text.NRSV: Because this people has rejected the waters of Shiloah that flow gently, and melts with fear before Rezin and the son of Remaliah;
text.Rotherham: Because this people hath refused the waters of Shiloah which flow softly,—and are rejoicing with Rezin and the son of Remaliah,
text.DRB: Forasmuch as this people hath cast away the waters of Siloe, that go with silence, and hath rather taken Rasin, and the son of Romelia:
text.KJV: 8:6 For so much as this people refuseth the waters of Shiloah that goe softly, and reioyce in Rezin, and Remaliahs sonne:
text.NABRE: Because this people has rejected the waters of Shiloah that flow gently, And melts with fear at the display of Rezin and Remaliah’s son,

text.NRSV: therefore, the Lord is bringing up against it the mighty flood waters of the River, the king of Assyria and all his glory; it will rise above all its channels and overflow all its banks;
text.Rotherham: now therefore, lo! the Lord is about to bring up against them the waters, strong and many, of the River [="Euphrates"], even the king of Assyria and all his glory,—and he shall rise over all his channels, and flow over all his banks;
text.DRB: Therefore behold the Lord will bring upon them the waters of the river strong and many, the king of the Assyrians, and all his glory: and he shall come up over all his channels, and shall overflow all his banks,
text.KJV: 8:7 Now therefore behold, the Lord bringeth vp vpon them the waters of the riuer strong and many, euen the king of Assyria, and all his glory: and he shall come vp ouer all his channels, and goe ouer all his bankes.
text.NABRE: Therefore the Lord is bringing up against them the waters of the River, great and mighty, the king of Assyria and all his glory. It shall rise above all its channels, and overflow all its banks.

text.NRSV: it will sweep on into Judah as a flood, and, pouring over, it will reach up to the neck; and its outspread wings will fill the breadth of your land, O Immanuel.
text.Rotherham: and roll on throughout Judah—overflow and pass along, till unto the neck, he shall reach,—and it shall be, that, the stretching out of his wings, shall fill the breadth of thy land, O Immanuel!
text.DRB: And shall pass through Juda, overflowing, and going over shall reach even to the neck. And the stretching out of his wings shall fill the breadth of thy land, O Emmanuel.
text.KJV: 8:8 And hee shall passe through Iudah, he shall ouerflow and goe ouer, he shall reach euen to the necke; and the stretching out of his wings shall fill the breadth of thy land, O Immanuel.
text.NABRE: It shall roll on into Judah, it shall rage and pass on— up to the neck it shall reach. But his outspread wings will fill the width of your land, Emmanuel!

text.NRSV: Band together, you peoples, and be dismayed; listen, all you far countries; gird yourselves and be dismayed; gird yourselves and be dismayed!
text.Rotherham: Rage, O ye peoples,—and be overthrown, And give ear, all ye distant parts of the earth,—Gird yourselves and be overthrown, Gird yourselves, and be overthrown:
text.DRB: Gather yourselves together, O ye people, and be overcome, and give ear, all ye lands afar off: strengthen yourselves, and be overcome, gird yourselves, and be overcome.
text.KJV: 8:9 ¶ Associate your selues, O ye people, and yee shalbe broken in pieces; and giue eare all ye of farre countreys: gird your selues, and ye shalbe broken in pieces; gird your selues, and ye shalbe broken in pieces.
text.NABRE: Band together, O peoples, but be shattered! Give ear, all you distant lands! Arm yourselves, but be shattered! Arm yourselves, but be shattered!

text.NRSV: Take counsel together, but it shall be brought to naught; speak a word, but it will not stand, for God is with us.
text.Rotherham: Determine a plan,—and it shall be frustrated,—Speak a word, and it shall not stand, For With-us-is-GOD!
text.DRB: Take counsel together, and it shall be defeated: speak a word, and it shall not be done: because God is with us.
text.KJV: 8:10 Take counsell together, and it shall come to nought: speake the word, and it shall not stand; for God is with vs.
text.NABRE: Form a plan, it shall be thwarted; make a resolve, it shall not be carried out, for “With us is God!”

text.NRSV: For the Lord spoke thus to me while his hand rested hard on me, and warned me not to walk in the way of this people, saying:
text.Rotherham: For, thus, spake Yahweh unto me like a firm grasp of the hand,—when he admonished me, not to walk in the way of this people, saying:
text.DRB: For thus saith the Lord to me: As he hath taught me, with a strong arm, that I should not walk in the way of this people, saying:
text.KJV: 8:11 ¶ For the Lord spake thus to me with a strong hand, and instructed me that I should not walke in the way of this people, saying,
text.NABRE: Disciples of Isaiah. For thus said the Lord —his hand strong upon me—warning me not to walk in the way of this people:

text.NRSV: Do not call conspiracy all that this people calls conspiracy, and do not fear what it fears, or be in dread.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not say—A confederacy! of everything of which this people may say. A confederacy! And their fear, shall ye not fear or regard as awful:
text.DRB: Say ye not: A conspiracy: for all that this people speaketh, is a conspiracy: neither fear ye their fear, nor be afraid.
text.KJV: 8:12 Say ye not, A confederacie to all them, to whom this people shall say, A confederacie; neither feare yee their feare, nor be afraid.
text.NABRE: Do not call conspiracy what this people calls conspiracy, nor fear what they fear, nor feel dread.

text.NRSV: But the Lord of hosts, him you shall regard as holy; let him be your fear, and let him be your dread.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh of hosts, him, shall ye hallow,—And let, him, be your fear, and let, him inspire you with awe;
text.DRB: Sanctify the Lord of hosts himself: and let him be your fear, and let him be your dread.
text.KJV: 8:13 Sanctifie the Lord of hostes himselfe, and let him bee your feare, and let him be your dread.
text.NABRE: But conspire with the Lord of hosts; he shall be your fear, he shall be your dread.

text.NRSV: He will become a sanctuary, a stone one strikes against; for both houses of Israel he will become a rock one stumbles over— a trap and a snare for the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: So shall he become a hallowed asylum,—But a stone to strike against, and a rock to stumble over unto both houses of Israel A trap and a snare to the dweller in Jerusalem;
text.DRB: And he shall be a sanctification to you. But for a stone of stumbling, and for a rock of offence to the two houses of Israel, for a snare and a ruin to the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 8:14 And he shalbe for a sanctuary; but for a stone of stumbling and for a rocke of offence to both the houses of Israel, for a ginne, and for a snare to the inhabitants of Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: He shall be a snare, a stone for injury, A rock for stumbling to both the houses of Israel, A trap and a snare to those who dwell in Jerusalem;

text.NRSV: And many among them shall stumble; they shall fall and be broken; they shall be snared and taken.
text.Rotherham: And many, shall stumble among them,—and fall and be torn, and snared, and captured.
text.DRB: And very many of them shall stumble and fall, and shall be broken in pieces, and shall be snared, and taken.
text.KJV: 8:15 And many among them shall stumble and fall, and be broken, and be snared, and be taken.
text.NABRE: And many among them shall stumble; fallen and broken; snared and captured.

text.NRSV: Bind up the testimony, seal the teaching among my disciples.
text.Rotherham: Bind thou up the testimony,—Seal the instruction amongst my disciples.
text.DRB: Bind up the testimony, seal the law among my disciples.
text.KJV: 8:16 Binde vp the Testimonie, seale the Law among my disciples.
text.NABRE: Bind up my testimony, seal the instruction with my disciples.

text.NRSV: I will wait for the Lord, who is hiding his face from the house of Jacob, and I will hope in him.
text.Rotherham: I will therefore long for Yahweh, Who is hiding his face from the house of Jacob,—And will wait, for him.
text.DRB: And I will wait for the Lord, who hath hid his face from the house of Jacob, and I will look for him.
text.KJV: 8:17 And I wil wait vpon the Lord that hideth his face from the house of Iacob, and I will looke for him.
text.NABRE: I will trust in the Lord, who is hiding his face from the house of Jacob; yes, I will wait for him.

text.NRSV: See, I and the children whom the Lord has given me are signs and portents in Israel from the Lord of hosts, who dwells on Mount Zion.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I and the children whom Yahweh hath given me, are for signs and for wonders in Israel,—from Yahweh of hosts, who is making his habitation in Mount Zion.
text.DRB: Behold I and my children, whom the Lord hath given me for a sign, and for a wonder in Israel from the Lord of hosts, who dwelleth in mount Sion.
text.KJV: 8:18 Behold, I, and the children whom the Lord hath giuen me, are for signes, and for wonders in Israel: from the Lord of hostes, which dwelleth in mount Zion.
text.NABRE: Here am I and the children whom the Lord has given me: we are signs and portents in Israel from the Lord of hosts, who dwells on Mount Zion.

text.NRSV: Now if they say to you, “Consult the ghosts and the familiar spirits that chirp and mutter; should not a people consult its gods, the dead on behalf of the living, for teaching and for instruction?”
text.Rotherham: But, when they say unto you—Seek ye unto the necromancers and unto the wizards, who chirp, and who mutter, Should not a people seek, unto its God? In behalf of the living, [should it seek] unto the dead?
text.DRB: And when they shall say to you: Seek of pythons , and of diviners, who mutter in their enchantments: should not the people seek of their God, for the living of the dead ?
text.KJV: 8:19 ¶ And when they shall say vnto you; Seeke vnto them that haue familiar spirits, and vnto wizards that peepe and that mutter: should not a people seeke vnto their God? for the liuing, to the dead?
text.NABRE: And when they say to you, “Inquire of ghosts and soothsayers who chirp and mutter; should not a people inquire of their gods, consulting the dead on behalf of the living,

text.NRSV: Surely, for such a word as this, there is no dawn!
text.Rotherham: To the law and to the testimony! If they speak not according to this word, [It is], because they have no dawning day.
text.DRB: To the law rather, and to the testimony. And if they speak not according to this word, they shall not have the morning light.
text.KJV: 8:20 To the Law and to the Testimonie: if they speake not according to this word, it is because there is no light in them.
text.NABRE: for instruction and testimony?” Surely, those who speak like this are the ones for whom there is no dawn.

text.NRSV: They will pass through the land, greatly distressed and hungry; when they are hungry, they will be enraged and will curse their king and their God. They will turn their faces upward,
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall they pass through it hard pressed and hungry,—And it shall be when they hunger, then will they rage and revile their king and their god and turn their faces upwards;
text.DRB: And they shall pass by it, they shall fall, and be hungry: and when they shall be hungry, they will be angry, and curse their king, and their God, and look upwards.
text.KJV: 8:21 And they shall passe through it, hardly bestead and hungry: and it shall come to passe, that when they shall be hungry, they shall fret themselues, and curse their King, and their God, and looke vpward.
text.NABRE: He will pass through it hard-pressed and hungry, and when hungry, shall become enraged, and curse king and gods. He will look upward,

text.NRSV: or look to the earth, but will see only distress and darkness, the gloom of anguish; and they will be thrust into thick darkness.
text.Rotherham: And unto the land, shall they look hard, And lo! distress and darkness, the gloom of anguish, driven away!
text.DRB: And they shall look to the earth, and behold trouble and darkness, weakness and distress, and a mist following them, and they cannot fly away from their distress.
text.KJV: 8:22 And they shall looke vnto the earth: and behold trouble and darkenesse, dimnesse of anguish; and they shall be driuen to darkenesse.
text.NABRE: and will gaze at the earth, But will see only distress and darkness, oppressive gloom, murky, without light. The Promise of Salvation Under a New Davidic King.

text.NRSV: But there will be no gloom for those who were in anguish. In the former time he brought into contempt the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, but in the latter time he will make glorious the way of the sea, the land beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the nations.
text.Rotherham: For there is no gloom to her who had been in anguish, In the former time, he brought into dishonour The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, In the latter time, hath he brought into honour The Lake-way over the Jordan, Galilee of the nations.
text.DRB: At the first time the land of Zabulon, and the land of Nephtali was lightly touched: and at the last the way of the sea beyond the Jordan of the Galilee of the Gentiles was heavily loaded.
text.KJV: 9:1 Neuerthelesse the dimnesse shall not be such as was in her vexation; when at the first he lightly afflicted the land of Zebulun, and the land of Naphtali, and afterward did more grieuously afflict her by the way of the Sea, beyond Iordan in Galile of the nations.
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 The people who walked in darkness have seen a great light; Upon those who lived in a land of gloom a light has shone.

text.NRSV: The people who walked in darkness have seen a great light; those who lived in a land of deep darkness— on them light has shined.
text.Rotherham: the people who were walking in darkness, Have seen a great light,—The dwellers in a land death-shadowed, A light, hath shined upon them.
text.DRB: The people that walked in darkness, have seen a great light: to them that dwelt in the region of the shadow of death, light is risen.
text.KJV: 9:2 The people that walked in darknesse, haue seene a great light: they that dwel in the land of the shadow of death, vpon them hath the light shined.
text.NABRE: You have brought them abundant joy and great rejoicing; They rejoice before you as people rejoice at harvest, as they exult when dividing the spoils.

text.NRSV: You have multiplied the nation, you have increased its joy; they rejoice before you as with joy at the harvest, as people exult when dividing plunder.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast increased the exultation Thou hast made great the joy,—They joy before thee, according to the joy of harvest, As men exult when they distribute spoil.
text.DRB: Thou hast multiplied the nation, and hast not increased the joy. They shall rejoice before thee, as they that rejoice in the harvest, as conquerors rejoice after taking a prey, when they divide the spoils.
text.KJV: 9:3 Thou hast multiplied the nation, and not increased the ioy: they ioy before thee, according to the ioy in haruest, and as men reioyce when they diuide the spoile.
text.NABRE: For the yoke that burdened them, the pole on their shoulder, The rod of their taskmaster, you have smashed, as on the day of Midian.

text.NRSV: For the yoke of their burden, and the bar across their shoulders, the rod of their oppressor, you have broken as on the day of Midian.
text.Rotherham: For, the yoke of their burden, The cross-bar of their shoulder, The goad of their driver, Hast thou broken, as in the day of Midian.
text.DRB: For the yoke of their burden, and the rod of their shoulder, and the sceptre of their oppressor thou hast overcome, as in the day of Median.
text.KJV: 9:4 For thou hast broken the yoke of his burden, and the staffe of his shoulder, the rod of his oppressour, as in the day of Midian.
text.NABRE: For every boot that tramped in battle, every cloak rolled in blood, will be burned as fuel for fire.

text.NRSV: For all the boots of the tramping warriors and all the garments rolled in blood shall be burned as fuel for the fire.
text.Rotherham: Surely, every boot of one tramping in tumult, and the war-cloak rolled in blood Then shall serve for burning, food for fire;
text.DRB: For every violent taking of spoils, with tumult, and garment mingled with blood, shall be burnt, and be fuel for the fire.
text.KJV: 9:5 For euery battell of the warriour is with confused noise, and garments rolled in blood; but this shall be with burning and fewell of fire.
text.NABRE: For a child is born to us, a son is given to us; upon his shoulder dominion rests. They name him Wonder-Counselor, God-Hero, Father-Forever, Prince of Peace.

text.NRSV: For a child has been born for us, a son given to us; authority rests upon his shoulders; and he is named Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.
text.Rotherham: For, A Child, hath been born to us, A Son, hath been given to us, And the dominion is upon his shoulder,—And his Name hath been called Wonderful Counsellor, Mighty GOD, Father of Futurity, Prince of Prosperity.
text.DRB: For a CHILD IS BORN to us, and a son is given to us, and the government is upon his shoulder: and his name shall be called, Wonderful, Counsellor, God the Mighty, the Father of the world to come, the Prince of Peace.
text.KJV: 9:6 For vnto vs a child is borne, vnto vs a Sonne is giuen, and the gouernment shalbe vpon his shoulder: and his name shalbe called, Wonderfull, Counseller, The mightie God, The euerlasting Father, The Prince of peace.
text.NABRE: His dominion is vast and forever peaceful, Upon David’s throne, and over his kingdom, which he confirms and sustains By judgment and justice, both now and forever. The zeal of the Lord of hosts will do this!

text.NRSV: His authority shall grow continually, and there shall be endless peace for the throne of David and his kingdom. He will establish and uphold it with justice and with righteousness from this time forth and forevermore. The zeal of the Lord of hosts will do this.
text.Rotherham: Of the increase of dominion, and of prosperity, There shall be no end—Upon the throne of David and Upon his kingdom, By establishing it and By sustaining it, With justice and With righteousness,—From henceforth, Even unto times age-abiding: The jealousy of Yahweh of hosts, will perform this!
text.DRB: His empire shall be multiplied, and there shall be no end of peace: he shall sit upon the throne of David, and upon his kingdom; to establish it and strengthen it with judgment and with justice, from henceforth and for ever: the zeal of the Lord of hosts will perform this.
text.KJV: 9:7 Of the increase of his gouernment and peace there shall be no end, vpon the throne of Dauid & vpon his kingdome, to order it, and to stablish it with iudgement and with iustice, from henceforth euen for euer: the zeale of the Lord of hostes will performe this.
text.NABRE: Judgment on the Northern Kingdom The Lord has sent a word against Jacob, and it falls upon Israel;

text.NRSV: The Lord sent a word against Jacob, and it fell on Israel;
text.Rotherham: A word, hath My Lord, sent unto Jacob,—And it shall alight on Israel;
text.DRB: The Lord sent a word into Jacob, and it hath lighted upon Israel.
text.KJV: 9:8 ¶ The Lord sent a word into Iacob, and it hath lighted vpon Israel.
text.NABRE: And all the people know it— Ephraim and those who dwell in Samaria— those who say in arrogance and pride of heart,

text.NRSV: and all the people knew it— Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria— who said in pride and arrogance of heart:
text.Rotherham: and the people shall all of them know [it] —Ephraim and the dweller in Samaria—Who in pride and insolence of heart, are saying:
text.DRB: And all the people of Ephraim shall know, and the inhabitants of Samaria that say in the pride and haughtiness of their heart:
text.KJV: 9:9 And all the people shal know, euen Ephraim and the inhabitant of Samaria, that say in the pride and stoutnesse of heart;
text.NABRE: “Bricks have fallen, but we will rebuild with cut stone; Sycamores have been felled, but we will replace them with cedars.”

text.NRSV: “The bricks have fallen, but we will build with dressed stones; the sycamores have been cut down, but we will put cedars in their place.”
text.Rotherham: Bricks, have fallen down But with hewn stone, will we build,—Sycomores, have been felled, But with cedars, will we replace them.
text.DRB: The bricks are fallen down, but we will build with square stones: they have cut down the sycamores, but we will change them for cedars.
text.KJV: 9:10 The brickes are fallen downe, but we will build with hewen stones: the Sycomores are cut downe, but we will change them into Cedars.
text.NABRE: So the Lord raises up their foes against them and stirs up their enemies to action—

text.NRSV: So the Lord raised adversaries against them, and stirred up their enemies,
text.Rotherham: Therefore will Yahweh strengthen the adversaries of Resin against him,—And his enemies will he arouse:
text.DRB: And the Lord shall set up the enemies of Rasin over him, and shall bring on his enemies in a crowd:
text.KJV: 9:11 Therefore the Lord shall set vp the aduersaries of Rezin against him, and ioyne his enemies together.
text.NABRE: Aram from the east and the Philistines from the west— they devour Israel with open mouth. For all this, his wrath is not turned back, and his hand is still outstretched!

text.NRSV: the Arameans on the east and the Philistines on the west, and they devoured Israel with open mouth. For all this his anger has not turned away, and his hand is stretched out still.
text.Rotherham: Syrians before and Philistines behind, Thus have they devoured Israel with open mouth,—For all this, hath his anger, not turned back, But still, is his hand outstretched.
text.DRB: The Syrians from the east, and the Philistines from the west: and they shall devour Israel with open mouth, For all this his indignation is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
text.KJV: 9:12 The Syrians before, and the Philistines behinde, and they shall deuoure Israel with open mouth: for all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
text.NABRE: The people do not turn back to the one who struck them, nor do they seek the Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: The people did not turn to him who struck them, or seek the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Yet, the people, have not turned unto him that smote them,—And Yahweh of hosts, have they not sought.
text.DRB: And the people are not returned to him who hath struck them, and have not sought after the Lord of hosts.
text.KJV: 9:13 ¶ For the people turneth not vnto him that smiteth them, neither doe they seeke the Lord of hostes.
text.NABRE: So the Lord cuts off from Israel head and tail, palm branch and reed in one day.

text.NRSV: So the Lord cut off from Israel head and tail, palm branch and reed in one day—
text.Rotherham: Therefore hath Yahweh cut off from Israel—Head and tail Palm-top and rush, In one day.
text.DRB: And the Lord shall destroy out of Israel the head and the tail, him that bendeth down, and him that holdeth back, in one day.
text.KJV: 9:14 Therefore the Lord will cut off from Israel head and taile, branch and rush in one day.
text.NABRE: (The elder and the noble are the head, the prophet who teaches falsehood is the tail.)

text.NRSV: elders and dignitaries are the head, and prophets who teach lies are the tail;
text.Rotherham: The elder and favourite, he, is the head,—And the prophet teaching falsehood, he, is the tail;
text.DRB: The aged and honourable, he is the head: and the prophet that teacheth lies, he is the tail.
text.KJV: 9:15 The ancient and honourable, hee is the head: and the prophet that teacheth lies, he is the taile.
text.NABRE: Those who lead this people lead them astray, and those who are led are swallowed up.

text.NRSV: for those who led this people led them astray, and those who were led by them were left in confusion.
text.Rotherham: And they who should have led this people forward have been causing them to stray,—And, they who are led of them are destroyed,
text.DRB: And they that call this people blessed, shall cause them to err: and they that are called blessed, shall be thrown down headlong.
text.KJV: 9:16 For the leaders of this people cause them to erre, and they that are ledde of them, are destroyed.
text.NABRE: That is why the Lord does not spare their young men, and their orphans and widows he does not pity; For they are totally impious and wicked, and every mouth speaks folly. For all this, his wrath is not turned back, his hand is still outstretched!

text.NRSV: Therefore the Lord did not have pity on their young people, or compassion on their orphans and widows; for everyone was godless and an evildoer, and every mouth spoke folly. For all this his anger has not turned away, and his hand is stretched out still.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, over their choice young men, will My Lord not rejoice, And on their fatherless and their widows, will he not have compassion, For every one of them, is profane and an evildoer, And, every mouth is speaking baseness,—For all this, hath his anger, not turned back, But still, is his hand outstretched.
text.DRB: Therefore the Lord shall have no joy in their young men: neither shall he have mercy on their fatherless, and widows: for every one is a hypocrite and wicked, and every mouth hath spoken folly. For all this his indignation is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
text.KJV: 9:17 Therfore the Lord shall haue no ioy in their yong men, neither shall haue mercy on their fatherlesse & widowes: for euery one is an hypocrite, and an euil doer, and euery mouth speaketh folly: for all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
text.NABRE: For wickedness burns like fire, devouring brier and thorn; It kindles the forest thickets, which go up in columns of smoke.

text.NRSV: For wickedness burned like a fire, consuming briers and thorns; it kindled the thickets of the forest, and they rolled up in a column of smoke.
text.Rotherham: For lawlessness, hath consumed like fire, Briars and thorns, doth it devour,—Yea it hath kindled upon the thickets of the forest, And they have rolled up as a column of smoke.
text.DRB: For wickedness is kindled as a fire, it shall devour the brier and the thorn: and shall kindle in the thicket of the forest, and it shall be wrapped up in smoke ascending on high.
text.KJV: 9:18 ¶ For wickednes burneth as the fire: it shall deuoure the briers and thornes, and shall kindle in the thickets of the forrest, and they shall mount vp like the lifting vp of smoke.
text.NABRE: At the wrath of the Lord of hosts the land quakes, and the people are like fuel for fire; no one spares his brother.

text.NRSV: Through the wrath of the Lord of hosts the land was burned, and the people became like fuel for the fire; no one spared another.
text.Rotherham: Through the wrath of Yahweh of hosts, is the land consumed,—And the people have come to be, as fuel for fire, A man unto his own brother, sheweth not pity;
text.DRB: By the wrath of the Lord of hosts the land is troubled, and the people shall be as fuel for the fire: no man shall spare his brother.
text.KJV: 9:19 Through the wrath of the Lord of hosts is the land darkened, and the people shall be as the fuell of the fire: no man shall spare his brother.
text.NABRE: They hack on the right, but remain hungry; they devour on the left, but are not filled. Each devours the flesh of the neighbor;

text.NRSV: They gorged on the right, but still were hungry, and they devoured on the left, but were not satisfied; they devoured the flesh of their own kindred;
text.Rotherham: And he hath slain on the right, and yet is hungry, And hath eaten on the left, yet are they not satisfied,—Every one, the flesh of his own arm, will they eat:
text.DRB: And he shall turn to the right hand, and shall be hungry: and shall eat on the left hand, and shall not be filled: every one shall eat the flesh of his own arm: Manasses Ephraim, and Ephraim Manasses, and they together shall be against Juda.
text.KJV: 9:20 And he shall snatch on the right hand, and be hungry, and he shall eate on the left hand, and they shall not bee satisfied: they shall eate euery man the flesh of his owne arme.
text.NABRE: Manasseh devours Ephraim, and Ephraim Manasseh, together they turn on Judah. For all this, his wrath is not turned back, his hand is still outstretched!

text.NRSV: Manasseh devoured Ephraim, and Ephraim Manasseh, and together they were against Judah. For all this his anger has not turned away, and his hand is stretched out still.
text.Rotherham: Manasseh—Ephraim, and Ephraim—Manasseh, They together—against Judah,—For all this, hath his anger not turned back, But still, is his hand outstretched.
text.DRB: After all these things his indignation is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
text.KJV: 9:21 Manasseh, Ephraim: and Ephraim, Manasseh: and they together shalbe against Iudah: for all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.

text.NRSV: Ah, you who make iniquitous decrees, who write oppressive statutes,
text.Rotherham: Alas! for them who ordain iniquitous decrees,—And, busy writers, who make a business of writing mischief:
text.DRB: Woe to them that make wicked laws: and when they write, write injustice:
text.KJV: 10:1 Woe vnto them that decree vnrighteous decrees, and that write grieuousnesse which they haue prescribed:
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 Perversion of Justice Ah! Those who enact unjust statutes, who write oppressive decrees,

text.NRSV: to turn aside the needy from justice and to rob the poor of my people of their right, that widows may be your spoil, and that you may make the orphans your prey!
text.Rotherham: Turning aside from judgment, the poor, And robbing, of justice, the oppressed of my people,—So that, widows, become, their spoil, And of the fatherless, they make prey.
text.DRB: To oppress the poor in judgment, and do violence to the cause of the humble of my people: that widows might be their prey, and that they might rob the fatherless.
text.KJV: 10:2 To turne aside the needy from iudgement, and to take away the right from the poore of my people, that widdowes may be their pray, and that they may robbe the fatherles.
text.NABRE: Depriving the needy of judgment, robbing my people’s poor of justice, Making widows their plunder, and orphans their prey!

text.NRSV: What will you do on the day of punishment, in the calamity that comes from far away? To whom will you flee for help, and where will you leave your wealth,
text.Rotherham: What, then, will ye do for the day of visitation, And for the devastation, which from afar, shall come? Unto whom, will ye flee for help? And where will ye leave your gory?
text.DRB: What will you do in the day of visitation, and of the calamity which cometh from afar? to whom will ye flee for help? and where will ye leave your glory?
text.KJV: 10:3 And what wil ye doe in the day of visitation, and in the desolation which shall come from farre? To whom wil ye flee for helpe? And where will yee leaue your glory?
text.NABRE: What will you do on the day of punishment, when the storm comes from afar? To whom will you flee for help? Where will you leave your wealth,

text.NRSV: so as not to crouch among the prisoners or fall among the slain? For all this his anger has not turned away, and his hand is stretched out still.
text.Rotherham: Without me, one hath bowed under a prisoner Yea under the slain, do they fall! For all this, hath his anger, not turned back, But still, is his hand outstretched.
text.DRB: That you be not bowed down under the bond, and fall with the slain? In all these things his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
text.KJV: 10:4 Without mee they shall bowe downe vnder the prisoners, and they shall fall vnder the slaine: for all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
text.NABRE: Lest it sink beneath the captive or fall beneath the slain? For all this, his wrath is not turned back, his hand is still outstretched!

text.NRSV: Ah, Assyria, the rod of my anger— the staff in their hands is my fury!
text.Rotherham: Alas! for Assyria, the rod of mine anger,—Yea, the very staff in their hand, is, my displeasure:
text.DRB: Woe to the Assyrian, he is the rod and the staff of my anger, and my indignation is in their hands.
text.KJV: 10:5 ¶ O Assyrian, the rod of mine anger, and the staffe in their hand is mine indignation.
text.NABRE: Judgment on Assyria Ah! Assyria, the rod of my wrath, the staff I wield in anger.

text.NRSV: Against a godless nation I send him, and against the people of my wrath I command him, to take spoil and seize plunder, and to tread them down like the mire of the streets.
text.Rotherham: Against an irreligious nation, will I send him, Yea against the people with whom I am wroth, will I command him,—To capture spoil And lay hold on prey, And cause them to be trodden down as the mire of the lanes.
text.DRB: I will send him to a deceitful nation, and I will give him a charge against the people of my wrath, to take away the spoils, and to lay hold on the prey, and to tread them down like the mire of the streets.
text.KJV: 10:6 I will send him against an hypocriticall nation, and against the people of my wrath will I giue him a charge to take the spoile, and to take the praye, and to tread them downe like the mire of the streets.
text.NABRE: Against an impious nation I send him, and against a people under my wrath I order him To seize plunder, carry off loot, and to trample them like the mud of the street.

text.NRSV: But this is not what he intends, nor does he have this in mind; but it is in his heart to destroy, and to cut off nations not a few.
text.Rotherham: But as for him, not so, doth he deem, And in his heart, not so, doth he think,—For, to destroy, is in his heart, And to cut off nations, not a few;
text.DRB: But he shall not take it so, and his heart shall not think so: but his heart shall be set to destroy, and to cut off nations not a few.
text.KJV: 10:7 Howbeit he meaneth not so, neither doth his heart thinke so, but it is in his heart to destroy, and cut off nations not a few.
text.NABRE: But this is not what he intends, nor does he have this in mind; Rather, it is in his heart to destroy, to make an end of not a few nations.

text.NRSV: For he says: “Are not my commanders all kings?
text.Rotherham: For he saith:—Are not, my generals, all alike, kings?
text.DRB: For he shall say:
text.KJV: 10:8 For he saith, Are not my princes altogether kings?
text.NABRE: For he says, “Are not my commanders all kings?”

text.NRSV: Is not Calno like Carchemish? Is not Hamath like Arpad? Is not Samaria like Damascus?
text.Rotherham: Is not, Calno, like Carchemish? Is not, Hamath, like Arpad? Is not, Samaria, like Damascus?
text.DRB: Are not my princes as so many kings? is not Calano as Charcamis: and Emath as Arphad? is not Samaria as Damascus?
text.KJV: 10:9 Is not Calno, as Carchemish? Is not Hamath, as Arpad? Is not Samaria, as Damascus?
text.NABRE: “Is not Calno like Carchemish, Or Hamath like Arpad, or Samaria like Damascus?

text.NRSV: As my hand has reached to the kingdoms of the idols whose images were greater than those of Jerusalem and Samaria,
text.Rotherham: As my hand hath reached unto the kingdoms of idols,—whose images did excel them of Jerusalem and Samaria,
text.DRB: As my hand hath found the kingdoms of the idol, so also their idols of Jerusalem, and of Samaria.
text.KJV: 10:10 As my hand hath found the kingdomes of the idoles, and whose grauen images did excell them of Ierusalem and of Samaria:
text.NABRE: Just as my hand reached out to idolatrous kingdoms that had more images than Jerusalem and Samaria—

text.NRSV: shall I not do to Jerusalem and her idols what I have done to Samaria and her images?”
text.Rotherham: Shall I not as I have done to Samaria and her idols, so, do to Jerusalem and her images?
text.DRB: Shall I not, as I have done to Samaria and her idols, so do to Jerusalem and her idols?
text.KJV: 10:11 Shall I not, as I haue done vnto Samaria and her idoles, so doe to Ierusalem and her idoles?
text.NABRE: Just as I treated Samaria and her idols, shall I not do to Jerusalem and her graven images?”

text.NRSV: When the Lord has finished all his work on Mount Zion and on Jerusalem, he will punish the arrogant boasting of the king of Assyria and his haughty pride.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore it shall come to pass—When My Lord shall finish his whole work upon Mount Zion and upon Jerusalem, I will bring punishment—Upon the fruit of the greatness of heart of the king of Assyria. And upon the vainglory of his uplifted eyes,
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass, that when the Lord shall have performed all his works in mount Sion, and in Jerusalem, I will visit the fruit of the proud heart of the king of Assyria, and the glory of the haughtiness of his eyes.
text.KJV: 10:12 Wherefore it shall come to passe, that when the Lord hath performed his whole worke vpon mount Zion, and on Ierusalem, I will punish the fruit of the stout heart of the king of Assyria, and the glory of his high lookes.
text.NABRE: But when the Lord has brought to an end all his work on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, I will punish the utterance of the king of Assyria’s proud heart, and the boastfulness of his haughty eyes.

text.NRSV: For he says: “By the strength of my hand I have done it, and by my wisdom, for I have understanding; I have removed the boundaries of peoples, and have scavenged their treasures; like a bull I have brought down those who sat on thrones.
text.Rotherham: For he hath said—By the strength of mine own hand, have I effectually wrought, And by my wisdom—for I have discernment,—That I might remove the bounds of peoples, Yea their treasures, have I plundered That I might lay prostrate as a mighty one the inhabitants;
text.DRB: For he hath said: By the strength of my own hand I have done it, and by my own wisdom I have understood: and I have removed the bounds of the people, and have taken the spoils of the princes, and as a mighty man hath pulled down them that sat on high.
text.KJV: 10:13 For hee saith, By the strength of my hand I haue done it, and by my wisedome, for I am prudent: and I haue remooued the bounds of the people, and haue robbed their treasures, and I haue put downe the inhabitants like a valiant man.
text.NABRE: For he says: “By my own power I have done it, and by my wisdom, for I am shrewd. I have moved the boundaries of peoples, their treasures I have pillaged, and, like a mighty one, I have brought down the enthroned.

text.NRSV: My hand has found like a nest the wealth of the peoples; and as one gathers eggs that have been left, so I have gathered all the earth; and there was none that moved a wing, or opened its mouth, or chirped.”
text.Rotherham: And my hand hath found as a nest the wealth of the peoples, And as the gathering of eggs that are forsaken, all the earth, have, I, gathered,—And there was none to flap a wing, or open a mouth or chirp.
text.DRB: And my hand hath found the strength of the people as a nest; and as eggs are gathered, that are left, so have I gathered all the earth: and there was none that moved the wing, or opened the mouth, or made the least noise.
text.KJV: 10:14 And my hand hath found as a nest the riches of the people: and as one gathereth egges that are left, haue I gathered all the earth, and there was none that moued the wing, or opened the mouth, or peeped.
text.NABRE: My hand has seized, like a nest, the wealth of nations. As one takes eggs left alone, so I took in all the earth; No one fluttered a wing, or opened a mouth, or chirped!”

text.NRSV: Shall the ax vaunt itself over the one who wields it, or the saw magnify itself against the one who handles it? As if a rod should raise the one who lifts it up, or as if a staff should lift the one who is not wood!
text.Rotherham: Shall the axe boast itself against him that heweth therewith? Or, the saw, magnify itself, against him that wieldeth it? As if a rod, could wield, them who lift it up! As if a staff, could raise, what is, not wood!
text.DRB: Shall the axe boast itself against him that cutteth with it? or shall the saw exalt itself against him by whom it is drawn? as if a rod should lift itself up against him that lifteth it up, and a staff exalt itself, which is but wood.
text.KJV: 10:15 Shall the axe boast it selfe against him that heweth therewith? Or shal the sawe magnifie it selfe against him that shaketh it? as if the rod should shake it selfe against them that lift it vp, or as if the staffe should lift vp it selfe, as if it were no wood.
text.NABRE: Will the ax boast against the one who hews with it? Will the saw exalt itself above the one who wields it? As if a rod could sway the one who lifts it, or a staff could lift the one who is not wood!

text.NRSV: Therefore the Sovereign, the Lord of hosts, will send wasting sickness among his stout warriors, and under his glory a burning will be kindled, like the burning of fire.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, shall the Lord, Yahweh of hosts, Send, among his fat ones, leanness, And under his glory, shall he kindle a kindling, like the kindling of fire;
text.DRB: Therefore the sovereign Lord, the Lord of hosts, shall send leanness among his fat ones: and under his glory shall be kindled a burning, as it were the burning of a fire.
text.KJV: 10:16 Therefore shall the Lord, the Lord of hosts, send among his fat ones leannesse, and vnder his glory hee shall kindle a burning, like the burning of a fire.
text.NABRE: Therefore the Lord, the Lord of hosts, will send leanness among his fat ones, And under his glory there will be a kindling like the kindling of fire.

text.NRSV: The light of Israel will become a fire, and his Holy One a flame; and it will burn and devour his thorns and briers in one day.
text.Rotherham: So shall, the Light of Israel, become, a fire, And his Holy One, a flame, Which shall burn and consume his thorns and his briars, in one day;
text.DRB: And the light of Israel shall be as a fire, and the Holy One thereof as a flame: and his thorns and his briers shall be set on fire, and shall be devoured in one day.
text.KJV: 10:17 And the light of Israel shall bee for a fire, and his Holy One for a flame: and it shall burne and deuoure his thornes and his briers in one day:
text.NABRE: The Light of Israel will become a fire, the Holy One, a flame, That burns and consumes its briers and its thorns in a single day.

text.NRSV: The glory of his forest and his fruitful land the Lord will destroy, both soul and body, and it will be as when an invalid wastes away.
text.Rotherham: And the glory of his forest and of his garden land, both soul and body, shall it bring to an end,—And it shall be like the wasting away of a consumptive.
text.DRB: And the glory of his forest, and of his beautiful hill, shall be consumed from the soul even to the flesh, and he shall run away through fear.
text.KJV: 10:18 And shall consume the glory of his forrest, and of his fruitfull field both soule and body: and they shall bee as when a standerd bearer fainteth.
text.NABRE: And the glory of its forests and orchards will be consumed, soul and body, and it will be like a sick man who wastes away.

text.NRSV: The remnant of the trees of his forest will be so few that a child can write them down.
text.Rotherham: And, the remnant of his forest-trees, few in number, shall become,—yea, a child, might describe them!
text.DRB: And they that remain of the trees of his forest shall be so few, that they shall easily be numbered, and a child shall write them down.
text.KJV: 10:19 And the rest of the trees of his forrest shall be few, that a child may write them.
text.NABRE: And the remnant of the trees in his forest will be so few, that any child can record them.
