text.NRSV: On that day the remnant of Israel and the survivors of the house of Jacob will no more lean on the one who struck them, but will lean on the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, in truth.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, That the remnant of Israel. And the escaped of the house of Jacob Shall, no more again, lean upon him that smote them,—But shall lean upon Yahweh the Holy One of Israel, in truth.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the remnant of Israel, and they that shall escape of the house of Jacob, shall lean no more upon him that striketh them: but they shall lean upon the Lord the Holy One of Israel, in truth.
text.KJV: 10:20 ¶ And it shal come to passe in that day, that the remnant of Israel, and such as are escaped of the house of Iacob, shall no more againe stay vpon him that smote them: but shall stay vpon the Lord, the Holy One of Israel in trueth.
text.NABRE: On that day The remnant of Israel, the survivors of the house of Jacob, will no more lean upon the one who struck them; But they will lean upon the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, in truth.

text.NRSV: A remnant will return, the remnant of Jacob, to the mighty God.
text.Rotherham: A remnant, shall return, The remnant of Jacob,—unto the mighty GOD;
text.DRB: The remnant shall be converted, the remnant, I say, of Jacob, to the mighty God.
text.KJV: 10:21 The remnant shall returne, euen the remnant of Iacob, vnto the mightie God.
text.NABRE: A remnant will return, the remnant of Jacob, to the mighty God.

text.NRSV: For though your people Israel were like the sand of the sea, only a remnant of them will return. Destruction is decreed, overflowing with righteousness.
text.Rotherham: For though thy people, O Israel shall be as the sand of the sea, A [mere] remnant, shall return of them,—A full end decreed, bringeth in, justice, like a flood;
text.DRB: For if thy people, O Israel, shall be as the sand of the sea, a remnant of them shall be converted , the consumption abridged shall overflow with justice.
text.KJV: 10:22 For though thy people Israel be as the sand of the sea, yet a remnant of them shall returne: the consumption decreed shall ouerflow with righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: Though your people, O Israel, were like the sand of the sea, Only a remnant of them will return; their destruction is decreed, as overflowing justice demands.

text.NRSV: For the Lord God of hosts will make a full end, as decreed, in the midst of all the earth.
text.Rotherham: For, a full end and that a decreed one, is My Lord Yahweh of hosts executing in the midst of all the earth.
text.DRB: For the Lord God of hosts shall make a consumption, and an abridgment in the midst of all the land.
text.KJV: 10:23 For the Lord God of hostes shall make a consumption, euen determined in the middest of all the land.
text.NABRE: For the Lord, the God of hosts, is about to carry out the destruction decreed in the midst of the whole land.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God of hosts: O my people, who live in Zion, do not be afraid of the Assyrians when they beat you with a rod and lift up their staff against you as the Egyptians did.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh of hosts, Do not fear O my people dwelling in Zion, because of Assyria,—When with his rod, he would smite thee, And when his staff, he would lift up against thee in the manner of Egypt;
text.DRB: Therefore, thus saith the Lord the God of hosts: O my people that dwellest in Sion, be not afraid of the Assyrian: he shall strike thee with his rod, and he shall lift up his staff over thee in the way of Egypt.
text.KJV: 10:24 ¶ Therfore thus saith the Lord God of hostes, O my people that dwellest in Zion, be not afraide of the Assyrian: he shall smite thee with a rod, and shall lift vp his staffe against thee, after the maner of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Therefore thus says the Lord, the God of hosts: My people, who dwell in Zion, do not fear the Assyrian, though he strikes you with a rod, and raises his staff against you as did the Egyptians.

text.NRSV: For in a very little while my fury will come to an end, and my anger will be directed to their destruction.
text.Rotherham: For, yet a very little while, and displeasure shall end, Yea, mine anger, over their destruction.
text.DRB: For yet a little and a very little while, and my indignation shall cease, and my wrath shall be upon their wickedness.
text.KJV: 10:25 For yet a very litle while, and the indignation shall cease, and mine anger in their destruction.
text.NABRE: For just a brief moment more, and my wrath shall be over, and my anger shall be set for their destruction.

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts will wield a whip against them, as when he struck Midian at the rock of Oreb; his staff will be over the sea, and he will lift it as he did in Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh of hosts, will brandish over him, a scourge—Like the smiting of Midian at the rock Oreb,—And his staff being over the sea, He will lift it up in the manner of Egypt;
text.DRB: And the Lord of hosts shall raise up a scourge against him, according to the slaughter of Madian in the rock of Oreb, and his rod over the sea, and he shall lift it up in the way of Egypt.
text.KJV: 10:26 And the Lord of hostes shall stirre vp a scourge for him, according to the slaughter of Midian at the rocke Oreb: and as his rod was vpon the Sea, so shall he lift it vp after the manner of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord of hosts will raise against them a scourge such as struck Midian at the rock of Oreb; and he will raise his staff over the sea as he did in Egypt.

text.NRSV: On that day his burden will be removed from your shoulder, and his yoke will be destroyed from your neck. He has gone up from Rimmon,
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in that day, That his burden shall remove from off thy shoulder, And his yoke from off thy neck,—Yea, the yoke shall be broken because of fatness.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass in that day, that his burden shall be taken away from off thy shoulder, and his yoke from off thy neck, and the yoke shall putrify at the presence of the oil .
text.KJV: 10:27 And it shall come to passe in that day, that his burden shalbe taken away from off thy shoulder, and his yoke from off thy necke, and the yoke shalbe destroyed because of the anointing.
text.NABRE: On that day, His burden shall be taken from your shoulder, and his yoke shattered from your neck. The March of an Enemy Army He has come up from Rimmon,

text.NRSV: he has come to Aiath; he has passed through Migron, at Michmash he stores his baggage;
text.Rotherham: He hath come in unto Ayyath, Hath passed through Migron,—At Michmash, storeth his baggage:
text.DRB: He shall come into Aiath , he shall pass into Magron: at Machmas he shall lay up his carriages.
text.KJV: 10:28 He is come to Aiath, hee is passed to Migron: at Michmash he hath laid vp his cariages.
text.NABRE: he has reached Aiath, passed through Migron, at Michmash he has stored his supplies.

text.NRSV: they have crossed over the pass, at Geba they lodge for the night; Ramah trembles, Gibeah of Saul has fled.
text.Rotherham: They have passed over the pass, Geba is his halting-place,—Terror-stricken is Ramah, Gibeah of Saul, hath fled!
text.DRB: They have passed in haste, Gaba is our lodging: Rama was astonished, Gabaath of Saul fled away.
text.KJV: 10:29 They are gone ouer the passage: they haue taken vp their lodging at Geba, Ramah is afraid, Gebeah of Saul is fled.
text.NABRE: He has crossed the ravine, at Geba he has camped for the night. Ramah trembles, Gibeah of Saul has fled.

text.NRSV: Cry aloud, O daughter Gallim! Listen, O Laishah! Answer her, O Anathoth!
text.Rotherham: Make shrill thy voice, O daughter of Gallim! Hearken, O Laishah, Answer, O Anathoth!
text.DRB: Lift up thy voice, O daughter of Gallim, attend, O Laisa, poor Anathoth.
text.KJV: 10:30 Lift vp thy voice, O daughter of Gallim: cause it to bee heard vnto Laish, O poore Anathoth.
text.NABRE: Cry and shriek, Bath-Gallim! Hearken, Laishah! Answer her, Anathoth!

text.NRSV: Madmenah is in flight, the inhabitants of Gebim flee for safety.
text.Rotherham: Madmenah, hath fled,—The inhabitants of Gebim, have gone into safety:
text.DRB: Medemena is removed: ye inhabitants of Gabim, take courage.
text.KJV: 10:31 Madmenah is remooued, the inhabitants of Gebim gather themselues to flee.
text.NABRE: Madmenah is in flight, the inhabitants of Gebim seek refuge.

text.NRSV: This very day he will halt at Nob, he will shake his fist at the mount of daughter Zion, the hill of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: While yet to-day, in Nob, he tarrieth, He brandisheth his hand toward The mount of the daughter of Zion, The hill of Jerusalem.
text.DRB: It is yet day enough, to remain in Nobe: he shall shake his hand against the mountain of the daughter of Sion, the hill of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 10:32 As yet shall hee remaine at Nob that day: he shall shake his hand against the mount of the daughter of Zion, the hill of Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: Even today he will halt at Nob, he will shake his fist at the mount of daughter Zion, the hill of Jerusalem!

text.NRSV: See, the Lord, the Lord of hosts, will lop the boughs with terrifying power; the lofty in stature will be hewn down, and the exalted will be brought low.
text.Rotherham: Behold! the Lord, Yahweh of hosts, is about to lop off a tree-crown, with a crash,—Then shall, they who are lifted on high, be hewn down, And, the lofty, be laid low;
text.DRB: Behold the sovereign Lord of hosts shall break the earthen vessel with terror, and the tall of stature shall be cut down, and the lofty shall be humbled.
text.KJV: 10:33 Behold, the Lord, the Lord of hostes shall lop the bough with terrour: and the high ones of stature shal be hewen downe, and the haughtie shalbe humbled.
text.NABRE: Now the Lord, the Lord of hosts, is about to lop off the boughs with terrible violence; The tall of stature shall be felled, and the lofty ones shall be brought low;

text.NRSV: He will hack down the thickets of the forest with an ax, and Lebanon with its majestic trees will fall.
text.Rotherham: Then will he fell the thickets of the forest with iron,—And, Lebanon by a majestic one shall fall.
text.DRB: And the thickets of the forest shall be cut down with iron, and Libanus with its high ones shall fall.
text.KJV: 10:34 And he shall cut downe the thickets of the forrests with yron, and Lebanon shall fall by a mightie one.
text.NABRE: He shall hack down the forest thickets with an ax, and Lebanon in its splendor shall fall.

text.NRSV: A shoot shall come out from the stump of Jesse, and a branch shall grow out of his roots.
text.Rotherham: But there shall come forth a shoot from the stock of Jesse,—And, a sprout from his roots, shall bear fruit;
text.DRB: And there shall come forth a rod out of the root of Jesse, and a flower shall rise up out of his root.
text.KJV: 11:1 And there shall come forth a rod out of the stemme of Iesse, and a branch shal grow out of his rootes.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 The Ideal Davidic King But a shoot shall sprout from the stump of Jesse, and from his roots a bud shall blossom.

text.NRSV: The spirit of the Lord shall rest on him, the spirit of wisdom and understanding, the spirit of counsel and might, the spirit of knowledge and the fear of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the spirit of Yahweh shall rest upon him,—The spirit of wisdom and understanding, The spirit of counsel and might, The spirit of knowledge and reverence of Yahweh;
text.DRB: And the spirit of the Lord shall rest upon him: the spirit of wisdom, and of understanding, the spirit of counsel, and of fortitude, the spirit of knowledge, and of godliness.
text.KJV: 11:2 And the Spirit of the Lord shall rest vpon him, the spirit of wisedome and vnderstanding, the spirit of counsell and might, the spirit of knowledge, and of the feare of the Lord:
text.NABRE: The spirit of the Lord shall rest upon him: a spirit of wisdom and of understanding, A spirit of counsel and of strength, a spirit of knowledge and of fear of the Lord,

text.NRSV: His delight shall be in the fear of the Lord. He shall not judge by what his eyes see, or decide by what his ears hear;
text.Rotherham: So will he find fragrance, in the reverence of Yahweh, And not, by the sight of his eyes, will he judge, Nor by the hearing of his ears, will he decide;
text.DRB: And he shall be filled with the spirit of the fear of the Lord. He shall not judge according to the sight of the eyes, nor reprove according to the hearing of the ears.
text.KJV: 11:3 And shal make him of quicke vnderstanding in the feare of the Lord, and he shall not iudge after the sight of his eyes, neither reproue after the hearing of his eares.
text.NABRE: and his delight shall be the fear of the Lord. Not by appearance shall he judge, nor by hearsay shall he decide,

text.NRSV: but with righteousness he shall judge the poor, and decide with equity for the meek of the earth; he shall strike the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips he shall kill the wicked.
text.Rotherham: But he will judge with righteousness! them who are poor, And decide with equity! for the oppressed of the land,—And he will smite the land With the sceptre of his mouth, And with the breath of his lips, will he slay the lawless one;
text.DRB: But he shall judge the poor with justice, and shall reprove with equity for the meek of the earth: and he shall strike the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips he shall slay the wicked.
text.KJV: 11:4 But with righteousnesse shall he iudge the poore, and reprooue with equitie, for the meeke of the earth: and he shall smite the earth with the rodde of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips shall he slay the wicked.
text.NABRE: But he shall judge the poor with justice, and decide fairly for the land’s afflicted. He shall strike the ruthless with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips he shall slay the wicked.

text.NRSV: Righteousness shall be the belt around his waist, and faithfulness the belt around his loins.
text.Rotherham: And righteousness shall be the girdle of his loins,—And faithfulness the girdle of his reins;
text.DRB: And justice shall be the girdle of his loins: and faith the girdle of his reins.
text.KJV: 11:5 And righteousnesse shalbe the girdle of his loines, and faithfulnesse the girdle of his reines.
text.NABRE: Justice shall be the band around his waist, and faithfulness a belt upon his hips.

text.NRSV: The wolf shall live with the lamb, the leopard shall lie down with the kid, the calf and the lion and the fatling together, and a little child shall lead them.
text.Rotherham: And the wolf shall dwell with the lamb, And, the leopard, with the kid, shall lie down,—And the calf and the young lion and the fatling—together, With, a little child, leading them;
text.DRB: The wolf shall dwell with the lamb: and the leopard shall lie down with the kid: the calf and the lion, and the sheep shall abide together, and a little child shall lead them.
text.KJV: 11:6 The wolfe also shall dwell with the lambe, and the leopard shall lie downe with the kid: and the calfe and the yong lion, and the fatling together, and a litle child shall lead them.
text.NABRE: Then the wolf shall be a guest of the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the young goat; The calf and the young lion shall browse together, with a little child to guide them.

text.NRSV: The cow and the bear shall graze, their young shall lie down together; and the lion shall eat straw like the ox.
text.Rotherham: And the cow, with the bear shall find pasture, Together, shall their young ones, lie down,—And, the lion, like the ox, shall eat straw;
text.DRB: The calf and the bear shall feed: their young ones shall rest together: and the lion shall eat straw like the ox.
text.KJV: 11:7 And the cow and the beare shall feed, their yong ones shall lie downe together: and the lyon shall eate straw like the oxe.
text.NABRE: The cow and the bear shall graze, together their young shall lie down; the lion shall eat hay like the ox.

text.NRSV: The nursing child shall play over the hole of the asp, and the weaned child shall put its hand on the adder’s den.
text.Rotherham: And the sucking child shall caress over the hole of the asp, Yea over the viper’s den, hath the weaned child stretched out his hand:
text.DRB: And the sucking child shall play on the hole of the asp: and the weaned child shall thrust his hand into the den of the basilisk.
text.KJV: 11:8 And the sucking childe shall play on the hole of the aspe, and the weaned childe shall put his hand on the cockatrice denne.
text.NABRE: The baby shall play by the viper’s den, and the child lay his hand on the adder’s lair.

text.NRSV: They will not hurt or destroy on all my holy mountain; for the earth will be full of the knowledge of the Lord as the waters cover the sea.
text.Rotherham: They shall not hurt—nor destroy, in all my holy mountain,—For filled is the land with the knowledge of Yahweh, As the waters, to the sea, form a covering.
text.DRB: They shall not hurt, nor shall they kill in all my holy mountain, for the earth is filled with the knowledge of the Lord, as the covering waters of the sea.
text.KJV: 11:9 They shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountaine: for the earth shall bee full of the knowledge of the Lord, as the waters couer the sea.
text.NABRE: They shall not harm or destroy on all my holy mountain; for the earth shall be filled with knowledge of the Lord, as water covers the sea.

text.NRSV: On that day the root of Jesse shall stand as a signal to the peoples; the nations shall inquire of him, and his dwelling shall be glorious.
text.Rotherham: And there shall come to be, in that day, A root of Jesse, which shall be standing as an ensign of peoples, Unto him, shall nations seek,—And, his resting-place, shall be, glorious.
text.DRB: In that day the root of Jesse, who standeth for an ensign of the people, him the Gentiles shall beseech, and his sepulchre shall be glorious.
text.KJV: 11:10 ¶ And in that day there shall bee a roote of Iesse, which shall stand for an ensigne of the people; to it shall the Gentiles seeke, and his rest shall bee glorious.
text.NABRE: Restoration On that day, The root of Jesse, set up as a signal for the peoples— Him the nations will seek out; his dwelling shall be glorious.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord will extend his hand yet a second time to recover the remnant that is left of his people, from Assyria, from Egypt, from Pathros, from Ethiopia, from Elam, from Shinar, from Hamath, and from the coastlands of the sea.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, That My Lord will again a second time set his hand, to possess himself of the remnant of his people,—who shall be left—from Assyria and from Egypt and from Pathros and from Ethiopia and from Elam and from Shinar and from Hamath and from the Coastlands of the sea;
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the Lord shall set his hand the second time to possess the remnant of his people, which shall be left from the Assyrians, and from Egypt, and from Phetros, and from Ethiopia, and from Elam, and from Sennaar, and from Emath, and from the islands of the sea.
text.KJV: 11:11 And it shall come to passe in that day, that the Lord shall set his hande againe the second time, to recouer the remnant of his people which shalbe left, from Assyria, and from Egypt, & from Pathros, and from Cush, and from Elam, and from Shinar, and from Hamath, and from the ylands of the Sea.
text.NABRE: On that day, The Lord shall again take it in hand to reclaim the remnant of his people that is left from Assyria and Egypt, Pathros, Ethiopia, and Elam, Shinar, Hamath, and the isles of the sea.

text.NRSV: He will raise a signal for the nations, and will assemble the outcasts of Israel, and gather the dispersed of Judah from the four corners of the earth.
text.Rotherham: And he will lift up a standard to the nations, And will gather the outcasts of Israel,—And the dispersed of Judah, will he collect, From the four corners of the earth.
text.DRB: And he shall set up a standard unto the nations, and shall assemble the fugitives of Israel, and shall gather together the dispersed of Juda from the four quarters of the earth.
text.KJV: 11:12 And he shall set vp an ensigne for the nations, and shall assemble the outcasts of Israel, and gather together the dispersed of Iudah, from the foure corners of the earth.
text.NABRE: He shall raise a signal to the nations and gather the outcasts of Israel; The dispersed of Judah he shall assemble from the four corners of the earth.

text.NRSV: The jealousy of Ephraim shall depart, the hostility of Judah shall be cut off; Ephraim shall not be jealous of Judah, and Judah shall not be hostile towards Ephraim.
text.Rotherham: And the envy of Ephraim shall be removed, And the vexatious doings of Judah, shall be cut off: Ephraim shall not envy Judah, and Judah, shall not vex Ephraim;
text.DRB: And the envy of Ephraim shall be taken away, and the enemies of Juda shall perish: Ephraim shall not envy Juda, and Juda shall not fight against Ephraim.
text.KJV: 11:13 The enuie also of Ephraim shal depart, and the aduersaries of Iudah shalbe cut off: Ephraim shall not enuie Iudah, and Iudah shall not vexe Ephraim.
text.NABRE: The envy of Ephraim shall pass away, and those hostile to Judah shall be cut off; Ephraim shall not envy Judah, and Judah shall not be hostile to Ephraim;

text.NRSV: But they shall swoop down on the backs of the Philistines in the west, together they shall plunder the people of the east. They shall put forth their hand against Edom and Moab, and the Ammonites shall obey them.
text.Rotherham: But they shall fly on the wing of the Philistines in the west, Together, shall they make prey of the sons of the east,—On Edom and Moab, putting forth their hand, With the sons of Ammon hearkening unto them.
text.DRB: But they shall fly upon the shoulders of the Philistines by the sea, they together shall spoil the children of the east: Edom, and Moab shall be under the rule of their hand, and the children of Ammon shall be obedient.
text.KJV: 11:14 But they shall fly vpon the shoulders of the Philistines toward the West, they shall spoile them of the East together: they shall lay their hand vpon Edom and Moab, and the children of Ammon shall obey them.
text.NABRE: But they shall swoop down on the foothills of the Philistines to the west, together they shall plunder the people of the east; Edom and Moab shall be their possessions, and the Ammonites their subjects.

text.NRSV: And the Lord will utterly destroy the tongue of the sea of Egypt; and will wave his hand over the River with his scorching wind, and will strike it into seven channels that it may be crossed dryshod.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh, will devote to destruction, the gulf of the Egyptian sea, And will brandish his hand against the River [="Euphrates"], in the full force of his spirit,—And will smite it in the seven streams And cause a marching through in sandals,
text.DRB: And the Lord shall lay waste the tongue of the sea of Egypt, and shall lift up his hand over the river in the strength of his spirit: and he shall strike it in the seven streams, so that men may pass through it in their shoes.
text.KJV: 11:15 And the Lord shall vtterly destroy the tongue of the Egyptian sea, and with his mighty wind shall hee shake his hand ouer the riuer, and shall smite it in the seuen streames, and make men goe ouer dry-shod.
text.NABRE: The Lord shall dry up the tongue of the Sea of Egypt, and wave his hand over the Euphrates with his fierce wind, And divide it into seven streamlets, so that it can be crossed in sandals.

text.NRSV: So there shall be a highway from Assyria for the remnant that is left of his people, as there was for Israel when they came up from the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: So shall there be a highway for the remnant of his people who shall be left out of Assyria,—Like as it was to Israel, in the day that he came up out of the land of Egypt!
text.DRB: And there shall be a highway for the remnant of my people, which shall be left from the Assyrians: as there was for Israel in the day that he came up out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: 11:16 And there shalbe an high way for the remnant of his people, which shalbe left from Assyria; like as it was to Israel in the day that hee came vp out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: There shall be a highway for the remnant of his people that is left from Assyria, As there was for Israel when it came up from the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: You will say in that day: I will give thanks to you, O Lord, for though you were angry with me, your anger turned away, and you comforted me.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt say, therefore, in that day, I will praise thee, O Yahweh! Though thou hast been angry with me, Thine anger turneth back. And thou dost comfort me.
text.DRB: And thou shalt say in that day: I will give thanks to thee, O Lord, for thou wast angry with me: thy wrath is turned away, and thou hast comforted me.
text.KJV: 12:1 And in that day thou shalt say, O Lord, I will praise thee: though thou wast angrie with mee, thine anger is turned away, and thou comfortedst me.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 Song of Thanksgiving On that day, you will say: I give you thanks, O Lord; though you have been angry with me, your anger has abated, and you have consoled me.

text.NRSV: Surely God is my salvation; I will trust, and will not be afraid, for the Lord God is my strength and my might; he has become my salvation.
text.Rotherham: Lo, GOD is my salvation! I will trust and not dread,—For, my might and melody, is Yah, Yahweh, And he hath become mine, by salvation.
text.DRB: Behold, God is my saviour, I will deal confidently, and will not fear: O because the Lord is my strength, and my praise, and he is become my salvation.
text.KJV: 12:2 Behold, God is my saluation: I will trust, and not be afraid; for the Lord IEHOVAH is my strength and my song, he also is become my saluation.
text.NABRE: God indeed is my salvation; I am confident and unafraid. For the Lord is my strength and my might, and he has been my salvation.

text.NRSV: With joy you will draw water from the wells of salvation.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye draw water, with rejoicing,—out of the fountains of salvation.
text.DRB: You shall draw waters with joy out of the saviour's fountains:
text.KJV: 12:3 Therefore with ioy shall yee draw water out of the wels of saluation.
text.NABRE: With joy you will draw water from the fountains of salvation,

text.NRSV: And you will say in that day: Give thanks to the Lord, call on his name; make known his deeds among the nations; proclaim that his name is exalted.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall say in that day, Praise Yahweh. Call upon his Name, Make known among the peoples, his doings,—Bring to remembrance that, exalted, is his Name!
text.DRB: And you shall say in that day: Praise ye the Lord, and call upon his name: make his works known among the people: remember that his name is high.
text.KJV: 12:4 And in that day shall yee say; Praise the Lord, call vpon his name, declare his doings among the people, make mention that his name is exalted.
text.NABRE: And you will say on that day: give thanks to the Lord, acclaim his name; Among the nations make known his deeds, proclaim how exalted is his name.

text.NRSV: Sing praises to the Lord, for he has done gloriously; let this be known in all the earth.
text.Rotherham: Praise in song Yahweh, For a splendid thing, hath he done,—Well known, is this in all the earth.
text.DRB: Sing ye to the Lord, for he hath done great things: shew this forth in all the earth.
text.KJV: 12:5 Sing vnto the Lord; for hee hath done excellent things: this is knowen in all the earth.
text.NABRE: Sing praise to the Lord for he has done glorious things; let this be known throughout all the earth.

text.NRSV: Shout aloud and sing for joy, O royal Zion, for great in your midst is the Holy One of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Make shrill thy voice and sing out thou inhabitress of Zion,—That great in the midst of thee, is the Holy One of Israel.
text.DRB: Rejoice, and praise, O thou habitation of Sion: for great is he that is in the midst of thee, the Holy One of Israel.
text.KJV: 12:6 Cry out and shout thou inhabitant of Zion: for great is the holy one of Israel in the midst of thee.
text.NABRE: Shout with exultation, City of Zion, for great in your midst is the Holy One of Israel!

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning Babylon that Isaiah son of Amoz saw.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Babylon,—of which Isaiah, son of Amoz had vision:—
text.DRB: The burden of Babylon , which Isaias the son of Amos saw.
text.KJV: 13:1 The burden of Babylon, which Isaiah the sonne of Amoz did see.
text.NABRE: C. Oracles Against the Foreign Nations Chapter 13 Babylon. An oracle concerning Babylon; a vision of Isaiah, son of Amoz.

text.NRSV: On a bare hill raise a signal, cry aloud to them; wave the hand for them to enter the gates of the nobles.
text.Rotherham: Upon a bare mountain, lift ye up a standard, Raise high the voice to them, Wave the hand, That they may enter the doors of nobles.
text.DRB: Upon the dark mountain lift ye up a banner, exalt the voice, lift up the hand, and let the rulers go into the gates.
text.KJV: 13:2 Lift yee vp a banner vpon the high mountaine, exalt the voice vnto them, shake the hand, that they may goe into the gates of the nobles.
text.NABRE: Upon the bare mountains set up a signal; cry out to them, Beckon for them to enter the gates of the nobles.

text.NRSV: I myself have commanded my consecrated ones, have summoned my warriors, my proudly exulting ones, to execute my anger.
text.Rotherham: I myself, have given charge to my hallowed ones,—Yea I have called My heroes in showing mine anger, My proudly exulting ones.
text.DRB: I have commanded my sanctified ones, and have called my strong ones in my wrath, them that rejoice in my glory.
text.KJV: 13:3 I haue commanded my sanctified ones: I haue also called my mightie ones for mine anger, euen them that reioyce in my highnesse.
text.NABRE: I have commanded my consecrated ones, I have summoned my warriors, eager and bold to carry out my anger.

text.NRSV: Listen, a tumult on the mountains as of a great multitude! Listen, an uproar of kingdoms, of nations gathering together! The Lord of hosts is mustering an army for battle.
text.Rotherham: The noise of a multitude in the mountains, A resemblance of many people,—The noise of a tumult of kingdoms Nations gathered together, Yahweh of hosts, mustering a host for battle!
text.DRB: The noise of a multitude in the mountains, as it were of many people, the noise of the sound of kings, of nations gathered together: the Lord of hosts hath given charge to the troops of war.
text.KJV: 13:4 The noise of a multitude in the mountaines, like as of a great people: a tumultuous noise of the kingdomes of nations gathered together: the Lord of hostes mustereth the hoste of the battell.
text.NABRE: Listen! the rumble on the mountains: that of an immense throng! Listen! the noise of kingdoms, nations assembled! The Lord of hosts is mustering an army for battle.

text.NRSV: They come from a distant land, from the end of the heavens, the Lord and the weapons of his indignation, to destroy the whole earth.
text.Rotherham: They are coming in From a land far away. From the utmost bound of the heavens,—Yahweh—with his weapons of indignation, To destroy the land.
text.DRB: To them that come from a country afar off, from the end of heaven: the Lord and the instruments of his wrath, to destroy the whole land.
text.KJV: 13:5 They come from a farre countrey from the end of heauen, euen the Lord and the weapons of his indignation, to destroy the whole land.
text.NABRE: They come from a far-off country, and from the end of the heavens, The Lord and the instruments of his wrath, to destroy all the land.

text.NRSV: Wail, for the day of the Lord is near; it will come like destruction from the Almighty!
text.Rotherham: Howl ye! for at hand is the day of Yahweh,—As a veritable destruction from the Almighty, shall it come.
text.DRB: Howl ye, for the day of the Lord is near: it shall come as a destruction from the Lord.
text.KJV: 13:6 ¶ Howle yee; for the day of the Lord is at hand; it shall come as a destruction from the Almighty.
text.NABRE: Howl, for the day of the Lord is near; as destruction from the Almighty it comes.

text.NRSV: Therefore all hands will be feeble, and every human heart will melt,
text.Rotherham: For this cause—All hands, shall hang down,—and Every mortal heart, melt.
text.DRB: Therefore shall all hands be faint, and every heart of man shall melt,
text.KJV: 13:7 Therefore shall all hands bee faint, and euery mans heart shall melt.
text.NABRE: Therefore all hands fall helpless, every human heart melts,

text.NRSV: and they will be dismayed. Pangs and agony will seize them; they will be in anguish like a woman in labor. They will look aghast at one another; their faces will be aflame.
text.Rotherham: And they shall be in distress—Writhings and pains, shall lay hold, As a woman in childbirth, shall they be in pangs,—Everyone, at his neighbour, shall look in amazement, Faces of flames, their faces!
text.DRB: And shall be broken. Gripings and pains shall take hold of them, they shall be in pain as a woman in labour. Every one shall be amazed at his neighbour, their countenances shall be as faces burnt.
text.KJV: 13:8 And they shalbe afraid: pangs and sorrowes shall take hold of them, they shalbe in paine as a woman that trauelleth: they shalbe amazed one at another, their faces shalbe as flames.
text.NABRE: and they are terrified, Pangs and sorrows take hold of them, like a woman in labor they writhe; They look aghast at each other, their faces aflame.

text.NRSV: See, the day of the Lord comes, cruel, with wrath and fierce anger, to make the earth a desolation, and to destroy its sinners from it.
text.Rotherham: Lo! the day of Yahweh, coming in, Fierce and overflowing, and burning with anger,—To devote the earth to desolation, And her sinners, will he destroy out of it.
text.DRB: Behold, the day of the Lord shall come, a cruel day, and full of indignation, and of wrath, and fury, to lay the land desolate, and to destroy the sinners thereof out of it.
text.KJV: 13:9 Behold, the day of the Lord commeth, cruell both with wrath and fierce anger, to lay the land desolate; and he shall destroy the sinners thereof out of it.
text.NABRE: Indeed, the day of the Lord comes, cruel, with wrath and burning anger; To lay waste the land and destroy the sinners within it!

text.NRSV: For the stars of the heavens and their constellations will not give their light; the sun will be dark at its rising, and the moon will not shed its light.
text.Rotherham: For the stars of the heavens and their constellations, shall not flash forth their light,—Obscured shall be the sun in his going forth, And the moon, shall not shed her light.
text.DRB: For the stars of heaven, and their brightness shall not display their light: the sun shall be darkened in his rising, and the moon shall not shine with her light.
text.KJV: 13:10 For the starres of heauen, and the constellations thereof shall not giue their light: the sunne shalbe darkened in his going forth, and the moone shall not cause her light to shine.
text.NABRE: The stars of the heavens and their constellations will send forth no light; The sun will be dark at its rising, and the moon will not give its light.

text.NRSV: I will punish the world for its evil, and the wicked for their iniquity; I will put an end to the pride of the arrogant, and lay low the insolence of tyrants.
text.Rotherham: And I will visit, upon the inhabited earth, calamity!, And upon the lawless, their punishment, And will quiet the arrogance of the proud, And the loftiness of tyrants, will I lay low.
text.DRB: And I will visit the evils of the world, and against the wicked for their iniquity: and I will make the pride of infidels to cease, and will bring down the arrogancy of the mighty.
text.KJV: 13:11 And I will punish the world for their euill, and the wicked for their iniquitie; and I will cause the arrogancie of the proud to cease, and will lay low the hautinesse of the terrible.
text.NABRE: Thus I will punish the world for its evil and the wicked for their guilt. I will put an end to the pride of the arrogant, the insolence of tyrants I will humble.

text.NRSV: I will make mortals scarcer than pure gold, and humans than the gold of Ophir.
text.Rotherham: I will cause, a man, to be more precious than fine gold,—Even a son of earth than, the finest gold of Ophir.
text.DRB: A man shall be more precious than gold, yea a man than the finest of gold.
text.KJV: 13:12 I will make a man more pretious then fine gold; euen a man then the golden wedge of Ophir.
text.NABRE: I will make mortals more rare than pure gold, human beings, than the gold of Ophir.

text.NRSV: Therefore I will make the heavens tremble, and the earth will be shaken out of its place, at the wrath of the Lord of hosts in the day of his fierce anger.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, the heavens, will I disturb, And, the earth, shall tremble out of her place,—In the wrath of Yahweh of hosts, And in the day of the glow of his anger.
text.DRB: For this I will trouble the heaven: and the earth shall be moved out of her place, for the indignation of the Lord of hosts, and for the day of his fierce wrath.
text.KJV: 13:13 Therefore I will shake the heauens, and the earth shall remoue out of her place in the wrath of the Lord of hostes, and in the day of his fierce anger.
text.NABRE: For this I will make the heavens tremble and the earth shall be shaken from its place, At the wrath of the Lord of hosts on the day of his burning anger.

text.NRSV: Like a hunted gazelle, or like sheep with no one to gather them, all will face to their own people, and all will flee to their own land.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be—Like a gazelle that is chased, And like a sheep with none to lift it up,—Each, to his own people, will they turn, And, each, to his own land, will they flee:
text.DRB: And they shall be as a doe fleeing away, and as a sheep: and there shall be none to gather them together: every man shall turn to his own people, and every one shall flee to his own land.
text.KJV: 13:14 And it shalbe as the chased Roe, and as a sheepe that no man taketh vp: they shall euery man turne to his owne people, and flee euery one into his owne land.
text.NABRE: Like a hunted gazelle, or a flock that no one gathers, They shall turn each to their own people and flee each to their own land.

text.NRSV: Whoever is found will be thrust through, and whoever is caught will fall by the sword.
text.Rotherham: Every one found, shall be thrust through,—And, every one taken, shall fall by the sword;
text.DRB: Every one that shall be found, shall be slain: and every one that shall come to their aid, shall fall by the sword.
text.KJV: 13:15 Euery one that is found shall be thrust through: and euery one that is ioyned vnto them, shall fall by the sword.
text.NABRE: Everyone who is taken shall be run through; and everyone who is caught shall fall by the sword.

text.NRSV: Their infants will be dashed to pieces before their eyes; their houses will be plundered, and their wives ravished.
text.Rotherham: And, their infants, shall be dashed to the ground, before their eyes,—Plundered shall be, their houses, and their wives ravished.
text.DRB: Their infants shall be dashed in pieces before their eyes: their houses shall be pillaged, and their wives shall be ravished.
text.KJV: 13:16 Their children also shalbe dashed to pieces before their eyes, their houses shalbe spoiled, & their wiues rauished.
text.NABRE: Their infants shall be dashed to pieces in their sight; their houses shall be plundered and their wives ravished.

text.NRSV: See, I am stirring up the Medes against them, who have no regard for silver and do not delight in gold.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! stirring up against them the Medes,—Who of silver, shall take no account, And as for gold, they shall not delight in it;
text.DRB: Behold I will stir up the Medes against them, who shall not seek silver, nor desire gold:
text.KJV: 13:17 Beholde, I will stirre vp the Medes against them, which shall not regard siluer, and as for gold, they shall not delight in it.
text.NABRE: I am stirring up against them the Medes, who think nothing of silver and take no delight in gold.

text.NRSV: Their bows will slaughter the young men; they will have no mercy on the fruit of the womb; their eyes will not pity children.
text.Rotherham: And, bows, shall dash the young to pieces,—And on the fruit of the womb, will they have no pity, Over children, will their eye throw no shield.
text.DRB: But with their arrows they shall kill the children, and shall have no pity upon the sucklings of the womb, and their eye shall not spare their sons.
text.KJV: 13:18 Their bowes also shall dash the yong men to pieces, and they shall haue no pitie on the fruit of the wombe; their eye shall not spare children.
text.NABRE: With their bows they shall shatter the young men, And the fruit of the womb they shall not spare, nor shall their eye take pity on children.

text.NRSV: And Babylon, the glory of kingdoms, the splendor and pride of the Chaldeans, will be like Sodom and Gomorrah when God overthrew them.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall Babylon—The most lovely of kingdoms, The majestic beauty of the Chaldees—Become as in the divine overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah.
text.DRB: And that Babylon, glorious among kingdoms, the famous pride of the Chaldeans, shall be even as the Lord destroyed Sodom and Gomorrha.
text.KJV: 13:19 ¶ And Babylon the glory of kingdomes, the beautie of the Chaldees excellencie, shall be as when God ouerthrew Sodom and Gomorrah.
text.NABRE: And Babylon, the jewel of kingdoms, the glory and pride of the Chaldeans, Shall become like Sodom and Gomorrah, overthrown by God.

text.NRSV: It will never be inhabited or lived in for all generations; untilled by Arabs, shepherds will not make their flocks lie down there.
text.Rotherham: It shall not be dwelt in for ever, Neither shall it be inhabited from generation to generation,—Neither shall encamp there, an Arab, Nor, shepherds, fold their flocks there.
text.DRB: It shall no more be inhabited for ever, and it shall not be founded unto generation and generation: neither shall the Arabian pitch his tents there, nor shall shepherds rest there.
text.KJV: 13:20 It shall neuer be inhabited, neither shall it be dwelt in from generation to generation: neither shall the Arabian pitch tent there, neither shal the shepheards make their fold there.
text.NABRE: It shall never be inhabited, nor dwelt in, from age to age; Arabians shall not pitch their tents there, nor shepherds rest their flocks there.

text.NRSV: But wild animals will lie down there, and its houses will be full of howling creatures; there ostriches will live, and there goat-demons will dance.
text.Rotherham: Then shall lie down there, wild beasts, And filled shall be their houses with owls,—Then shall inhabit there, the ostrich, And shaggy creatures, shall dance there.
text.DRB: But wild beasts shall rest there, and their houses shall be filled with serpents, and ostriches shall dwell there, and the hairy ones shall dance there:
text.KJV: 13:21 But wilde beastes of the desert shall lye there, and their houses shalbe full of dolefull creatures, and owles shall dwell there, and Satyres shall daunce there.
text.NABRE: But desert demons shall rest there and owls shall fill the houses; There ostriches shall dwell, and satyrs shall dance.

text.NRSV: Hyenas will cry in its towers, and jackals in the pleasant palaces; its time is close at hand, and its days will not be prolonged.
text.Rotherham: And jackals shall answer, in their citadels, And wild dogs, in their voluptuous palaces,—And near to come is her time, And, her days, shall not be delayed.
text.DRB: And owls shall answer one another there, in the houses thereof, and sirens in the temples of pleasure.
text.KJV: 13:22 And the wilde beastes of the Ilands shal cry in their desolate houses, and dragons in their pleasant palaces: and her time is neere to come, and her dayes shall not be prolonged.
text.NABRE: Wild dogs shall dwell in its castles, and jackals in its luxurious palaces. Its time is near at hand and its days shall not be prolonged.

text.NRSV: But the Lord will have compassion on Jacob and will again choose Israel, and will settle them in their own land; and aliens will join them and attach themselves to the house of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh will have compassion on Jacob, And will yet choose Israel, And will give them rest upon their own soil,—And the sojourner, shall join himself, unto them, And they shall attach themselves unto the house of Jacob;
text.DRB: Her time is near at hand, and her days shall not be prolonged. For the Lord will have mercy on Jacob, and will yet choose out of Israel, and will make them rest upon their own ground: and the stranger shall be joined with them, and shall adhere to the house of Jacob.
text.KJV: 14:1 For the Lord wil haue mercie on Iacob, and wil yet choose Israel, and set them in their owne land: and the strangers shalbe ioyned with them, and they shal cleaue to the house of Iacob.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 Restoration of Israel. But the Lord will take pity on Jacob and again choose Israel, and will settle them on their own land; foreigners will join them and attach themselves to the house of Jacob.

text.NRSV: And the nations will take them and bring them to their place, and the house of Israel will possess them as male and female slaves in the Lord’s land; they will take captive those who were their captors, and rule over those who oppressed them.
text.Rotherham: And peoples shall take them, and bring them into their own place, And the house of Israel shall possess themselves of them upon the soil of Yahweh for servants and for handmaids,—Thus shall they be taking captive their captors, And shall tread down their oppressors.
text.DRB: And the people shall take them, and bring them into their place: and the house of Israel shall possess them in the land of the Lord for servants and handmaids: and they shall make them captives that had taken them, and shall subdue their oppressors.
text.KJV: 14:2 And the people shall take them, and bring them to their place: and the house of Israel shall possesse them in the land of the Lord, for seruants and handmaides: and they shall take them captiues, whose captiues they were, and they shall rule ouer their oppressours.
text.NABRE: The nations will take them and bring them to their place, and the house of Israel will possess them as male and female slaves on the Lord’s land; they will take captive their captors and rule over their oppressors.

text.NRSV: When the Lord has given you rest from your pain and turmoil and the hard service with which you were made to serve,
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in the day when Yahweh shall give thee rest from thy toil, and from thy disquiet,—and from the hard service which had been laid upon thee,
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass in that day, that when God shall give thee rest from thy labour, and from thy vexation, and from the hard bondage, wherewith thou didst serve before,
text.KJV: 14:3 And it shall come to passe in the day that the Lord shal giue thee rest from thy sorrow, and from thy feare, and from the hard bondage wherein thou wast made to serue,
text.NABRE: Downfall of the King of Babylon. On the day when the Lord gives you rest from your sorrow and turmoil, from the hard service with which you served,

text.NRSV: you will take up this taunt against the king of Babylon: How the oppressor has ceased! How his insolence has ceased!
text.Rotherham: that thou shalt take up this taunt over the king of Babylon, and shalt say:—How hath ceased the oppressor! Ceased the exactress!
text.DRB: Thou shalt take up this parable against the king of Babylon, and shalt say: How is the oppressor come to nothing, the tribute hath ceased?
text.KJV: 14:4 ¶ That thou shalt take vp this prouerbe against the king of Babylon, and say; How hath the oppressour ceased? the golden citie ceased?
text.NABRE: you will take up this taunt-song against the king of Babylon: How the oppressor has come to an end! how the turmoil has ended!

text.NRSV: The Lord has broken the staff of the wicked, the scepter of rulers,
text.Rotherham: Broke hath Yahweh the staff of the lawless,—The sceptre of despots;
text.DRB: The Lord hath broken the staff of the wicked, the rod of the rulers,
text.KJV: 14:5 The Lord hath broken the staffe of the wicked, and the scepter of the rulers.
text.NABRE: The Lord has broken the rod of the wicked, the staff of the tyrants

text.NRSV: that struck down the peoples in wrath with unceasing blows, that ruled the nations in anger with unrelenting persecution.
text.Rotherham: Smiting peoples in passion With stroke unremitting,—Trampling, in anger, on nations, Persecution unhindered.
text.DRB: That struck the people in wrath with an incurable wound, that brought nations under in fury, that persecuted in a cruel manner.
text.KJV: 14:6 He who smote the people in wrath with a continuall stroke; hee that ruled the nations in anger, is persecuted and none hindereth.
text.NABRE: That struck the peoples in wrath with relentless blows; That ruled the nations in anger, with boundless persecution.

text.NRSV: The whole earth is at rest and quiet; they break forth into singing.
text.Rotherham: Quiet, at rest, the whole earth,—Men have burst into shouting!
text.DRB: The whole earth is quiet and still, it is glad and hath rejoiced.
text.KJV: 14:7 The whole earth is at rest and is quiet: they breake foorth into singing.
text.NABRE: The whole earth rests peacefully, song breaks forth;

text.NRSV: The cypresses exult over you, the cedars of Lebanon, saying, “Since you were laid low, no logger comes up against us.”
text.Rotherham: Yea the pine-trees, make mirth at thee, Cedars of Lebanon, Since thou wast laid low, No feller hath come up against us!
text.DRB: The fir trees also have rejoiced over thee, and the cedars of Libanus, saying: Since thou hast slept, there hath none come up to cut us down.
text.KJV: 14:8 Yea the firre trees reioyce at thee, and the cedars of Lebanon, saying, Since thou art layd downe, no feller is come vp against vs.
text.NABRE: The very cypresses rejoice over you, the cedars of Lebanon: “Now that you are laid to rest, no one comes to cut us down.”

text.NRSV: Sheol beneath is stirred up to meet you when you come; it rouses the shades to greet you, all who were leaders of the earth; it raises from their thrones all who were kings of the nations.
text.Rotherham: Hades beneath, is excited about thee To meet thine arrival,—Rousing up, for thee Shades, All the he-goats of earth! Maketh rise from their thrones, All the kings of the nations.
text.DRB: Hell below was in an uproar to meet thee at thy coming, it stirred up the giants for thee. All the princes of the earth are risen up from their thrones, all the princes of nations.
text.KJV: 14:9 Hell from beneath is mooued for thee to meet thee at thy comming: it stirreth vp the dead for thee, euen all the chiefe ones of the earth; it hath raised vp from their thrones, all the kings of the nations.
text.NABRE: Below, Sheol is all astir preparing for your coming; Awakening the shades to greet you, all the leaders of the earth; Making all the kings of the nations rise from their thrones.

text.NRSV: All of them will speak and say to you: “You too have become as weak as we! You have become like us!”
text.Rotherham: All of them, answer, and say to thee,—Thou too, made strengthless, as we! Unto us, art thou like!
text.DRB: All shall answer, and say to thee: Thou also art wounded as well as we, thou art become like unto us.
text.KJV: 14:10 All they shall speake and say vnto thee; Art thou also become weake as we? art thou become like vnto vs?
text.NABRE: All of them speak out and say to you, “You too have become weak like us, you are just like us!

text.NRSV: Your pomp is brought down to Sheol, and the sound of your harps; maggots are the bed beneath you, and worms are your covering.
text.Rotherham: Brought down to Hades, thy pride. The hum of thy harps, Beneath thee, is spread out corruption, And, thy coverlet—worms!
text.DRB: Thy pride is brought down to hell, thy carcass is fallen down: under thee shall the moth be strewed, and worms shall be thy covering.
text.KJV: 14:11 Thy pompe is brought downe to the graue, and the noyse of thy violes: the worme is spread vnder thee, and the wormes couer thee.
text.NABRE: Down to Sheol your pomp is brought, the sound of your harps. Maggots are the couch beneath you, worms your blanket.”

text.NRSV: How you are fallen from heaven, O Day Star, son of Dawn! How you are cut down to the ground, you who laid the nations low!
text.Rotherham: How hast thou fallen from heaven, O Shining One—Son of the Dawn! Hewn down to the earth, O crusher of nations!
text.DRB: How art thou fallen from heaven, O Lucifer , who didst rise in the morning? how art thou fallen to the earth, that didst wound the nations?
text.KJV: 14:12 How art thou fallen from heauen, O Lucifer, sonne of the morning? how art thou cut downe to the ground, which didst weaken the nations?
text.NABRE: How you have fallen from the heavens, O Morning Star, son of the dawn! How you have been cut down to the earth, you who conquered nations!

text.NRSV: You said in your heart, “I will ascend to heaven; I will raise my throne above the stars of God; I will sit on the mount of assembly on the heights of Zaphon;
text.Rotherham: Yet, thou, didst say in thy heart—The heavens, will I ascend, Above the stars of GOD, will I lift up my throne,—That I may sit in the Mount of Assembly In the Recesses of the North:
text.DRB: And thou saidst in thy heart: I will ascend into heaven, I will exalt my throne above the stars of God, I will sit in the mountain of the covenant, in the sides of the north.
text.KJV: 14:13 For thou hast said in thine heart; I wil ascend into heauen, I wil exalt my throne aboue the starres of God: I wil sit also vpon the mount of the congregation, in the sides of the North.
text.NABRE: In your heart you said: “I will scale the heavens; Above the stars of God I will set up my throne; I will take my seat on the Mount of Assembly, on the heights of Zaphon.

text.NRSV: I will ascend above the heights of the clouds, I will make myself like the Most High.”
text.Rotherham: I will mount on the hills of the clouds, I will match the Most High!
text.DRB: I will ascend above the height of the clouds, I will be like the most High.
text.KJV: 14:14 I wil ascend aboue the heights of the cloudes, I wil bee like the most High.
text.NABRE: I will ascend above the tops of the clouds; I will be like the Most High!”

text.NRSV: But you are brought down to Sheol, to the depths of the Pit.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, to Hades, shalt thou be brought down,—To the Recesses of the Pit!
text.DRB: But yet thou shalt be brought down to hell, into the depth of the pit.
text.KJV: 14:15 Yet thou shalt be brought downe to hel, to the sides of the pit.
text.NABRE: No! Down to Sheol you will be brought to the depths of the pit!

text.NRSV: Those who see you will stare at you, and ponder over you: “Is this the man who made the earth tremble, who shook kingdoms,
text.Rotherham: They who see thee, upon thee, will gaze, Upon thee, will thoughtfully muse,—Is this the man who startled the earth? Who terrified kingdoms?
text.DRB: They that shall see thee, shall turn toward thee, and behold thee. Is this the man that troubled the earth, that shook kingdoms,
text.KJV: 14:16 They that see thee shal narrowly looke vpon thee, and consider thee, saying; Is this the man that made the earth to tremble, that did shake kingdomes?
text.NABRE: When they see you they will stare, pondering over you: “Is this the man who made the earth tremble, who shook kingdoms?

text.NRSV: who made the world like a desert and overthrew its cities, who would not let his prisoners go home?”
text.Rotherham: Who made the world like a desert? And its cities, brake down? Its prisoners, he loosed not, Each one to his home?
text.DRB: That made the world a wilderness, and destroyed the cities thereof, that opened not the prison to his prisoners?
text.KJV: 14:17 That made the world as a wildernesse, and destroyed the cities thereof that opened not the house of his prisoners?
text.NABRE: Who made the world a wilderness, razed its cities, and gave captives no release?”

text.NRSV: All the kings of the nations lie in glory, each in his own tomb;
text.Rotherham: All the kings of the nations—they all, are lying in state Each one in his crypt;
text.DRB: All the kings of the nations have all of them slept in glory, every one in his own house.
text.KJV: 14:18 All the kings of the nations, euen all of them lie in glory, euery one in his owne house.
text.NABRE: All the kings of the nations lie in glory, each in his own tomb;

text.NRSV: but you are cast out, away from your grave, like loathsome carrion, clothed with the slain, those pierced by the sword, who go down to the stones of the Pit, like a corpse trampled underfoot.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, art flung out from thy grave, Like a scion detested, Beshrouded with slain, the pierced of the sword, Like a carcase trod underfoot:
text.DRB: But thou art cast out of thy grave, as an unprofitable branch defiled, and wrapped up among them that were slain by the sword, and art gone down to the bottom of the pit, as a rotten carcass.
text.KJV: 14:19 But thou art cast out of thy graue, like an abominable branch: and as the raiment of those that are slaine, thrust through with a sword, that goe downe to the stones of the pit, as a carkeis troden vnder feete.
text.NABRE: But you are cast forth without burial, like loathsome carrion, Covered with the slain, with those struck by the sword, a trampled corpse, Going down to the very stones of the pit.

text.NRSV: You will not be joined with them in burial, because you have destroyed your land, you have killed your people. May the offspring of evildoers nevermore be named!
text.Rotherham: As for them who go down to the stones of the Pit, Thou shalt not be united with them in burial; For thy land, thou didst ruin Thy people, didst slay,—Unnamed to times age-abiding, Be the seed of the wicked!
text.DRB: Thou shalt not keep company with them, even in burial: for thou hast destroyed thy land, thou hast slain thy people: the seed of the wicked shall not be named for ever.
text.KJV: 14:20 Thou shalt not be ioyned with them in buriall, because thou hast destroyed thy land, and slaine thy people: the seede of euill doers shall neuer be renowmed.
text.NABRE: You will never be together with them in the grave, For you have ruined your land, you have slain your people! Let him never be named, that offshoot of evil!

text.NRSV: Prepare slaughter for his sons because of the guilt of their father. Do not let them rise and possess the earth, and fill the face of the world with cities.
text.Rotherham: Prepare—for his sons—shambles. For the iniquity of their fathers,—They shall not rise nor possess the earth, Nor fill the face of the world with cities.
text.DRB: Prepare his children for slaughter for the iniquity of their fathers: they shall not rise up, nor inherit the land, nor fill the face of the world with cities.
text.KJV: 14:21 Prepare slaughter for his children for the iniquitie of their fathers, that they doe not rise nor possesse the land, nor fill the face of the world with cities.
text.NABRE: Make ready to slaughter his sons for the guilt of their fathers; Lest they rise and possess the earth, and fill the breadth of the world with cities.

text.NRSV: I will rise up against them, says the Lord of hosts, and will cut off from Babylon name and remnant, offspring and posterity, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: I will rise. Then. against them, Declareth Yahweh of hosts,—And will cut off from Babylon Record and remnant And scion and seed, Declareth Yahweh.
text.DRB: And I will rise up against them, saith the Lord of hosts: and I will destroy the name of Babylon, and the remains, and the bud, and the offspring, saith the Lord.
text.KJV: 14:22 For I will rise vp against them, sayth the Lord of hostes, and cut off from Babylon the name, and remnant, and sonne and nephew, sayth the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will rise up against them, says the Lord of hosts, and cut off from Babylon name and remnant, progeny and offspring, says the Lord.

text.NRSV: And I will make it a possession of the hedgehog, and pools of water, and I will sweep it with the broom of destruction, says the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: And will make her a possession for the bittern, And marshes of water,—And will sweep it with the besom of destruction, Declareth Yahweh of hosts.
text.DRB: And I will make it a possession for the ericius and pools of waters, and I will sweep it and wear it out with a besom, saith the Lord of hosts.
text.KJV: 14:23 I will also make it a possession for the Bitterne, and pooles of water: and I will sweepe it with the besome of destruction, sayth the Lord of hostes.
text.NABRE: I will make it a haunt of hoot owls and a marshland; I will sweep it with the broom of destruction, oracle of the Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts has sworn: As I have designed, so shall it be; and as I have planned, so shall it stand:
text.Rotherham: Yahweh of hosts, hath sworn, saying,—Surely as I intended, so, hath it come to pass, And as I purposed, the same, shall stand:—
text.DRB: The Lord of hosts hath sworn, saying: Surely as I have thought, so shall it be: and as I have purposed,
text.KJV: 14:24 ¶ The Lord of hostes hath sworne, saying; Surely as I haue thought, so shall it come to passe; and as I haue purposed, so shall it stand:
text.NABRE: God’s Plan for Assyria The Lord of hosts has sworn: As I have resolved, so shall it be; As I have planned, so shall it stand:

text.NRSV: I will break the Assyrian in my land, and on my mountains trample him underfoot; his yoke shall be removed from them, and his burden from their shoulders.
text.Rotherham: That I will break in pieces the Assyrian in my land, Yea upon my mountains, will I tread him underfoot,—So shall be removed from off them his yoke, And his burden, from off their shoulder shall be removed.
text.DRB: So shall it fall out: That I will destroy the Assyrian in my land, and upon my mountains tread him under foot: and his yoke shall be taken away from them, and his burden shall be taken off their shoulder.
text.KJV: 14:25 That I will breake the Assyrian in my land, and vpon my mountaines tread him vnder foote: then shall his yoke depart from off them, and his burden depart from off their shoulders.
text.NABRE: To break the Assyrian in my land and trample him on my mountains; Then his yoke shall be removed from them, and his burden from their shoulder.

text.NRSV: This is the plan that is planned concerning the whole earth; and this is the hand that is stretched out over all the nations.
text.Rotherham: This, is the purpose that is purposed upon all the earth,—And, this, the hand outstretched over all the nations;
text.DRB: This is the counsel, that I have purposed upon all the earth, and this is the hand that is stretched out upon all nations.
text.KJV: 14:26 This is the purpose, that is purposed vpon the whole earth: and this is the hand that is stretched out vpon all the nations.
text.NABRE: This is the plan proposed for the whole earth, and this the hand outstretched over all the nations.

text.NRSV: For the Lord of hosts has planned, and who will annul it? His hand is stretched out, and who will turn it back?
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh of hosts, hath purposed,—And who shall frustrate? And, his, is the hand outstretched, And who shall turn it back?
text.DRB: For the Lord of hosts hath decreed, and who can disannul it? and his hand is stretched out: and who shall turn it away?
text.KJV: 14:27 For the Lord of hostes hath purposed, and who shall disanull it? and his hand is stretched out, and who shall turne it backe?
text.NABRE: The Lord of hosts has planned; who can thwart him? His hand is stretched out; who can turn it back? Philistia.

text.NRSV: In the year that King Ahaz died this oracle came:
text.Rotherham: In the year that King Ahaz died, came this oracle:—
text.DRB: In the year that king Achaz died, was this burden:
text.KJV: 14:28 In the yeere that king Ahaz died, was this burden.
text.NABRE: In the year that King Ahaz died, there came this oracle:

text.NRSV: Do not rejoice, all you Philistines, that the rod that struck you is broken, for from the snake’s root will come forth an adder, and its fruit will be a flying fiery serpent.
text.Rotherham: Do not rejoice, Philistia, any of thee, In that the rod of him that smote thee, is broken,—For, out of the root of the serpent, shall come forth, a viper, And his fruit be a fiery dragon that flieth.
text.DRB: Rejoice not thou, whole Philistia, that the rod of him that struck thee is broken in pieces: for out of the root of the serpent shall come forth a basilisk, and his seed shall swallow the bird.
text.KJV: 14:29 ¶ Reioyce not thou whole Palestina, because the rod of him that smote thee is broken: for out of the serpents roote shall come foorth a cockatrice, and his fruite shall be a fierie flying serpent.
text.NABRE: Do not rejoice, Philistia, not one of you, that the rod which struck you is broken; For out of the serpent’s root shall come an adder, its offspring shall be a flying saraph.

text.NRSV: The firstborn of the poor will graze, and the needy lie down in safety; but I will make your root die of famine, and your remnant I will kill.
text.Rotherham: Then shall feed the firstborn of the poor, And the needy, in confidence lie down,—And I will kill with famine thy root, And thy residue, shall be slain.
text.DRB: And the firstborn of the poor shall be fed, and the poor shall rest with confidence: and I will make thy root perish with famine, and I will kill thy remnant.
text.KJV: 14:30 And the first borne of the poore shall feed, and the needy shall lie downe in safetie: and I will kill thy root with famine, and he shall slay thy remnant.
text.NABRE: In my pastures the poor shall graze, and the needy lie down in safety; But I will kill your root with famine that shall slay even your remnant.

text.NRSV: Wail, O gate; cry, O city; melt in fear, O Philistia, all of you! For smoke comes out of the north, and there is no straggler in its ranks.
text.Rotherham: Howl—O gate, Make outcry—O city, Dispersed art thou Philistia, all of thee,—For out of the north, a smoke cometh in, With no straggler in his ranks.
text.DRB: Howl, O gate; cry, O city: all Philistia is thrown down: for a smoke shall come from the north, and there is none that shall escape his troop.
text.KJV: 14:31 Howle, O gate, crie, O citie, thou whole Palestina art dissolued, for there shal come from the North a smoke, and none shall bee alone in his appointed times.
text.NABRE: Howl, O gate; cry out, O city! Philistia, all of you melts away! For there comes a smoke from the north, without a straggler in its ranks.

text.NRSV: What will one answer the messengers of the nation? “The Lord has founded Zion, and the needy among his people will find refuge in her.”
text.Rotherham: What, then, shall one answer the messengers of a nation? That Yahweh, hath founded Zion, And in her, shall seek refuge the oppressed of his people.
text.DRB: And what shall be answered to the messengers of the nations? That the Lord hath founded Sion, and the poor of his people shall hope in him.
text.KJV: 14:32 What shall one then answere the messengers of the nation? that the Lord hath founded Zion, and the poore of his people shall trust in it.
text.NABRE: What will one answer the messengers of the nations? “The Lord has established Zion, and in her the afflicted of his people find refuge.”

text.NRSV: An oracle concerning Moab. Because Ar of Moab is laid waste in a night, Moab is undone; because Kir of Moab is laid waste in a night, Moab is undone.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Moab: Because, in a night, was laid waste Ar of Moab—destroyed! Because, in a night, was laid waste Kir of Moab—destroyed,
text.DRB: The burden of Moab. Because in the night Ar of Moab is laid waste, it is silent: because the wall of Moab is destroyed in the night, it is silent.
text.KJV: 15:1 The burden of Moab: because in the night Ar of Moab is laide waste and brought to silence; because in the night Kir of Moab is laide waste, and brought to silence:
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 Moab Oracle on Moab: Laid waste in a night, Ar of Moab is destroyed; Laid waste in a night, Kir of Moab is destroyed.

text.NRSV: Dibon has gone up to the temple, to the high places to weep; over Nebo and over Medeba Moab wails. On every head is baldness, every beard is shorn;
text.Rotherham: He hath gone up to Bayith and Dibon, to the high places, to weep,—On Nebo and on Medeba, Moab is howling, On all their heads, a baldness, Every beard, clipped.
text.DRB: The house is gone up, and Dibon to the high places to mourn over Nabo, and over Medaba, Moab hath howled: on all their heads shall be baldness, and every beard shall be shaven.
text.KJV: 15:2 Hee is gone vp to Baijth, and to Dibon, the high places, to weepe: Moab shall howle ouer Nebo, and ouer Medeba, on all their heads shalbe baldnesse, and euery beard cut off.
text.NABRE: Daughter Dibon has gone up to the high places to weep; Over Nebo and over Medeba Moab is wailing. Every head is shaved, every beard sheared off.

text.NRSV: in the streets they bind on sackcloth; on the housetops and in the squares everyone wails and melts in tears.
text.Rotherham: In their streets, have they girded them with sackcloth,—On their housetops, and in their broadways, every one is howling—melting in tears;
text.DRB: In their streets they are girded with sackcloth: on the tops of their houses, and in their streets all shall howl and come down weeping.
text.KJV: 15:3 In their streetes they shall girde themselues with sackecloth: on the toppes of their houses, and in their streetes euery one shall howle, weeping abundantly.
text.NABRE: In the streets they wear sackcloth, and on the rooftops; In the squares everyone wails, streaming with tears.

text.NRSV: Heshbon and Elealeh cry out as far as Jahaz their voice is heard; therefore the loins of Moab quiver; his soul trembles.
text.Rotherham: And Heshbon, hath made outcry, and Elealeh, Unto Jahaz, hath been heard their voice,—For this cause, do the armed men of Moab roar, Every man’s soul, quivereth to him.
text.DRB: Hesebon shall cry, and Eleale, their voice is heard even to Jasa. For this shall the well appointed men of Moab howl, his soul shall howl to itself.
text.KJV: 15:4 And Heshbon shall cry, and Elealeh: their voice shalbe heard euen vnto Iahaz: therefore the armed souldiers of Moab shall crie out, his life shall be grieuous vnto him.
text.NABRE: Heshbon and Elealeh cry out, they are heard as far as Jahaz. At this the loins of Moab tremble, his soul quivers within him;

text.NRSV: My heart cries out for Moab; his fugitives flee to Zoar, to Eglath-shelishiyah. For at the ascent of Luhith they go up weeping; on the road to Horonaim they raise a cry of destruction;
text.Rotherham: Mine own heart, for Moab continueth to make outcry, Her fugitive, as far as Zoar, is like a heifer of three years; For the accent of Luhith, with weeping, they ascend, For by the way of Horonaim—an outcry of destruction, they excite;
text.DRB: My heart shall cry to Moab, the bars thereof shall flee unto Segor a heifer of three years old: for by the ascent of Luith they shall go up weeping: and in the way of Oronaim they shall lift up a cry of destruction.
text.KJV: 15:5 My heart shall cry out for Moab; his fugitiues shall flee vnto Zoar, an heifer of three yeeres olde: for by the mounting vp of Luhith with weeping shall they goe it vp: for in the way of Horonaim, they shall raise vp a crie of destruction.
text.NABRE: My heart cries out for Moab, his fugitives reach Zoar, Eglath-shelishiyah: The ascent of Luhith they ascend weeping; On the way to Horonaim they utter rending cries;

text.NRSV: the waters of Nimrim are a desolation; the grass is withered, the new growth fails, the verdure is no more.
text.Rotherham: For, the waters of Nimrim, shall become desolation,—For grass, hath dried up, Herbage hath failed, Green thing, hath not sprung up!
text.DRB: For the waters of Nemrim shall be desolate, for the grass is withered away, the spring is faded, all the greenness is perished.
text.KJV: 15:6 For the waters of Nimrim shall be desolate: for the hay is withered away, the grasse faileth, there is no greene thing.
text.NABRE: The waters of Nimrim have become a waste, The grass is withered, new growth is gone, nothing is green.

text.NRSV: Therefore the abundance they have acquired and what they have laid up they carry away over the Wadi of the Willows.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, the savings they had made and that which they had stored, Over the torrent-bed of the willows, shall they bear them.
text.DRB: According to the greatness of their work, is their visitation also: they shall lead them to the torrent of the willows .
text.KJV: 15:7 Therefore the abundance they haue gotten, and that which they haue laide vp, shall they cary away to the brooke of the willowes.
text.NABRE: So now whatever they have acquired or stored away they carry across the Wadi of the Poplars.

text.NRSV: For a cry has gone around the land of Moab; the wailing reaches to Eglaim, the wailing reaches to Beer-elim.
text.Rotherham: For the outcry hath gone round the boundary of Moab,—As far as Eglaim, the howling thereof, And to Beer-elim, the howling thereof.
text.DRB: For the cry is gone round about the border of Moab: the howling thereof unto Gallim, and unto the well of Elim the cry thereof.
text.KJV: 15:8 For the cry is gone round about the borders of Moab: the howling thereof vnto Eglaim, and the howling thereof vnto Beer-Elim.
text.NABRE: The cry has gone round the territory of Moab; As far as Eglaim his wailing, even at Beer-elim his wailing.

text.NRSV: For the waters of Dimon are full of blood; yet I will bring upon Dimon even more— a lion for those of Moab who escape, for the remnant of the land.
text.Rotherham: For the waters of Dimon, are full of blood, For I will lay upon Dimon new troubles,—To the escaped of Moab, the lions, Even to the survivors on the soil.
text.DRB: For the waters of Dibon are filled with blood: for I will bring more upon Dibon: the lion upon them that shall flee of Moab, and upon the remnant of the land.
text.KJV: 15:9 For the waters of Dimon shalbe full of blood: for I will bring more vpon Dimon, lyons vpon him that escapeth of Moab, and vpon the remnant of the land.
text.NABRE: The waters of Dimon are filled with blood, but I will bring still more upon Dimon: Lions for those who are fleeing from Moab and for those who remain in the land!

text.NRSV: Send lambs to the ruler of the land, from Sela, by way of the desert, to the mount of daughter Zion.
text.Rotherham: Send ye the lamb due to the ruler of the land, From Sela towards the desert,—Unto the mount of the daughter of Zion;
text.DRB: Send forth, O Lord, the lamb, the ruler of the earth, from Petra of the desert, to the mount of the daughter of Sion.
text.KJV: 16:1 Send ye the lambe to the ruler of the land from Sela to the wildernesse, vnto the mount of the daughter of Zion.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 Send them forth, hugging the earth like reptiles, from Sela across the desert, to the mount of daughter Zion.

text.NRSV: Like fluttering birds, like scattered nestlings, so are the daughters of Moab at the fords of the Arnon.
text.Rotherham: Yet shall it be that—Like wandering birds from a nest cast forth, Shall be the daughters of Moab, at the fords of Arnon.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass, that as a bird fleeing away, and as young ones flying out of the nest, so shall the daughters of Moab be in the passage of Arnon.
text.KJV: 16:2 For it shalbe that as a wandering bird cast out of the nest: so the daughters of Moab shalbe at the fordes of Arnon.
text.NABRE: Like flushed birds, like scattered nestlings, Are the daughters of Moab at the fords of the Arnon.

text.NRSV: “Give counsel, grant justice; make your shade like night at the height of noon; hide the outcasts, do not betray the fugitive;
text.Rotherham: Bring thou, in counsel, Execute thou judgment, Make as the night, thy shadow in the midst of high noon,—Hide thou the outcasts, The wanderer, do not thou reveal.
text.DRB: Take counsel, gather a council: make thy shadow as the night in the midday: hide them that flee, and betray not them that wander about.
text.KJV: 16:3 Take counsell, execute Iudgement, make thy shadow as the night in the middest of the nooneday, hide the outcastes, bewray not him that wandereth.
text.NABRE: Offer counsel, take their part; at high noon make your shade like the night; Hide the outcasts, do not betray the fugitives.

text.NRSV: let the outcasts of Moab settle among you; be a refuge to them from the destroyer.” When the oppressor is no more, and destruction has ceased, and marauders have vanished from the land,
text.Rotherham: Let mine own outcasts, sojourn with thee, O Moab, become thou a covert to them from the face of the spoiler,—For vanished is the oppressor, At an end is extortion, They who tread down have ceased out of the land.
text.DRB: My fugitives shall dwell with thee: O Moab, be thou a covert to them from the face of the destroyer: for the dust is at an end, the wretch is consumed: he hath failed, that trod the earth under foot.
text.KJV: 16:4 Let mine outcasts dwel with thee, Moab, be thou a couert to them from the face of the spoiler: for the extortioner is at an end, the spoiler ceaseth, the oppressours are consumed out of the land.
text.NABRE: Let the outcasts of Moab live with you, be their shelter from the destroyer. When there is an end to the oppressor, when destruction has ceased, and the marauders have vanished from the land,

text.NRSV: then a throne shall be established in steadfast love in the tent of David, and on it shall sit in faithfulness a ruler who seeks justice and is swift to do what is right.
text.Rotherham: So shall be established, in lovingkindness, a throne, And one shall sit thereon, in truth, in the home of David,—Judging—and seeking justice and speeding righteousness.
text.DRB: And a throne shall be prepared in mercy, and one shall sit upon it in truth in the tabernacle of David, judging and seeking judgment and quickly rendering that which is just.
text.KJV: 16:5 And in mercy shall the throne be established, and hee shal sit vpon it in trueth, in the tabernacle of Dauid, iudging and seeking iudgement, and hasting righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: A throne shall be set up in mercy, and on it shall sit in fidelity, in David’s tent, A judge upholding right, prompt to do justice.

text.NRSV: We have heard of the pride of Moab —how proud he is!— of his arrogance, his pride, and his insolence; his idle boasting is false.
text.Rotherham: We have heard of the arrogance of Moab, Proud exceedingly! His haughtiness and his arrogance and his passion, Not true, are his boastings.
text.DRB: We have heard of the pride of Moab, he is exceeding proud: his pride and his arrogancy, and his indignation is more than his strength.
