text.DRB: And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father's house, divers kinds of vessels, every little vessel, from the vessels of cups even to every instrument of music.
text.KJV: 22:24 And they shall hang vpon him all the glory of his fathers house, the offspring and the issue, all vessels of small quantitie: from the vessels of cups, euen to all the vessels of flagons.
text.NABRE: On him shall hang all the glory of his ancestral house: descendants and offspring, all the little dishes, from bowls to jugs.

text.NRSV: On that day, says the Lord of hosts, the peg that was fastened in a secure place will give way; it will be cut down and fall, and the load that was on it will perish, for the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: In that day,—Declareth Yahweh of hosts Shall the peg, give way, that was fastened in a sure place,—Yea it shall be cut off and fall And the burden that was upon it shall perish, For, Yahweh, hath spoken!
text.DRB: In that day, saith the Lord of hosts, shall the peg be removed, that was fastened in the sure place: and it shall be broken and shall fall: and that which hung thereon, shall perish, because the Lord hath spoken it.
text.KJV: 22:25 In that day, sayth the Lord of hostes, shall the naile that is fastened in the sure place, be remooued, and be cut downe and fall: and the burden that was vpon it shall bee cut off: for the Lord hath spoken it.
text.NABRE: On that day, says the Lord of hosts, the peg fixed in a firm place shall give way, break off and fall, and the weight that hung on it shall be done away with; for the Lord has spoken.

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning Tyre. Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for your fortress is destroyed. When they came from Cyprus, they learned of it.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Tyre,—Howl! ye ships of Tarshish, For it is laid too waste to be a haven to enter, From the land of Cyprus, hath it been unveiled to them.
text.DRB: The burden of Tyre. Howl, ye ships of the sea, for the house is destroyed, from whence they were wont to come: from the land of Cethim it is revealed to them.
text.KJV: 23:1 The burden of Tyre. Howle yee ships of Tarshish, for it is laide waste, so that there is no house, no entring in: from the land of Chittim it is reuealed to them.
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 Tyre and Sidon Oracle on Tyre: Wail, ships of Tarshish, for your port is destroyed; From the land of the Kittim the news reaches them.

text.NRSV: Be still, O inhabitants of the coast, O merchants of Sidon, your messengers crossed over the sea
text.Rotherham: Be dumb, ye inhabitants of the Coast,—Whom the merchants of Zidon, passing over the sea, once replenished;
text.DRB: Be silent, you that dwell in the island: the merchants of Sidon passing over the sea, have filled thee.
text.KJV: 23:2 Be still, yee inhabitants of the yle, thou whom the merchants of Zidon, that passe ouer the sea, haue replenished.
text.NABRE: Silence! you who dwell on the coast, you merchants of Sidon, Whose messengers crossed the sea

text.NRSV: and were on the mighty waters; your revenue was the grain of Shihor, the harvest of the Nile; you were the merchant of the nations.
text.Rotherham: Yea on mighty waters, was the grain of Shihor, The harvest of the Nile, was her increase,—And so she became a mart of nations.
text.DRB: The seed of the Nile in many waters, the harvest of the river is her revenue: and she is become the mart of the nations.
text.KJV: 23:3 And by great waters the seede of Sihor, the haruest of the riuer is her reuenew, and she is a mart of nations.
text.NABRE: over the deep waters, Whose revenue was the grain of Shihor, the harvest of the Nile, you who were the merchant among the nations.

text.NRSV: Be ashamed, O Sidon, for the sea has spoken, the fortress of the sea, saying: “I have neither labored nor given birth, I have neither reared young men nor brought up young women.”
text.Rotherham: Turn thou pale, O Zidon, For spoken hath the sea, the fortress of the sea saying,—I have neither been in pangs nor given birth I have neither brought up young men nor promoted virgins.
text.DRB: Be thou ashamed, O Sidon: for the sea speaketh, even the strength of the sea, saying: I have not been in labour, nor have I brought forth, nor have I nourished up young men, nor brought up virgins.
text.KJV: 23:4 Be thou ashamed, O Zidon; for the sea hath spoken, euen the strength of the sea, saying; I trauell not, nor bring foorth children, neither doe I nourish vp yong men, nor bring vp virgines.
text.NABRE: Be ashamed, Sidon, fortress on the sea, for the sea has spoken, “I have not been in labor, nor given birth, nor raised young men, nor reared young women.”

text.NRSV: When the report comes to Egypt, they will be in anguish over the report about Tyre.
text.Rotherham: Like the report of Egypt, They shall be in pangs at the like report of Tyre.
text.DRB: When it shall be heard in Egypt, they will be sorry when they shall hear of Tyre:
text.KJV: 23:5 As at the report concerning Egypt, so shal they be sorely pained at the report of Tyre.
text.NABRE: When the report reaches Egypt they shall be in anguish at the report about Tyre.

text.NRSV: Cross over to Tarshish; wail, O inhabitants of the coast!
text.Rotherham: Pass ye over to Tarshish,—Howl ye inhabitants of the Coast:
text.DRB: Pass over the seas, howl, ye inhabitants of the island.
text.KJV: 23:6 Passe ye ouer to Tarshish, howle ye inhabitants of the yle.
text.NABRE: Pass over to Tarshish, wail, you who dwell on the coast!

text.NRSV: Is this your exultant city whose origin is from days of old, whose feet carried her to settle far away?
text.Rotherham: Is this to you an exultation? Though from ancient days, is her antiquity, Yet shall her own feet carry her away, far off to dwell.
text.DRB: Is not this your city, which gloried from of old in her antiquity? her feet shall carry her afar off to sojourn.
text.KJV: 23:7 Is this your ioyous citie, whose antiquitie is of ancient dayes? her owne feete shall cary her afarre off to soiourne.
text.NABRE: Is this your exultant city, whose origin is from old, Whose feet have taken her to dwell in distant lands?

text.NRSV: Who has planned this against Tyre, the bestower of crowns, whose merchants were princes, whose traders were the honored of the earth?
text.Rotherham: Who hath purposed this, against Tyre, The bestower of crowns,—Whose merchants are princes, Her traders the honourable of the earth?
text.DRB: Who hath taken this counsel against Tyre, that was formerly crowned, whose merchants were princes, and her traders the nobles of the earth?
text.KJV: 23:8 Who hath taken this counsell against Tyre the crowning citie, whose merchants are princes, whose traffiquers are the honourable of the earth?
text.NABRE: Who has planned such a thing against Tyre, the bestower of crowns, Whose merchants are princes, whose traders are the earth’s honored men?

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts has planned it— to defile the pride of all glory, to dishonor all the honored of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh of hosts, hath purposed it,—To humble the pride of all beauty, To make of little esteem all the honourable of the earth.
text.DRB: The Lord of hosts hath designed it, to pull down the pride of all glory, and bring to disgrace all the glorious ones of the earth.
text.KJV: 23:9 The Lord of hostes hath purposed it, to staine the pride of all glory, and to bring into contempt all the honorable of the earth.
text.NABRE: The Lord of hosts has planned it, to disgrace the height of all beauty, to degrade all the honored of the earth.

text.NRSV: Cross over to your own land, O ships of Tarshish; there is no more restraint.
text.Rotherham: Pass through thy land as the Nile,—O daughter of Tarshish, there is no restraint any longer!
text.DRB: Pass thy land as a river, O daughter of the sea, thou hast a girdle no more.
text.KJV: 23:10 Passe through thy land as a riuer O daughter of Tarshish: there is no more strength.
text.NABRE: Cross to your own land, ship of Tarshish; the harbor is no more.

text.NRSV: He has stretched out his hand over the sea, he has shaken the kingdoms; the Lord has given command concerning Canaan to destroy its fortresses.
text.Rotherham: His hand, hath he stretched out over the sea, He hath shaken kingdoms,—Yahweh, hath given command against the Phoenician coast, To destroy her fortresses.
text.DRB: He stretched out his hand over the sea, he troubled kingdoms: the Lord hath given a charge against Chanaan, to destroy the strong ones thereof.
text.KJV: 23:11 He stretched out his hand ouer the sea, hee shooke the kingdomes: the Lord hath giuen a commandement against the merchant citie, to destroy the strong holdes thereof.
text.NABRE: His hand he stretches out over the sea, he shakes kingdoms; The Lord commanded the destruction of Canaan’s strongholds:

text.NRSV: He said: “You will exult no more, O oppressed virgin daughter Sidon; rise, cross over to Cyprus— even there you will have no rest.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore hath he said,—No more, again, do thou exult, Thou violated virgin daughter of Zidon,—To Cyprus, arise and pass over, Even there, shall one find thee no rest.
text.DRB: And he said: Thou shalt glory no more, O virgin daughter of Sidon, who art oppressed: arise and sail over to Cethim, there also thou shalt have no, rest.
text.KJV: 23:12 And he said, Thou shalt no more reioice, O thou oppressed virgin, daughter of Zidon: arise, passe ouer to Chittim, there also shalt thou haue no rest.
text.NABRE: Crushed, you shall exult no more, virgin daughter Sidon. Arise, pass over to the Kittim, even there you shall find no rest.

text.NRSV: Look at the land of the Chaldeans! This is the people; it was not Assyria. They destined Tyre for wild animals. They erected their siege-towers, they stripped her palaces bare, they made her a ruin.
text.Rotherham: Lo! the land of the Chaldeans, This is the people that was not, Assyria, founded it for the inhabitants of the desert,—They set up its siege-towers, They demolished its palaces Made it a ruin!
text.DRB: Behold the land of the Chaldeans, there was not such a people, the Assyrian founded it: they have led away the strong ones thereof into captivity, they have destroyed the houses thereof, they have brought it to ruin.
text.KJV: 23:13 Behold, the land of the Caldeans, this people was not till the Assyrian founded it for them that dwel in the wildernesse: they set vp the towers thereof, they raised vp the palaces thereof, and he brought it to ruine.
text.NABRE: Look at the land of the Chaldeans, the people that has ceased to be. Assyria founded it for ships, raised its towers, Only to tear down its palaces, and turn it into a ruin.

text.NRSV: Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for your fortress is destroyed.
text.Rotherham: Howl, ye ships of Tarshish,—For laid waste is your fortress.
text.DRB: Howl, O ye ships of the sea, for your strength is laid waste.
text.KJV: 23:14 Howle ye ships of Tarshish: for your strength is laid waste.
text.NABRE: Lament, ships of Tarshish, for your stronghold is destroyed.

text.NRSV: From that day Tyre will be forgotten for seventy years, like the lifetime of one king. At the end of seventy years, it will happen to Tyre as in the song of the prostitute:
text.Rotherham: So shall it be in that day, That Tyre shall be forgotten seventy years, According to the days of a certain king: At the end of seventy years, shall it befall Tyre according to the song of the harlot:
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass in that day that thou, O Tyre, shalt be forgotten, seventy years, according to the days of one king: but after seventy years, there shall be unto Tyre as the song of a harlot.
text.KJV: 23:15 And it shall come to passe in that day, that Tyre shall be forgotten seuentie yeeres according to the dayes of one king: after the end of seuentie yeeres shall Tyre sing as an harlot.
text.NABRE: On that day, Tyre shall be forgotten for seventy years, the lifetime of one king. At the end of seventy years, the song about the prostitute will be Tyre’s song:

text.NRSV: Take a harp, go about the city, you forgotten prostitute! Make sweet melody, sing many songs, that you may be remembered.
text.Rotherham: Take thou a lyre, Go round the city, O harlot forgotten,—Sweetly touch the strings Lengthen out the song, That thou mayest be called to mind.
text.DRB: Take a harp, go about the city, thou harlot that hast been forgotten: sing well, sing many a song, that thou mayst be remembered.
text.KJV: 23:16 Take an harpe, goe about the city thou harlot, that hast beene forgotten, make sweet melody, sing many songs, that thou mayest be remembred.
text.NABRE: Take a harp, go about the city, forgotten prostitute; Pluck the strings skillfully, sing many songs, that you may be remembered.

text.NRSV: At the end of seventy years, the Lord will visit Tyre, and she will return to her hire, and will prostitute herself with all the kingdoms of the world on the face of the earth.
text.Rotherham: So shall it be, at the end of seventy years, That Yahweh will visit Tyre, And she will return to her hire,—Yea she will play the harlot—with all the kingdoms of the earth, upon the face of the ground.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass after seventy years, that the Lord will visit Tyre, and will bring her back again to her traffic: and she shall commit fornication again with all the kingdoms of the world upon the face of the earth.
text.KJV: 23:17 ¶ And it shall come to passe after the ende of seuentie yeeres, that the Lord will visite Tyre, and shee shall turne to her hire, and shall commit fornication with all the kingdomes of the world vpon the face of the earth.
text.NABRE: At the end of the seventy years the Lord shall visit Tyre. She shall return to her hire and serve as prostitute with all the world’s kingdoms on the face of the earth.

text.NRSV: Her merchandise and her hire will be dedicated to the Lord; it will not be stored or hoarded, but her merchandise will supply abundant food and fine clothing for those who live in the presence of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But her merchandise and her hire, shall be hallowed unto Yahweh, It shall not be stored up, nor hoarded,—For, to them who dwell before Yahweh, shall her merchandise belong, That they may eat to satisfaction And have stately apparel.
text.DRB: And her merchandise and her hire shall be sanctified to the Lord : they shall not be kept in store, nor laid up: for her merchandise shall be for them that shall dwell before the Lord, that they may eat unto fulness, and be clothed for a continuance.
text.KJV: 23:18 And her merchandize and her hire shall be holinesse to the Lord: it shall not be treasured nor laid vp: for her merchandize shalbe for them that dwell before the Lord, to eate sufficiently, and for durable clothing.
text.NABRE: But her merchandise and her hire shall be sacred to the Lord. It shall not be stored up or laid away; instead, her merchandise shall belong to those who dwell before the Lord, to eat their fill and clothe themselves in choice attire.

text.NRSV: See, the Lord is about to lay waste the earth and make it desolate, and he will twist its surface and scatter its inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: Lo! Yahweh emptying the earth and laying it waste,—And he will overturn the face thereof, And scatter them who dwell therein.
text.DRB: Behold the Lord shall lay waste the earth, and shall strip it, and shall afflict the face thereof, and scatter abroad the inhabitants thereof.
text.KJV: 24:1 Behold, the Lord maketh the earth emptie, and maketh it waste, and turneth it vpside downe, and scattereth abroad the inhabitants thereof.
text.NABRE: D. Apocalypse of Isaiah Chapter 24 Judgment upon the World and the Lord’s Enthronement on Mount Zion See! The Lord is about to empty the earth and lay it waste; he will twist its surface, and scatter its inhabitants:

text.NRSV: And it shall be, as with the people, so with the priest; as with the slave, so with the master; as with the maid, so with her mistress; as with the buyer, so with the seller; as with the lender, so with the borrower; as with the creditor, so with the debtor.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be—As the people, so, the priest, As the servant, so his lord, As the maid, so, her mistress,—As the buyer, so, the seller, As the lender, so, the borrower, As the debtor, so! his creditor.
text.DRB: And it shall be as with the people, so with the priest: and as with the servant, so with his master: as with the handmaid, so with her mistress: as with the buyer, so with the seller: as with the lender, so with the borrower: as with him that calleth for his money, so with him that oweth.
text.KJV: 24:2 And it shall be as with the people, so with the priest, as with the seruant, so with his master, as with the maid, so with her mistresse, as with the buyer, so with the seller, as with the lender, so with the borower, as with the taker of vsurie, so with the giuer of vsurie to him.
text.NABRE: People and priest shall fare alike: servant and master, Maid and mistress, buyer and seller, Lender and borrower, creditor and debtor.

text.NRSV: The earth shall be utterly laid waste and utterly despoiled; for the Lord has spoken this word.
text.Rotherham: Emptied—emptied—shall be the earth yea pillaged—pillaged,—For, Yahweh, hath spoken this word.
text.DRB: With desolation shall the earth be laid waste, and it shall be utterly spoiled: for the Lord hath spoken this word.
text.KJV: 24:3 The land shall be vtterly emptied, and vtterly spoiled: for the Lord hath spoken this word.
text.NABRE: The earth shall be utterly laid waste, utterly stripped, for the Lord has decreed this word.

text.NRSV: The earth dries up and withers, the world languishes and withers; the heavens languish together with the earth.
text.Rotherham: Mourneth, fadeth, the earth Languisheth, fadeth, the world,—Languished have the lofty of the people of the earth.
text.DRB: The earth mourned, and faded away, and is weakened: the world faded away, the height of the people of the earth is weakened.
text.KJV: 24:4 The earth mourneth and fadeth away, the world languisheth and fadeth away, the haughtie people of the earth doe languish.
text.NABRE: The earth mourns and fades, the world languishes and fades; both heaven and earth languish.

text.NRSV: The earth lies polluted under its inhabitants; for they have transgressed laws, violated the statutes, broken the everlasting covenant.
text.Rotherham: Yea the earth itself is profaned under them who dwell therein,—For they have Set aside laws, Gone beyond statute, Broken an age-abiding covenant.
text.DRB: And the earth is infected by the inhabitants thereof: because they have transgressed the laws, they have changed the ordinance, they have broken the everlasting covenant.
text.KJV: 24:5 The earth also is defiled vnder the inhabitants thereof: because they haue transgressed the lawes, changed the ordinance, broken the euerlasting couenant.
text.NABRE: The earth is polluted because of its inhabitants, for they have transgressed laws, violated statutes, broken the ancient covenant.

text.NRSV: Therefore a curse devours the earth, and its inhabitants suffer for their guilt; therefore the inhabitants of the earth dwindle, and few people are left.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, a curse, hath devoured the earth, And punished are the dwellers therein,—For this cause, are burned the inhabitants of the earth, And the men left remaining—are few.
text.DRB: Therefore shall a curse devour the earth, and the inhabitants thereof shall sin: and therefore they that dwell therein shall be mad, and few men shall be left.
text.KJV: 24:6 Therefore hath the curse deuoured the earth, and they that dwell therin are desolate: therefore the inhabitants of the earth are burned, and few men left.
text.NABRE: Therefore a curse devours the earth, and its inhabitants pay for their guilt; Therefore they who dwell on earth have dwindled, and only a few are left.

text.NRSV: The wine dries up, the vine languishes, all the merry-hearted sigh.
text.Rotherham: Mourneth the new wine. Withereth the vine,—Sighing are all the merryhearted:
text.DRB: The vintage hath mourned, the vine hath languished away, all the merryhearted have sighed.
text.KJV: 24:7 The new wine mourneth, the vine languisheth, all the merrie hearted doe sigh.
text.NABRE: The new wine mourns, the vine languishes, all the merry-hearted groan.

text.NRSV: The mirth of the tambourines is stilled, the noise of the jubilant has ceased, the mirth of the lyre is stilled.
text.Rotherham: Ceased hath the mirth of timbrels, Ended is the noise of the uproarious,—Ceased hath the mirth of the lyre:
text.DRB: The mirth of timbrels hath ceased, the noise of them that rejoice is ended, the melody of the harp is silent.
text.KJV: 24:8 The mirth of tabrets ceaseth, the noise of them that reioyce, endeth, the ioy of the harpe ceaseth.
text.NABRE: Stilled are the cheerful timbrels, ended the shouts of the jubilant, stilled the cheerful harp.

text.NRSV: No longer do they drink wine with singing; strong drink is bitter to those who drink it.
text.Rotherham: With a song, they drink not wine,—Bitter is strong drink, to them who drink it:
text.DRB: They shall not drink wine with a song: the drink shall be bitter to them that drink it.
text.KJV: 24:9 They shall not drinke wine with a song, strong drinke shall bee bitter to them that drinke it.
text.NABRE: They no longer drink wine and sing; strong brew is bitter to those who drink it.

text.NRSV: The city of chaos is broken down, every house is shut up so that no one can enter.
text.Rotherham: Broken down is the city of desolation,—Shut up every house that it cannot be entered.
text.DRB: The city of vanity is broken down, every house is shut up, no man cometh in.
text.KJV: 24:10 The city of confusion is broken downe: euery house is shut vp, that no man may come in.
text.NABRE: Broken down is the city of chaos, every house is shut against entry.

text.NRSV: There is an outcry in the streets for lack of wine; all joy has reached its eventide; the gladness of the earth is banished.
text.Rotherham: There is an outcry concerning wine in the streets,—Darkened is all joy, Departed the gladness of the earth.
text.DRB: There shall be a crying for wine in the streets: all mirth is forsaken: the joy of the earth is gone away.
text.KJV: 24:11 There is a crying for wine in the streets, all ioy is darkened, the mirth of the land is gone.
text.NABRE: In the streets they cry out for lack of wine; all joy has grown dim, cheer is exiled from the land.

text.NRSV: Desolation is left in the city, the gates are battered into ruins.
text.Rotherham: There is left in the city. desolation,—And to ruins, have been broken the gate.
text.DRB: Desolation is left in the city, and calamity shall oppress the gates.
text.KJV: 24:12 In the citie is left desolation, and the gate is smitten with destruction.
text.NABRE: In the city nothing remains but desolation, gates battered into ruins.

text.NRSV: For thus it shall be on the earth and among the nations, as when an olive tree is beaten, as at the gleaning when the grape harvest is ended.
text.Rotherham: When, thus, it shall be in the earth in the midst of the peoples, [There shall be] like the shaking of an olive-tree, like the going round to pick when closed is the harvest.
text.DRB: For it shall be thus in the midst of the earth, in the midst of the people, as if a few olives, that remain, should be shaken out of the olive tree: or grapes, when the vintage is ended.
text.KJV: 24:13 ¶ When thus it shalbe in the midst of the land among the people: there shall be as the shaking of an oliue tree, and as the gleaning grapes when the vintage is done.
text.NABRE: For thus it shall be in the midst of the earth, among the peoples, As when an olive tree has been beaten, as with a gleaning when the vintage is done.

text.NRSV: They lift up their voices, they sing for joy; they shout from the west over the majesty of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: They, shall lift up their voice—shall raise a tremulous note,—On account of the splendour of Yahweh, have they made a shrill cry, on the West;
text.DRB: These shall lift up their voice, and shall give praise: when the Lord shall be glorified, they shall make a joyful noise from the sea.
text.KJV: 24:14 They shal lift vp their voice, they shal sing, for the maiesty of the Lord, they shall crie aloud from the sea.
text.NABRE: These shall lift up their voice, they shall sing for joy in the majesty of the Lord, they shall shout from the western sea:

text.NRSV: Therefore in the east give glory to the Lord; in the coastlands of the sea, to the name of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, In the Regions of Light, give ye glory to Yahweh,—In the Coastlands of the Sea, unto the Name of Yahweh. God of Israel,
text.DRB: Therefore glorify ye the Lord in instruction: the name of the Lord God of Israel in the islands of the sea.
text.KJV: 24:15 Wherefore, glorifie ye the Lord in the fires, euen the Name of the Lord God of Israel in the yles of the Sea.
text.NABRE: “Therefore, in the east give glory to the Lord! In the coastlands of the sea, to the name of the Lord, the God of Israel!”

text.NRSV: From the ends of the earth we hear songs of praise, of glory to the Righteous One. But I say, I pine away, I pine away. Woe is me! For the treacherous betray, with much treachery the treacherous betray.
text.Rotherham: From the uttermost part of the earth, melodies, have we heard—Beauty, to the righteous one! But I had said—Ruin to me! Ruin to me! Woe to me! Traitors, have betrayed, Yea traitorously, have traitors betrayed!
text.DRB: From the ends of the earth we have heard praises, the glory of the just one. And I said: My secret to myself, my secret to myself, woe is me: the prevaricators have prevaricated, and with the prevarication of transgressors they have prevaricated.
text.KJV: 24:16 ¶ From the vttermost part of the earth haue we heard songs, euen glory to the righteous: but I said, My leannesse, my leannesse, woe vnto me: the treacherous dealers haue dealt treacherously, yea the treacherous dealers haue dealt very treacherously.
text.NABRE: From the end of the earth we hear songs: “Splendor to the Just One!” But I said, “I am wasted, wasted away. Woe is me! The traitors betray; with treachery have the traitors betrayed!

text.NRSV: Terror, and the pit, and the snare are upon you, O inhabitant of the earth!
text.Rotherham: Terror and pit and snare,—are upon thee, O inhabitant of the earth!
text.DRB: Fear, and the pit, and the snare are upon thee, O thou inhabitant of the earth.
text.KJV: 24:17 Feare, and the pit, & the snare are vpon thee, O inhabitant of the earth.
text.NABRE: Terror, pit, and trap for you, inhabitant of the earth!

text.NRSV: Whoever flees at the sound of the terror shall fall into the pit; and whoever climbs out of the pit shall be caught in the snare. For the windows of heaven are opened, and the foundations of the earth tremble.
text.Rotherham: So shall it be—He that fleeth from the sound of the terror! shall fall into the pit, And, he that getteth up out of the midst of the pit, shall be captured in the snare,—For, the windows on high, have opened, And shaken are the foundations of earth.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass, that he that shall flee from the noise of the fear, shall fall into the pit: and he that shall rid himself out of the pit, shall be taken in the snare: for the flood-gates from on high are opened, and the foundations of the earth shall be shaken.
text.KJV: 24:18 And it shall come to passe, that he who fleeth from the noise of the feare, shall fall into the pit; and he that commeth vp out of the midst of the pit, shalbe taken in the snare: for the windowes from on high are open, and the foundations of the earth doe shake.
text.NABRE: One who flees at the sound of terror will fall into the pit; One who climbs out of the pit will be caught in the trap. For the windows on high are open and the foundations of the earth shake.

text.NRSV: The earth is utterly broken, the earth is torn asunder, the earth is violently shaken.
text.Rotherham: The earth breaketh, breaketh,—The earth crasheth, crasheth, The earth tottereth, tottereth;
text.DRB: With breaking shall the earth be broken, with crushing shall the earth be crushed, with trembling shall the earth be moved.
text.KJV: 24:19 The earth is vtterly broken downe, the earth is cleane dissolued, the earth is moued exceedingly.
text.NABRE: The earth will burst asunder, the earth will be shaken apart, the earth will be convulsed.

text.NRSV: The earth staggers like a drunkard, it sways like a hut; its transgression lies heavy upon it, and it falls, and will not rise again.
text.Rotherham: The earth staggereth—staggereth like a drunken man, And rocketh to and fro like a night-hut,—So shall be heavy upon her, her transgression, And she shall fall and not again rise.
text.DRB: With shaking shall the earth be shaken as a drunken man, and shall be removed as the tent of one night: and the iniquity thereof shall be heavy upon it, and it shall fall, and not rise again.
text.KJV: 24:20 The earth shall reele to and fro, like a drunkard, and shall be remooued like a cottage, and the transgression thereof shall be heauie vpon it, and it shall fall, and not rise againe.
text.NABRE: The earth will reel like a drunkard, sway like a hut; Its rebellion will weigh it down; it will fall, never to rise again.”

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord will punish the host of heaven in heaven, and on earth the kings of the earth.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be in that day, That Yahweh will bring punishment Upon the host of the height in the height,—And upon the kings of the ground on the ground.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass, that in that day the Lord shall visit upon the host of heaven on high , and upon the kings of the earth, on the earth.
text.KJV: 24:21 And it shall come to passe in that day, that the Lord shall punish the hoste of the high ones that are on high, and the kings of the earth vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: On that day the Lord will punish the host of the heavens in the heavens, and the kings of the earth on the earth.

text.NRSV: They will be gathered together as prisoners in a pit; they will be shut up in a prison, and after many days they will be punished.
text.Rotherham: And they shall be swept together in a crowd, fettered for a pit, And shall be lowered into a dungeon,—And, after many days, shall they be punished.
text.DRB: And they shall be gathered together as in the gathering of one bundle into the pit, and they shall be shut up there in prison: and after many days they shall be visited.
text.KJV: 24:22 And they shalbe gathered together as prisoners are gathered in the pit, and shall be shut vp in the prison, and after many dayes shall they bee visited.
text.NABRE: They will be gathered together like prisoners into a pit; They will be shut up in a dungeon, and after many days they will be punished.

text.NRSV: Then the moon will be abashed, and the sun ashamed; for the Lord of hosts will reign on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, and before his elders he will manifest his glory.
text.Rotherham: Then shall blush the silvery moon, Then turn pale the glowing sun,—Because Yahweh of hosts hath become king In Mount Zion And in Jerusalem, And before his Elders in glory,
text.DRB: And the moon shall blush, and the sun shall be ashamed, when the Lord of hosts shall reign in mount Sion, and in Jerusalem, and shall be glorified in the sight of his ancients.
text.KJV: 24:23 Then the Moone shall be confounded, and the Sunne ashamed, when the Lord of hosts shall reigne in mount Zion and in Ierusalem, and before his ancients gloriously.
text.NABRE: Then the moon will blush and the sun be ashamed, For the Lord of hosts will reign on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, glorious in the sight of the elders.

text.NRSV: O Lord, you are my God; I will exalt you, I will praise your name; for you have done wonderful things, plans formed of old, faithful and sure.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh! my God, thou art, I will exalt thee, I will praise thy Name, For thou hast done a wonderful thing,—Purposes of long ago Faithfulness in truth.
text.DRB: O Lord, thou art my God, I will exalt thee, and give glory to thy name: for thou hast done wonderful things, thy designs of old faithful, amen.
text.KJV: 25:1 O Lord, thou art my God, I will exalt thee, I will praise thy Name; for thou hast done wonderfull things; thy counsels of old are faithfulnesse and trueth.
text.NABRE: Chapter 25 Praise for God’s Deliverance and the Celebration in Zion O Lord, you are my God, I extol you, I praise your name; For you have carried out your wonderful plans of old, faithful and true.

text.NRSV: For you have made the city a heap, the fortified city a ruin; the palace of aliens is a city no more, it will never be rebuilt.
text.Rotherham: For thou hast made, of a citadel, a mound, of a defenced city, a ruin,—palaces for foreigners to be no city, To times age-abiding, shall it not be built.
text.DRB: For thou hast reduced the city to a heap, the strong city to ruin, the house of strangers, to be no city, and to be no more built up for ever.
text.KJV: 25:2 For thou hast made of a citie, an heape; of a defenced city, a ruine: a palace of strangers, to be no citie, it shall neuer be built.
text.NABRE: For you have made the city a heap, the fortified city a ruin, The castle of the insolent, a city no more, not ever to be rebuilt.

text.NRSV: Therefore strong peoples will glorify you; cities of ruthless nations will fear you.
text.Rotherham: For this cause! shall glorify thee—a strong people, the city of tyrannous nations shall revere thee;
text.DRB: Therefore shall a strong people praise thee, the city of mighty nations shall fear thee.
text.KJV: 25:3 Therefore shall the strong people glorifie thee, the city of the terrible nations shall feare thee.
text.NABRE: Therefore a strong people will honor you, ruthless nations will fear you.

text.NRSV: For you have been a refuge to the poor, a refuge to the needy in their distress, a shelter from the rainstorm and a shade from the heat. When the blast of the ruthless was like a winter rainstorm,
text.Rotherham: For thou didst become A refuge to the weak. A refuge to the needy, when distress was upon him,—A shelter from the storm. A shade from the heat, When the blast of tyrants was like a storm against a wall.
text.DRB: Because thou hast been a strength to the poor, a strength to the needy in his distress: a refuge from the whirlwind, a shadow from the heat. For the blast of the mighty is like a whirlwind beating against a wall.
text.KJV: 25:4 For thou hast bene a strength to the poore, a strength to the needy in his distresse, a refuge from the storme, a shadow from the heat, when the blast of the terrible ones is as a storme against the wall.
text.NABRE: For you have been a refuge to the poor, a refuge to the needy in their distress; Shelter from the rain, shade from the heat. When the blast of the ruthless was like a winter rain,

text.NRSV: the noise of aliens like heat in a dry place, you subdued the heat with the shade of a cloud; the song of the ruthless was stilled.
text.Rotherham: As heat in a desert, the pomp of foreigners, wilt thou subdue,—Heat—with the shade of a cloud, The song of tyrants! become low.
text.DRB: Thou shalt bring down the tumult of strangers, as heat in thirst: and as with heat under a burning cloud, thou shalt make the branch of the mighty to wither away.
text.KJV: 25:5 Thou shalt bring downe the noise of strangers, as the heat in a dry place; euen the heat with the shadow of a cloud: the branch of the terrible ones shalbe brought low.
text.NABRE: the roar of strangers like heat in the desert, You subdued the heat with the shade of a cloud, the rain of the tyrants was vanquished.

text.NRSV: On this mountain the Lord of hosts will make for all peoples a feast of rich food, a feast of well-aged wines, of rich food filled with marrow, of well-aged wines strained clear.
text.Rotherham: Then will Yahweh of hosts prepare for all the peoples in this mountain, A banquet of fat things, A banquet of old wines,—Of fat things full of marrow, Of old wines well refined;
text.DRB: And the Lord of hosts shall make unto all people in this mountain, a feast of fat things, a feast of wine, of fat things full of marrow, of wine purified from the lees.
text.KJV: 25:6 ¶ And in this mountaine shall the Lord of hostes make vnto all people a feast of fat things, a feast of wines on the lees, of fat things full of marrow, of wines on the lees well refined.
text.NABRE: On this mountain the Lord of hosts will provide for all peoples A feast of rich food and choice wines, juicy, rich food and pure, choice wines.

text.NRSV: And he will destroy on this mountain the shroud that is cast over all peoples, the sheet that is spread over all nations; he will swallow up death forever.
text.Rotherham: And he will swallow up in this mountain, The mask of the veil, the veil that is upon all the peoples,—And the web that is woven over all the nations.
text.DRB: And he shall destroy in this mountain the face of the bond with which all people were tied, and the web that he began over all nations.
text.KJV: 25:7 And he wil destroy in this mountaine the face of the couering cast ouer all people, and the vaile that is spread ouer all nations.
text.NABRE: On this mountain he will destroy the veil that veils all peoples, The web that is woven over all nations.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord God will wipe away the tears from all faces, and the disgrace of his people he will take away from all the earth, for the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: Having swallowed up death itself victoriously, My Lord, Yahweh, will wipe away, tears from off all faces,—And the reproach of his own people, will he remove from off all the earth, For, Yahweh, hath spoken.
text.DRB: He shall cast death down headlong for ever: and the Lord God shall wipe away tears from every face, and the reproach of his people he shall take away from off the whole earth: for the Lord hath spoken it.
text.KJV: 25:8 He will swallow vp death in victorie, and the Lord God wil wipe away teares from off al faces, and the rebuke of his people shall he take away from off all the earth: for the Lord hath spoken it.
text.NABRE: He will destroy death forever. The Lord God will wipe away the tears from all faces; The reproach of his people he will remove from the whole earth; for the Lord has spoken.

text.NRSV: It will be said on that day, Lo, this is our God; we have waited for him, so that he might save us. This is the Lord for whom we have waited; let us be glad and rejoice in his salvation.
text.Rotherham: So shall it be said, in that day, Lo! our God, is this! We waited for him, that he might save us,—This, is Yahweh! We waited for him, Let us exult and rejoice in his salvation.
text.DRB: And they shall say in that day: Lo, this is our God, we have waited for him, and he will save us: this is the Lord, we have patiently waited for him, we shall rejoice and be joyfull in his salvation.
text.KJV: 25:9 ¶ And it shalbe said in that day, Loe, this is our God, we haue waited for him, and he will saue vs: this is the Lord, we haue waited for him, we wil be glad, and reioyce in his saluation.
text.NABRE: On that day it will be said: “Indeed, this is our God; we looked to him, and he saved us! This is the Lord to whom we looked; let us rejoice and be glad that he has saved us!”

text.NRSV: For the hand of the Lord will rest on this mountain. The Moabites shall be trodden down in their place as straw is trodden down in a dung-pit.
text.Rotherham: For the hand of Yahweh will settle down in this mountain,—Then shall Moab be trodden down in its place, Like the treading down of a strawheap in the water of a dunghill;
text.DRB: For the hand of the Lord shall rest in this mountain: and Moab shall be trodden down under him, as straw is broken in pieces with the wain.
text.KJV: 25:10 For in this mountaine shall the hand of the Lord rest, and Moab shalbe troden downe vnder him, euen as straw is troden downe for the dounghill.
text.NABRE: Judgment on Moab For the hand of the Lord will rest on this mountain, but Moab will be trodden down as straw is trodden down in the mire.

text.NRSV: Even if they spread out their hands in the midst of it, as swimmers spread out their hands to swim, their pride will be laid low despite the struggle of their hands.
text.Rotherham: Should he spread forth his hands in the midst thereof, As a swimmer spreadeth forth to swim, Then would be laid low his pride, together with the devices of his hands.
text.DRB: And he shall stretch forth his hands under him, as he that swimmeth stretcheth forth his hands to swim: and he shall bring down his glory with the dashing of his hands.
text.KJV: 25:11 And hee shall spread foorth his hands in the midst of them, as hee that swimmeth spreadeth foorth his hands to swimme: and hee shall bring downe their pride together with the spoiles of their hands.
text.NABRE: He will spread out his hands in its midst, as a swimmer spreads out his hands to swim; His pride will be brought low despite his strokes.

text.NRSV: The high fortifications of your walls will be brought down, laid low, cast to the ground, even to the dust.
text.Rotherham: Yea, the lofty stronghold of thy walls, Hath he brought down—Laid low—Levelled to the ground even unto the dust.
text.DRB: And the bulwarks of thy high walls shall fall, and be brought low, and shall be pulled down to the ground, even to the dust.
text.KJV: 25:12 And the fortresse of the high fort of thy walles shall hee bring downe, lay low, and bring to the ground, euen to the dust.
text.NABRE: The high-walled fortress he will raze, bringing it low, leveling it to the ground, to the very dust.

text.NRSV: On that day this song will be sung in the land of Judah: We have a strong city; he sets up victory like walls and bulwarks.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall be sung this song, in the land of Judah,—A strong city, have we! Salvation, will he set for walls and rampart,
text.DRB: In that day shall this canticle be sung the land of Juda. Sion the city of our strength a saviour, a wall and a bulwark shall be set therein.
text.KJV: 26:1 In that day shall this song bee sung in the land of Iudah; Wee haue a strong citie, saluation will God appoint for walles and bulwarkes.
text.NABRE: Chapter 26 Judah’s Praise and Prayer for Deliverance. On that day this song shall be sung in the land of Judah: “A strong city have we; he sets up victory as our walls and ramparts.

text.NRSV: Open the gates, so that the righteous nation that keeps faith may enter in.
text.Rotherham: Open ye the gates,—That there may enter in—a righteous nation preserving fidelity.
text.DRB: Open ye the gates, and let the just nation, that keepeth the truth, enter in.
text.KJV: 26:2 Open ye the gates, that the righteous nation which keepeth the trueth may enter in.
text.NABRE: Open up the gates that a righteous nation may enter, one that keeps faith.

text.NRSV: Those of steadfast mind you keep in peace—in peace because they trust in you.
text.Rotherham: A purpose sustained, thou wilt guard, [saying], Prosper! Prosper! Because in thee, hath he been led to trust.
text.DRB: The old error is passed away: thou wilt keep peace: peace, because we have hoped in thee.
text.KJV: 26:3 Thou wilt keepe him in perfect peace, whose minde is stayed on thee; because he trusteth in thee.
text.NABRE: With firm purpose you maintain peace; in peace, because of our trust in you.”

text.NRSV: Trust in the Lord forever, for in the Lord God you have an everlasting rock.
text.Rotherham: Trust ye in Yahweh, unto futurity,—For, in Yah, Yahweh, is a rock of ages.
text.DRB: You have hoped in the Lord for evermore, in the Lord God mighty for ever.
text.KJV: 26:4 Trust ye in the Lord for euer: for in the Lord Iehouah is euerlasting strength.
text.NABRE: Trust in the Lord forever! For the Lord is an eternal Rock.

text.NRSV: For he has brought low the inhabitants of the height; the lofty city he lays low. He lays it low to the ground, casts it to the dust.
text.Rotherham: For he hath brought down the inhabitants of the height the city exalted,—He layeth it low, Layeth it low even to the ground, Levelleth it, even to the dust:
text.DRB: For he shall bring down them that dwell on high, the high city he shall lay low. He shall bring it down even to the ground, he shall pull it down even to the dust.
text.KJV: 26:5 ¶ For hee bringeth downe them that dwell on high, the loftie citie he layeth it low; he layeth it low, euen to the ground, he bringeth it euen to the dust.
text.NABRE: He humbles those who dwell on high, the lofty city he brings down, Brings it down to the ground, levels it to the dust.

text.NRSV: The foot tramples it, the feet of the poor, the steps of the needy.
text.Rotherham: The foot trampleth it,—The feet of the lowly, The steps of the weak,
text.DRB: The foot shall tread it down, the feet of the poor, the steps of the needy.
text.KJV: 26:6 The foote shall treade it downe, euen the feete of the poore, and the steps of the needie.
text.NABRE: The feet of the needy trample on it— the feet of the poor.

text.NRSV: The way of the righteous is level; O Just One, you make smooth the path of the righteous.
text.Rotherham: The path of a righteous man, is, even,—O Upright One! the track of a righteous man, thou makest level.
text.DRB: The way of the just is right, the path of the just is right to walk in.
text.KJV: 26:7 The way of the iust is vprightnesse: thou most vpright, doest weigh the path of the iust.
text.NABRE: The way of the just is smooth; the path of the just you make level.

text.NRSV: In the path of your judgments, O Lord, we wait for you; your name and your renown are the soul’s desire.
text.Rotherham: Surely in the path of thy regulations, O Yahweh, we waited for thee,—Unto thy Name and unto thy Memorial, was there a longing of soul:
text.DRB: And in the way of thy judgments, O Lord, we have patiently waited for thee: thy name, and thy remembrance are the desire of the soul.
text.KJV: 26:8 Yea in the way of thy Iudgements, O Lord, haue we waited for thee; the desire of our soule is to thy Name, and to the remembrance of thee.
text.NABRE: The course of your judgments, Lord, we await; your name and your memory are the desire of our souls.

text.NRSV: My soul yearns for you in the night, my spirit within me earnestly seeks you. For when your judgments are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world learn righteousness.
text.Rotherham: With my soul, longed I for thee in the night, Yea with my spirit within me, I kept on searching for thee,—For, when thy regulations [extend] to the earth, The inhabitants of the world will have learned, righteousness.
text.DRB: My soul hath desired thee in the night: yea, and with my spirit within me in the morning early I will watch to thee. When thou shalt do thy judgments on the earth, the inhabitants of the world shall learn justice.
text.KJV: 26:9 With my soule haue I desired thee in the night, yea with my spirit within me will I seeke thee early: for when thy iudgements are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world will learne righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: My soul yearns for you at night, yes, my spirit within me seeks you at dawn; When your judgment comes upon the earth, the world’s inhabitants learn justice.

text.NRSV: If favor is shown to the wicked, they do not learn righteousness; in the land of uprightness they deal perversely and do not see the majesty of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Let favour be shewed to the lawless, he hath not learned righteousness, In a land of honest dealings, he acteth perversely,—And seeth not the splendour of Yahweh.
text.DRB: Let us have pity on the wicked, but he will not learn justice: in the land of the saints he hath done wicked things, and he shall not see the glory of the Lord.
text.KJV: 26:10 Let fauour be shewed to the wicked, yet will hee not learne righteousnesse: in the land of vprightnesse will he deale vniustly, and will not behold the maiestie of the Lord.
text.NABRE: The wicked, when spared, do not learn justice; in an upright land they act perversely, and do not see the majesty of the Lord.

text.NRSV: O Lord, your hand is lifted up, but they do not see it. Let them see your zeal for your people and be ashamed. Let the fire for your adversaries consume them.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh though thy hand be lifted up,—yet do they not see, Would they might see—and turn pale at a people’s zeal,—Surely, the fire of thine enemies, must consume them!
text.DRB: Lord, let thy hand be exalted, and let them not see: let the envious people see, and be confounded: and let fire devour thy enemies.
text.KJV: 26:11 Lord, when thy hand is lifted vp, they will not see: but they shall see, and be ashamed for their enuie at the people, yea the fire of thine enemies shall deuoure them.
text.NABRE: Lord, your hand is raised high, but they do not perceive it; Let them be put to shame when they see your zeal for your people: let the fire prepared for your enemies consume them.

text.NRSV: O Lord, you will ordain peace for us, for indeed, all that we have done, you have done for us.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, thou wilt ensure prosperity for us,—For even all our works, hast thou wrought for us,
text.DRB: Lord, thou wilt give us peace: for thou hast wrought all our works for us.
text.KJV: 26:12 ¶ Lord, thou wilt ordaine peace for vs: for thou also hast wrought all our workes in vs.
text.NABRE: Lord, you will decree peace for us, for you have accomplished all we have done.

text.NRSV: O Lord our God, other lords besides you have ruled over us, but we acknowledge your name alone.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, our God! Lords other than thee, have owned us,—By thyself alone, will we call upon thy Name.
text.DRB: O Lord our God, other lords besides thee have had dominion over us, only in thee let us remember thy name.
text.KJV: 26:13 O Lord our God, other lordes besides thee haue had dominion ouer vs: but by thee only will we make mention of thy Name.
text.NABRE: Lord, our God, lords other than you have ruled us; only because of you can we call upon your name.

text.NRSV: The dead do not live; shades do not rise—because you have punished and destroyed them, and wiped out all memory of them.
text.Rotherham: The dead, come not to life again, The shades, do not arise,—Therefore, thou hast visited and destroyed them, And caused to perish every memorial of them.
text.DRB: Let not the dead live, let not the giants rise again: therefore hast thou visited and destroyed them, and hast destroyed all their memory.
text.KJV: 26:14 They are dead, they shall not liue; they are deceased, they shall not rise: therefore hast thou visited and destroyed them, and made all their memory to perish.
text.NABRE: Dead they are, they cannot live, shades that cannot rise; Indeed, you have punished and destroyed them, and wiped out all memory of them.

text.NRSV: But you have increased the nation, O Lord, you have increased the nation; you are glorified; you have enlarged all the borders of the land.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast increased the nation, O Yahweh, Thou hast increased the nation thou hast gotten thyself glory, Thou hast extended far, all the ends of the land.
text.DRB: Thou hast been favourable to the nation, O Lord, thou hast been favourable to the nation: art thou glorified? thou hast removed all the ends of the earth far off.
text.KJV: 26:15 Thou hast increased the nation, O Lord, thou hast increased the nation, thou art glorified; thou hadst remooued it farre vnto all the ends of the earth.
text.NABRE: You have increased the nation, Lord, you have increased the nation, have added to your glory, you have extended far all the boundaries of the land.

text.NRSV: O Lord, in distress they sought you, they poured out a prayer when your chastening was on them.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh! in distress, they sought thee,—They poured out a whispered prayer, when thy chastening was upon them.
text.DRB: Lord, they have sought after thee in distress, in the tribulation of murmuring thy instruction was with them.
text.KJV: 26:16 Lord, in trouble haue they visited thee: they powred out a prayer when thy chastening was vpon them.
text.NABRE: Lord, oppressed by your punishment, we cried out in anguish under your discipline.

text.NRSV: As a woman with child draws near her time, writhes and cries out in her pangs, so were we because of you, O Lord;
text.Rotherham: Like as a woman with child—Draweth near to giving birth, Is in pain, Crieth out in her pangs So, were we before thee, O Yahweh;—
text.DRB: As a woman with child, when she draweth near the time of her delivery, is in pain, and crieth out in her pangs: so are we become in thy presence, O Lord.
text.KJV: 26:17 Like as a woman with childe that draweth neere the time of her deliuerie, is in paine and cryeth out in her pangs; so haue wee beene in thy sight, O Lord.
text.NABRE: As a woman about to give birth writhes and cries out in pain, so were we before you, Lord.

text.NRSV: we were with child, we writhed, but we gave birth only to wind. We have won no victories on earth, and no one is born to inhabit the world.
text.Rotherham: We were with child—We were in pain, As it were we brought forth wind,—Salvation, we could not accomplish for the earth, Neither were born the inhabitants of the world.
text.DRB: We have conceived, and been as it were in labour, and have brought forth wind: we have not wrought salvation on the earth, therefore the inhabitants of the earth have not fallen.
text.KJV: 26:18 Wee haue beene with childe, wee haue beene in paine, we haue as it were brought foorth winde, wee haue not wrought any deliuerance in the earth, neither haue the inhabitants of the world fallen.
text.NABRE: We conceived and writhed in pain, giving birth only to wind; Salvation we have not achieved for the earth, no inhabitants for the world were born.

text.NRSV: Your dead shall live, their corpses shall rise. Utterly awake and sing for joy, you who dwell in the dust! For your dew is a radiant dew, and the earth will give birth to those long dead.
text.Rotherham: Thy dead, shall come to life again, My dead body, they shall arise,—Awake and shout for joy, ye that dwell in the dust For, a dew of light, is thy dew, And, earth, to the shades shall give birth.
text.DRB: Thy dead men shall live, my slain shall rise again: awake, and give praise, ye that dwell in the dust: for thy dew is the dew of the light: and the land of the giants thou shalt pull down into ruin.
text.KJV: 26:19 Thy dead men shall liue, together with my dead body shall they arise: awake and sing yee that dwell in dust: for thy dewe is as the dewe of herbes, and the earth shall cast out the dead.
text.NABRE: But your dead shall live, their corpses shall rise! Awake and sing, you who lie in the dust! For your dew is a dew of light, and you cause the land of shades to give birth.

text.NRSV: Come, my people, enter your chambers, and shut your doors behind you; hide yourselves for a little while until the wrath is past.
text.Rotherham: Come my people, enter into thy chambers, And shut thy doors behind thee,—Hide thee as it were a little moment Till the indignation pass over.
text.DRB: Go, my people, enter into thy chambers, shut thy doors upon thee, hide thyself a little for a moment, until the indignation pass away.
text.KJV: 26:20 ¶ Come, my people, enter thou into thy chambers, and shut thy doores about thee; hide thy selfe as it were for a little moment, vntill the indignation be ouerpast.
text.NABRE: The Lord’s Response Go, my people, enter your chambers, and close the doors behind you; Hide yourselves for a brief moment, until the wrath is past.

text.NRSV: For the Lord is coming out of his place to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their iniquity; the earth will disclose the blood shed on it, and will no longer cover its slain.
text.Rotherham: For lo! Yahweh, is coming forth out of his place, To visit the iniquity of earth’s inhabitant upon him,—Therefore shall the earth unveil her shed-blood, And throw a covering, no longer over her slain.
text.DRB: For behold the Lord will come out of his place, to visit the iniquity of the inhabitant of the earth against him: and the earth shall disclose her blood, and shall cover her slain no more .
text.KJV: 26:21 For behold, the Lord commeth out of his place to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their iniquitie: the earth also shall disclose her blood, and shall no more couer her slaine.
text.NABRE: See, the Lord goes forth from his place, to punish the wickedness of the earth’s inhabitants; The earth will reveal the blood shed upon it, and no longer conceal the slain.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord with his cruel and great and strong sword will punish Leviathan the fleeing serpent, Leviathan the twisting serpent, and he will kill the dragon that is in the sea.
text.Rotherham: In that day, will Yahweh With his sword—the hard and the great and the strong, Bring punishment Upon Leviathan, the fleeing serpent, And upon Leviathan, the crooked serpent,—And will slay the monster which is in the sea.
text.DRB: In that day the Lord with his hard, and great, and strong sword shall visit leviathan the bar serpent, and leviathan the crooked serpent, and shall slay the whale that is in the sea.
text.KJV: 27:1 In that day the Lord with his sore and great and strong sworde shall punish Leuiathan the piercing serpent, euen Leuiathan that crooked serpent, and hee shall slay the dragon that is in the Sea.
text.NABRE: Chapter 27 The Judgment and Deliverance of Israel On that day, The Lord will punish with his sword that is cruel, great, and strong, Leviathan the fleeing serpent, Leviathan the coiled serpent; he will slay the dragon in the sea.

text.NRSV: On that day: A pleasant vineyard, sing about it!
text.Rotherham: In that day, A Wine-Vineyard! sing ye unto her:
text.DRB: In that day there shall be singing to the vineyard of pure wine.
text.KJV: 27:2 In that day, sing yee vnto her; A vineyard of red wine.
text.NABRE: On that day— The pleasant vineyard, sing about it!

text.NRSV: I, the Lord, am its keeper; every moment I water it. I guard it night and day so that no one can harm it;
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, am Watching over her, Every moment, will I water her,—Lest anyone injure her, Night and day, will I watch over her.
text.DRB: I am the Lord that keep it, I will suddenly give it drink : lest any hurt come to it, I keep it night and day.
text.KJV: 27:3 I the Lord doe keepe it; I will water it euery moment: lest any hurt it, I will keepe it night and day.
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, am its keeper, I water it every moment; Lest anyone harm it, night and day I guard it.

text.NRSV: I have no wrath. If it gives me thorns and briers, I will march to battle against it, I will burn it up altogether.
text.Rotherham: Fury, have I none,—Oh that there were delivered to me briars and thorns, in battle! I would march in among them I would set fire to them one and all.
text.DRB: There is no indignation in me : who shall make me a thorn and a brier in battle: shall march against it, shall I set it on fire together?
text.KJV: 27:4 Furie is not in mee: who would set the briars and thornes against me in battell? I would goe through them, I would burne them together.
text.NABRE: I am not angry. But if I were to find briers and thorns, In battle I would march against it; I would burn it all.

text.NRSV: Or else let it cling to me for protection, let it make peace with me, let it make peace with me.
text.Rotherham: Else, let one lay hold of my protection, Let him make peace with me,—Peace, let him make with me.
text.DRB: Or rather shall it take hold of my strength, shall it make peace with me, shall it make peace with me?
text.KJV: 27:5 Or let him take holde of my strength, that he may make peace with me, and he shall make peace with me.
text.NABRE: But if it holds fast to my refuge, it shall have peace with me; it shall have peace with me.

text.NRSV: In days to come Jacob shall take root, Israel shall blossom and put forth shoots, and fill the whole world with fruit.
text.Rotherham: In coming times, shall Jacob strike root, Israel, shall blossom and bud,—Then shall they fill the face of the world with fruit.
text.DRB: When they shall rush in unto Jacob, Israel shall blossom and bud, and they shall fill the face of the world with seed.
text.KJV: 27:6 Hee shall cause them that come of Iacob to take roote: Israel shall blossome and budde, and fill the face of the world with fruite.
text.NABRE: In days to come Jacob shall take root, Israel shall sprout and blossom, covering all the world with fruit.

text.NRSV: Has he struck them down as he struck those who struck them? Or have they been killed as those were killed who killed them?
text.Rotherham: Was it, with the smiting of his smiter, that he smote him? Or as with the slaying of his slayer, was he slain?
text.DRB: Hath he struck him according to the stroke of him that struck him? or is he slain, as he killed them that were slain by him?
text.KJV: 27:7 ¶ Hath hee smitten him, as hee smote those that smote him? or is hee slaine according to the slaughter of them that are slaine by him?
text.NABRE: Was he smitten as his smiter was smitten? Was he slain as his slayer was slain?

text.NRSV: By expulsion, by exile you struggled against them; with his fierce blast he swept them away in the day of the east wind.
text.Rotherham: By driving her away—by dismissing her, wouldest thou contend with her? He removed her by his rough wind in, a day of east wind.
text.DRB: In measure against measure, when it shall be cast off , thou shalt judge it. He hath meditated with his severe spirit in the day of heat.
text.KJV: 27:8 In measure when it shooteth foorth, thou wilt debate with it: hee stayeth his rough winde in the day of the East winde.
text.NABRE: Driving out and expelling, he struggled against it, carrying it off with his cruel wind on a day of storm.

text.NRSV: Therefore by this the guilt of Jacob will be expiated, and this will be the full fruit of the removal of his sin: when he makes all the stones of the altars like chalkstones crushed to pieces, no sacred poles or incense altars will remain standing.
text.Rotherham: Therefore hereby, shall a propitiatory—covering be put over the iniquity of Jacob, And all, this, is the fruit of taking away his sin,—When he maketh all the stones of an altar like chalk-stones that soon crumble, Sacred Stems and Sun Images, shall not arise.
text.DRB: Therefore upon this shall the iniquity of the house of Jacob be forgiven: and this is all the fruit, that the sin thereof should be taken away, when he shall have made all the stones of the altar, as burnt stones broken in pieces, the groves and temples shall not stand.
text.KJV: 27:9 By this therefore shall the iniquitie of Iacob be purged, and this is all the fruit, to take away his sinne: when he maketh all the stones of the Altar as chalke stones, that are beaten in sunder, the groues and images shall not stand vp.
text.NABRE: This, then, shall be the expiation of Jacob’s guilt, this the result of removing his sin: He shall pulverize all the stones of the altars like pieces of chalk; no asherahs or incense altars shall stand.

text.NRSV: For the fortified city is solitary, a habitation deserted and forsaken, like the wilderness; the calves graze there, there they lie down, and finish its boughs.
text.Rotherham: For the fortified city, is solitary, The dwelling forsaken and left as a wilderness,—There, shall the calf feed, And, there, lie down And shall consume the branches thereof:
text.DRB: For the strong city shall be desolate, the beautiful city shall be forsaken, and shall be left as a wilderness: there the calf shall feed, and there shall he lie down, and shall consume its branches.
text.KJV: 27:10 Yet the defenced citie shall be desolate, and the habitation forsaken, and left like a wildernesse: there shall the calfe feede, and there shall he lie downe, and consume the branches thereof.
text.NABRE: For the fortified city shall be desolate, an abandoned pasture, a forsaken wilderness; There calves shall graze, there they shall lie down, and consume its branches.

text.NRSV: When its branches are dry, they are broken; women come and make a fire of them. For this is a people without understanding; therefore he who made them will not have compassion on them, he who formed them will show them no favor.
text.Rotherham: When the cut-off boughs thereof are dry, they shall be broken to pieces, Women, coming, are kindling it! For it is not a people of understanding, For this cause, Will he that made him, not have compassion upon him, And, he that formed him, will shew him no favour.
text.DRB: Its harvest shall be destroyed with drought, women shall come and teach it: for it is not a wise people, therefore he that made it, shall not have mercy on it: and he that formed it, shall not spare it.
text.KJV: 27:11 When the boughes thereof are withered, they shall be broken off: the women come and set them on fire: for it is a people of no vnderstanding: therefore hee that made them will not haue mercie on them, and hee that formed them, will shewe them no fauour.
text.NABRE: When its boughs wither, they shall be broken off; and women shall come to kindle fires with them. For this is not an understanding people; therefore their maker shall not spare them; their creator shall not be gracious to them.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord will thresh from the channel of the Euphrates to the Wadi of Egypt, and you will be gathered one by one, O people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in that day, That Yahweh will beat off his fruit from the stream of the River [="Euphrates"], unto the torrent-valley of Egypt,—And, ye, shall be picked up, one by one O sons of Israel.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass, that in that day the Lord will strike from the channel of the river even to the torrent of Egypt, and you shall be gathered together one by one, O ye children of Israel.
text.KJV: 27:12 ¶ And it shall come to passe in that day, that the Lord shall beate off from the chanell of the riuer vnto the streame of Egypt, and ye shall bee gathered one by one, O ye children of Israel.
text.NABRE: On that day, The Lord shall beat out grain from the channel of the Euphrates to the Wadi of Egypt, and you shall be gleaned one by one, children of Israel.

text.NRSV: And on that day a great trumpet will be blown, and those who were lost in the land of Assyria and those who were driven out to the land of Egypt will come and worship the Lord on the holy mountain at Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, That there shall be a blowing with a great horn, Then shall come in Such as have wandered in the land of Assyria, And such as have been outcasts in the land of Egypt,—And they shall bow themselves down unto Yahweh In the holy mountain, In Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass, that in that day a noise shall be made with a great trumpet , and they that were lost, shall come from the land of the Assyrians, and they that were outcasts in the land of Egypt, and they shall adore the Lord in the holy mount in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 27:13 And it shall come to passe in that day, that the great trumpet shall bee blowen, and they shall come which were ready to perish in the land of Assyria, and the outcasts in the land of Egypt, and shall worship the Lord in the holy mount at Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: On that day, A great trumpet shall blow, and the lost in the land of Assyria and the outcasts in the land of Egypt Shall come and worship the Lord on the holy mountain, in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Ah, the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim, and the fading flower of its glorious beauty, which is on the head of those bloated with rich food, of those overcome with wine!
text.Rotherham: Alas! for the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim, And for his fading wreath of majestic beauty,—Which is on the head of the fertile valley, of them who are overcome with wine.
text.DRB: Woe to the crown of pride, to the drunkards of Ephraim , and to the fading flower the glory of his joy, who were on the head of the fat valley , staggering with wine.
text.KJV: 28:1 Woe to the crowne of pride, to the drunkards of Ephraim, whose glorious beauty is a fading flowre, which are on the head of the fat valleys of them that are ouercome with wine.
text.NABRE: E. The Lord Alone, Israel’s and Judah’s Salvation Chapter 28 The Fate of Samaria Ah! majestic garland of the drunkards of Ephraim, Fading blooms of his glorious beauty, at the head of the fertile valley, upon those stupefied with wine.

text.NRSV: See, the Lord has one who is mighty and strong; like a storm of hail, a destroying tempest, like a storm of mighty, overflowing waters, with his hand he will hurl them down to the earth.
text.Rotherham: Lo! My Lord hath one who is, strong and bold, Like a storm of hail, a destroying tempest, Like a storm of mighty waters overflowing, Hath he thrust it down to the earth with force:
text.DRB: Behold the Lord is mighty and strong, as a storm of hail: a destroying whirlwind, as the violence of many waters overflowing, and sent forth upon a spacious land.
text.KJV: 28:2 Behold, the Lord hath a mightie and strong one, which as a tempest of haile and a destroying storme, as a flood of mightie waters ouerflowing, shall cast downe to the earth with the hand.
text.NABRE: See, the Lord has a strong one, a mighty one, who, like an onslaught of hail, a destructive storm, Like a flood of water, great and overflowing, levels to the ground with violence;

text.NRSV: Trampled under foot will be the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim;
text.Rotherham: With the feet shall be trodden down, the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim,
text.DRB: The crown of pride of the drunkards of Ephraim shall be trodden under feet.
text.KJV: 28:3 The crowne of pride, the drunkards of Ephraim shall be troden vnder feete.
text.NABRE: With feet that will trample the majestic garland of the drunkards of Ephraim.

text.NRSV: and the fading flower of its glorious beauty, which is on the head of those bloated with rich food, will be like a first-ripe fig before the summer; whoever sees it, eats it up as soon as it is in hand.
text.Rotherham: So shall his fading wreath of majestic beauty, Which is on the head of the fertile valley, become—Like the first-ripe fig before fruit-harvest, Which when he that looketh upon it seeth while it is yet in his hand, he swalloweth it up.
text.DRB: And the fading flower the glory of his joy, who is on the head of the fat valley, shall be as a hasty fruit before the ripeness of autumn: which when he that seeth it shall behold, as soon as he taketh it in his hand, he will eat it up.
text.KJV: 28:4 And the glorious beautie which is on the head of the fat valley, shall bee a fading flowre, and as the hastie fruite before the summer: which when he that looketh vpon it, seeth it, while it is yet in his hand, he eateth it vp.
text.NABRE: The fading blooms of his glorious beauty at the head of the fertile valley Will be like an early fig before summer: whoever sees it, swallows it as soon as it is in hand.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord of hosts will be a garland of glory, and a diadem of beauty, to the remnant of his people;
text.Rotherham: In that day, will Yahweh of hosts become A crown of beauty, and A diadem of majesty,—Unto the remnant of his people:
text.DRB: In that day the Lord of hosts shall be a crown of glory, and a garland of joy to the residue of his people:
text.KJV: 28:5 ¶ In that day shall the Lord of hosts be for a crowne of glory, and for a diademe of beautie vnto the residue of his people:
text.NABRE: On that day the Lord of hosts will be a glorious crown And a brilliant diadem for the remnant of his people,

text.NRSV: and a spirit of justice to the one who sits in judgment, and strength to those who turn back the battle at the gate.
text.Rotherham: Even a spirit of justice—to him that presideth over justice, And strength to them who would turn back the battle at the gate.
text.DRB: And a spirit of judgment to him that sitteth in judgment, and strength to them that return out of the battle to the gate.
text.KJV: 28:6 And for a spirit of iudgement to him that sitteth in iudgement, and for strength to them that turne the battell to the gate.
text.NABRE: A spirit of judgment for the one who sits in judgment, And strength for those who turn back the battle at the gate.

text.NRSV: These also reel with wine and stagger with strong drink; the priest and the prophet reel with strong drink, they are confused with wine, they stagger with strong drink; they err in vision, they stumble in giving judgment.
text.Rotherham: But as for these, With wine, do they reel, and With strong drink, do they stagger,—Priest and prophet, reel with strong drink They are swallowed up through wine They stagger through strong drink, They reel in prophetic vision, They totter in pronouncing judgment.
text.DRB: But these also have been ignorant through wine, and through drunkenness have erred: the priest and the prophet have been ignorant through drunkenness, they are swallowed up with wine, they have gone astray in drunkenness, they have not known him that seeth, they have been ignorant of judgment.
text.KJV: 28:7 ¶ But they also haue erred through wine, and through strong drinke are out of the way: the priest and the prophet haue erred through strong drinke, they are swallowed vp of wine: they are out of the way through strong drinke, they erre in vision, they stumble in iudgement.
text.NABRE: Against Judah But these also stagger from wine and stumble from strong drink: Priest and prophet stagger from strong drink, overpowered by wine; They are confused by strong drink, they stagger in their visions, they totter when giving judgment.

text.NRSV: All tables are covered with filthy vomit; no place is clean.
text.Rotherham: For, all tables, are full of filthy vomit,—There is no place!
text.DRB: For all tables were full of vomit and filth, so that there was no more place.
text.KJV: 28:8 For all tables are full of vomite and filthinesse, so that there is no place cleane.
text.NABRE: Yes, all the tables are covered with vomit, with filth, and no place left clean.

text.NRSV: “To whom will he teach knowledge, and to whom will he explain the message? Those who are weaned from milk, those taken from the breast?
text.Rotherham: Whom, would he teach knowledge? And, whom, would he cause to understand the message? Them who are weaned from the milk? taken from the breasts?
text.DRB: Whom shall he teach knowledge? and whom shall he make to understand the hearing? them that are weaned from the milk, that are drawn away from the breasts.
text.KJV: 28:9 ¶ Whome shall he teach knowledge? and whom shall he make to vnderstand doctrine? them that are weaned from the milke, and drawen from the breasts.
text.NABRE: “To whom would he impart knowledge? To whom would he convey the message? To those just weaned from milk, those weaned from the breast?

text.NRSV: For it is precept upon precept, precept upon precept, line upon line, line upon line, here a little, there a little.”
text.Rotherham: For it is—Precept upon precept, precept upon precept, Line upon line, line upon line,—A little here a little there.
text.DRB: For command, command again ; command, command again; expect, expect again; expect, expect again: a little there, a little there.
text.KJV: 28:10 For precept must be vpon precept, precept vpon precept, line vpon line, line vpon line, here a litle, and there a litle.
text.NABRE: For he says, ‘Command on command, command on command, rule on rule, rule on rule, here a little, there a little!’”

text.NRSV: Truly, with stammering lip and with alien tongue he will speak to this people,
text.Rotherham: For, with a jabbering lip, and with an alien tongue, must he speak unto this people!
text.DRB: For with the speech of lips, and with another tongue he will speak to this people.
text.KJV: 28:11 For with stammering lips and another tongue will he speake to this people.
text.NABRE: Yes, with stammering lips and in a strange language he will speak to this people,

text.NRSV: to whom he has said, “This is rest; give rest to the weary; and this is repose”; yet they would not hear.
text.Rotherham: To whom he said—This, is the rest—give ye rest to the weary, and This, is the quietness,—But they were unwilling to hear.
text.DRB: To whom he said: This is my rest, refresh the weary, and this is my refreshing: and they would not hear.
text.KJV: 28:12 To whom he said, This is the rest wherwith ye may cause the weary to rest, and this is the refreshing, yet they would not heare.
text.NABRE: to whom he said: “This is the resting place, give rest to the weary; And this is the place of repose”— but they refused to hear.

text.NRSV: Therefore the word of the Lord will be to them, “Precept upon precept, precept upon precept, line upon line, line upon line, here a little, there a little;” in order that they may go, and fall backward, and be broken, and snared, and taken.
text.Rotherham: So the word of Yahweh must be to them—Precept upon precept, precept upon precept, Line upon line, line upon line, A little here, a little there,—That they may go and fall backward and be torn and snared and captured.
text.DRB: And the word of the Lord shall be to them: Command, command again; command, command again: expect, expect again; expect, expect again: a little there, a little there: that they may go, and fall backward, and be broken, and snared, and taken.
text.KJV: 28:13 But the word of the Lord was vnto them, precept vpon precept, precept vpon precept, line vpon line, line vpon line, here a litle and there a litle: that they might goe and fall backward, and be broken, and snared, and taken.
text.NABRE: So for them the word of the Lord shall be: “Command on command, command on command, Rule on rule, rule on rule, here a little, there a little!” So that when they walk, they shall stumble backward, broken, ensnared, and captured.

text.NRSV: Therefore hear the word of the Lord, you scoffers who rule this people in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, hear ye the word of Yahweh, Ye men who scoff,—Ye rulers of this people that is in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Wherefore hear the word of the Lord, ye scornful men, who rule over my people that is in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 28:14 ¶ Wherefore heare the worde of the Lord, yee scornefull men, that rule this people which is in Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: Therefore, hear the word of the Lord, you scoffers, who rule this people in Jerusalem:

text.NRSV: Because you have said, “We have made a covenant with death, and with Sheol we have an agreement; when the overwhelming scourge passes through it will not come to us; for we have made lies our refuge, and in falsehood we have taken shelter”;
text.Rotherham: Because ye have said—We have solemnised a covenant with death, And with hades, have we effected a vision,—The overflowing scourge when it sweepeth by, shall not reach unto us, For we have made lying our refuge. And in falsehood, have we hid ourselves,
text.DRB: For you have said: We have entered into a league with death, and we have made a covenant with hell. When the overflowing scourge shall pass through, it shall not come upon us: for we have placed our hope in lies, and by falsehood we are protected.
text.KJV: 28:15 Because ye haue said, Wee haue made a couenant with death, and with hell are we at agreement, when the ouerflowing scourge shall passe thorow, it shall not come vnto vs: for wee haue made lies our refuge, and vnder falsehood haue we hid our selues:
