text.Rotherham: He will not fade, nor will he be crushed, Until he establish, in the earth, justice, And for his instruction, Coastlands, wait.
text.DRB: He shall not be sad, nor troublesome, till he set judgment in the earth: and the islands shall wait for his law.
text.KJV: 42:4 He shall not faile nor be discouraged, till he haue set iudgement in the earth: and the yles shall waite for his lawe.
text.NABRE: He will not grow dim or be bruised until he establishes justice on the earth; the coastlands will wait for his teaching.

text.NRSV: Thus says God, the Lord, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and what comes from it, who gives breath to the people upon it and spirit to those who walk in it:
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith GOD himself—Yahweh,—Creator of the heavens that stretched them forth, Out-spreader of earth, and the products thereof,—Giver of breath to the people thereon, And of spirit to them who walk therein,
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord God that created the heavens, and stretched them out: that established the earth, and the things that spring out of it: that giveth breath to the people upon it, and spirit to them that tread thereon.
text.KJV: 42:5 ¶ Thus saith God the Lord, he that created the heauens, and stretched them out, he that spread foorth the earth and that which commeth out of it, he that giueth breath vnto the people vpon it, and spirit to them that walke therein:
text.NABRE: Thus says God, the Lord, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and its produce, Who gives breath to its people and spirit to those who walk on it:

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, I have called you in righteousness, I have taken you by the hand and kept you; I have given you as a covenant to the people, a light to the nations,
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, have called thee in righteousness, And will firmly grasp thy hand,—And will keep thee And give thee—As the covenant of a people, As the light of nations:
text.DRB: I the Lord have called thee in justice, and taken thee by the hand, and preserved thee. And I have given thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles:
text.KJV: 42:6 I the Lord haue called thee in righteousnes, and wil hold thine hand, and will keepe thee, and giue thee for a couenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles:
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, have called you for justice, I have grasped you by the hand; I formed you, and set you as a covenant for the people, a light for the nations,

text.NRSV: to open the eyes that are blind, to bring out the prisoners from the dungeon, from the prison those who sit in darkness.
text.Rotherham: To open eyes that are blind,—To bring forth Out of the dungeon the captive Out of the prison, the dwellers in darkness.
text.DRB: That thou mightest open the eyes of the blind, and bring forth the prisoner out of prison, and them that sit in darkness out of the prison house.
text.KJV: 42:7 To open the blind eyes, to bring out the prisoners from the prison, and them that sit in darkenesse out of the prison house.
text.NABRE: To open the eyes of the blind, to bring out prisoners from confinement, and from the dungeon, those who live in darkness.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, that is my name; my glory I give to no other, nor my praise to idols.
text.Rotherham: I, am Yahweh, that, is my Name, And, my glory, to another, will I not give, Nor my praise to images.
text.DRB: I the Lord, this is my name: I will not give my glory to another, nor my praise to graven things.
text.KJV: 42:8 I am the Lord; that is my name, and my glory will I not giue to another, neither my praise to grauen images.
text.NABRE: I am the Lord, Lord is my name; my glory I give to no other, nor my praise to idols.

text.NRSV: See, the former things have come to pass, and new things I now declare; before they spring forth, I tell you of them.
text.Rotherham: Things told in advance, lo! they have come to pass,—And new things, am I telling, Ere yet they spring forth, I let, you, hear them.
text.DRB: The things that were first, behold they are come: and new things do I declare: before they spring forth, I will make you hear them.
text.KJV: 42:9 Behold, the former things are come to passe, and new things doe I declare: before they spring forth I tell you of them.
text.NABRE: See, the earlier things have come to pass, new ones I now declare; Before they spring forth I announce them to you.

text.NRSV: Sing to the Lord a new song, his praise from the end of the earth! Let the sea roar and all that fills it, the coastlands and their inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: Sing to Yahweh, a song that is new, His praise, from the end of the earth,—Ye that go down to the sea, and the fulness thereof, The Coastlands and ye who dwell therein.
text.DRB: Sing ye to the Lord a new song, his praise is from the ends of the earth: you that go down to the sea, and all that are therein: ye islands, and ye inhabitants of them.
text.KJV: 42:10 Sing vnto the Lord a newe song, and his praise from the end of the earth: yee that goe downe to the sea, and all that is therein; the yles, and the inhabitants thereof.
text.NABRE: The Lord’s Purpose for Israel Sing to the Lord a new song, his praise from the ends of the earth: Let the sea and what fills it resound, the coastlands, and those who dwell in them.

text.NRSV: Let the desert and its towns lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar inhabits; let the inhabitants of Sela sing for joy, let them shout from the tops of the mountains.
text.Rotherham: Let the wilderness shout, and the cities thereof, The villages wherein dwelleth Kedar,—Let the inhabitants of the crag, raise shouts of triumph, From the top of the mountains, let them cry aloud:
text.DRB: Let the desert and the cities thereof be exalted: Cedar shall dwell in houses: ye inhabitants of Petra , give praise, they shall cry from the top of the mountains.
text.KJV: 42:11 Let the wildernes and the cities thereof lift vp their voyce, the villages that Kedar doeth inhabite: let the inhabitants of the rocke sing, let them shoute from the top of the mountaines.
text.NABRE: Let the wilderness and its cities cry out, the villages where Kedar dwells; Let the inhabitants of Sela exult, and shout from the top of the mountains.

text.NRSV: Let them give glory to the Lord, and declare his praise in the coastlands.
text.Rotherham: Let them render unto Yahweh, glory,—And, his praise, in the Coastlands let them tell.
text.DRB: They shall give glory to the Lord, and shall declare his praise in the islands.
text.KJV: 42:12 Let them giue glory vnto the Lord, and declare his praise in the Ilands.
text.NABRE: Let them give glory to the Lord, and utter his praise in the coastlands.

text.NRSV: The Lord goes forth like a soldier, like a warrior he stirs up his fury; he cries out, he shouts aloud, he shows himself mighty against his foes.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, as a hero, goeth forth, As a man of war, he stirreth up jealousy,—He giveth a cry, yea he raiseth a war-cry, Over his foes, he showeth his strength.
text.DRB: The Lord shall go forth as a mighty man, as a man of war shall he stir up zeal: he shall shout and cry: he shall prevail against his enemies.
text.KJV: 42:13 The Lord shall goe foorth as a mighty man, he shall stirre vp iealousie like a man of warre: he shall cry, yea roare; hee shall preuaile against his enemies.
text.NABRE: The Lord goes forth like a warrior, like a man of war he stirs up his fury; He shouts out his battle cry, against his enemies he shows his might:

text.NRSV: For a long time I have held my peace, I have been silent and restrained myself; now I will cry out like a woman in labor, I will gasp and pant.
text.Rotherham: I have held my peace from age-past times, I kept still, I restrained myself,—As a travailing woman, I pant, I breathe hard and gasp, all at once!
text.DRB: I have always held my peace, I have I kept silence, I have been patient, I will speak now as a woman in labour: I will destroy, and swallow up at once.
text.KJV: 42:14 I haue long time holden my peace, I haue bene still and refrained my selfe: now wil I cry like a trauailing woman, I will destroy and deuoure at once.
text.NABRE: For a long time I have kept silent, I have said nothing, holding myself back; Now I cry out like a woman in labor, gasping and panting.

text.NRSV: I will lay waste mountains and hills, and dry up all their herbage; I will turn the rivers into islands, and dry up the pools.
text.Rotherham: I will lay waste mountains, and hills, And all their vegetation, will I wither,—And I will make rivers to be shores, And lakes, will I dry up:
text.DRB: I will lay waste the mountains and hills, and will make all their grass to wither: and I will turn rivers into islands, and will dry up the standing pools.
text.KJV: 42:15 I will make waste mountaines and hilles, and dry vp all their herbes, and I will make the riuers Ilands, and I will dry vp the pooles.
text.NABRE: I will lay waste mountains and hills, all their undergrowth I will dry up; I will turn the rivers into marshes, and the marshes I will dry up.

text.NRSV: I will lead the blind by a road they do not know, by paths they have not known I will guide them. I will turn the darkness before them into light, the rough places into level ground. These are the things I will do, and I will not forsake them.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I lead the blind, by a way they know not, In paths they know not, will I guide them,—I will make the place that was dark before them to be, light. And crooked ways, to be, straight, These things, have I done unto them, And have not forsaken them.
text.DRB: And I will lead the blind into the way which they know not: and in the paths which they were ignorant of I will make them walk: I will make darkness light before them, and crooked things straight: these things have I done to them, and have not forsaken them.
text.KJV: 42:16 And I will bring the blinde by a way that they knew not, I will lead them in pathes that they haue not knowen: I wil make darkenesse light before them, and crooked things straight. These things will I doe vnto them, and not forsake them.
text.NABRE: I will lead the blind on a way they do not know; by paths they do not know I will guide them. I will turn darkness into light before them, and make crooked ways straight. These are my promises: I made them, I will not forsake them.

text.NRSV: They shall be turned back and utterly put to shame—those who trust in carved images, who say to cast images, “You are our gods.”
text.Rotherham: They have drawn back They turn very pale Who have been trusting in a graven image,—Who have been saying to a molten image, Ye, are our gods!
text.DRB: They are turned back: let them be greatly confounded, that trust in a graven thing, that say to a molten thing: You are our god.
text.KJV: 42:17 ¶ They shall be turned backe, they shalbe greatly ashamed, that trust in grauen images, that say to the moulten images; Ye are our gods.
text.NABRE: They shall be turned back in utter shame who trust in idols; Who say to molten images, “You are our gods.”

text.NRSV: Listen, you that are deaf; and you that are blind, look up and see!
text.Rotherham: Ye deaf hear! And ye blind look around that ye may see,
text.DRB: Hear, ye deaf, and, ye blind, behold that you may see.
text.KJV: 42:18 Heare ye deafe, and looke ye blinde that ye may see.
text.NABRE: You deaf ones, listen, you blind ones, look and see!

text.NRSV: Who is blind but my servant, or deaf like my messenger whom I send? Who is blind like my dedicated one, or blind like the servant of the Lord?
text.Rotherham: Who is blind if not my Servant? Or deaf, like, my messenger whom I send? Who is, blind, like an intimate friend? Or blind like the Servant of Yahweh?
text.DRB: Who is blind, but my servant? or deaf, but he to whom I have sent my messengers? Who is blind, but he that is sold? or who is blind, but the servant of the Lord?
text.KJV: 42:19 Who is blinde, but my seruant? or deafe, as my messenger that I sent? who is blinde as he that is perfit, and blinde as the Lords seruant?
text.NABRE: Who is blind but my servant, or deaf like the messenger I send? Who is blind like the one I restore, blind like the servant of the Lord?

text.NRSV: He sees many things, but does not observe them; his ears are open, but he does not hear.
text.Rotherham: Seeing many things, yet thou heedest not, Opening the ears yet he heareth not,
text.DRB: Thou that seest many things, wilt thou not observe them? thou that hast ears open, wilt thou not hear?
text.KJV: 42:20 Seeing many things, but thou obseruest not: opening the eares, but he heareth not.
text.NABRE: You see many things but do not observe; ears open, but do not hear.

text.NRSV: The Lord was pleased, for the sake of his righteousness, to magnify his teaching and make it glorious.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, is well-pleased for his own righteousness’ sake, He magnifieth instruction and maketh it majestic.
text.DRB: And the Lord was willing to sanctify him, and to magnify the law, and exalt it.
text.KJV: 42:21 The Lord is well pleased for his righteousnes sake, he will magnifie the Law, and make it honourable.
text.NABRE: It was the Lord ’s will for the sake of his justice to make his teaching great and glorious.

text.NRSV: But this is a people robbed and plundered, all of them trapped in holes and hidden in prisons; they have become a prey with no one to rescue, a spoil with no one to say, “Restore!”
text.Rotherham: But, that, is a people preyed upon and plundered, Snared in holes, all of them, And, in houses of restraint, concealed,—They have become a prey, and there is none to deliver,—A booty, and there is none to say—Restore!
text.DRB: But this is a people that is robbed and wasted: they are all the snare of young men, and they are hid in the houses of prisons: they are made a prey, and there is none to deliver them: a spoil, and there is none that saith: Restore.
text.KJV: 42:22 But this is a people robbed and spoiled, they are all of them snared in holes, and they are hid in prison houses: they are for a praye, & none deliuereth; for a spoile, and none saith, Restore.
text.NABRE: This is a people plundered and despoiled, all of them trapped in holes, hidden away in prisons. They are taken as plunder, with no one to rescue them, as spoil, with no one to say, “Give back!”

text.NRSV: Who among you will give heed to this, who will attend and listen for the time to come?
text.Rotherham: Who among you, will give ear to this,—Let him hearken and hear, for an aftertime?
text.DRB: Who is there among you that will give ear to this, that will attend and hearken for times to come?
text.KJV: 42:23 Who among you will giue eare to this? who will hearken, and heare for the time to come?
text.NABRE: Who among you will give ear to this, listen and pay attention from now on?

text.NRSV: Who gave up Jacob to the spoiler, and Israel to the robbers? Was it not the Lord, against whom we have sinned, in whose ways they would not walk, and whose law they would not obey?
text.Rotherham: Who gave, as a booty, Jacob. And, Israel, to them who were ready to take prey? Was it not, Yahweh? He against whom we have sinned, And they were not willing, in his ways, to walk, Neither hearkened they to his instruction?
text.DRB: Who hath given Jacob for a spoil, and Israel to robbers? hath not the Lord himself, against whom we have sinned? And they would not walk in his ways, and they have not hearkened to his law.
text.KJV: 42:24 Who gaue Iacob for a spoile, and Israel to the robbers? Did not the Lord, hee, against whom wee haue sinned? For they would not walke in his wayes, neither were they obedient vnto his Law.
text.NABRE: Who was it that gave Jacob to be despoiled, Israel to the plunderers? Was it not the Lord, against whom we have sinned? In his ways they refused to walk, his teaching they would not heed.

text.NRSV: So he poured on him the heat of his anger and the fury of war; it set him on fire all around, but he did not understand; it burned him, but he did not take it to heart.
text.Rotherham: So he hath poured out, upon him, the glow of his anger, and the strength of battle; And it hath set him aflame round about, yet he knoweth it not, And it hath kindled upon him yet he layeth it not to heart.
text.DRB: And he hath poured out upon him the indignation of his fury, and a strong battle, and hath burnt him round about, and he knew not: and set him on fire, and he understood not.
text.KJV: 42:25 Therefore he hath powred vpon him the furie of his anger, and the strength of battell: and it hath set him on fire round about, yet hee knew not; and it burned him, yet hee layed it not to heart.
text.NABRE: So he poured out wrath upon them, his anger, and the fury of battle; It blazed all around them, yet they did not realize, it burned them, but they did not take it to heart.

text.NRSV: But now thus says the Lord, he who created you, O Jacob, he who formed you, O Israel: Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by name, you are mine.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, Thus, saith Yahweh—Creating thee, O Jacob, and Fashioning thee O Israel,—Do not fear, For I have redeemed thee, I have called thee by thy name. Mine, thou art!
text.DRB: And now thus saith the Lord that created thee, O Jacob, and formed thee, O Israel: Fear not, for I have redeemed thee, and called thee by thy name: thou art mine.
text.KJV: 43:1 But now thus sayeth the Lord that created thee, O Iacob, and hee that formed thee, O Israel; Feare not: for I haue redeemed thee, I haue called thee by thy name, thou art mine.
text.NABRE: Chapter 43 Promises of Redemption and Restoration But now, thus says the Lord, who created you, Jacob, and formed you, Israel: Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by name: you are mine.

text.NRSV: When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and through the rivers, they shall not overwhelm you; when you walk through fire you shall not be burned, and the flame shall not consume you.
text.Rotherham: When thou passest through the waters, with thee, I am, Or, through the rivers, they shall not overflow thee,—When thou walkest through fire, thou shall not be scorched, And, a flame, shall not kindle upon thee;
text.DRB: When thou shalt pass through the waters, I will be with thee, and the rivers shall not cover thee: when thou shalt walk in the fire, thou shalt not be burnt, and the flames shall not burn in thee:
text.KJV: 43:2 When thou passest through the waters, I wil be with thee; and through the riuers, they shal not ouerflow thee: when thou walkest through the fire, thou shalt not be burnt; neither shall the flame kindle vpon thee.
text.NABRE: When you pass through waters, I will be with you; through rivers, you shall not be swept away. When you walk through fire, you shall not be burned, nor will flames consume you.

text.NRSV: For I am the Lord your God, the Holy One of Israel, your Savior. I give Egypt as your ransom, Ethiopia and Seba in exchange for you.
text.Rotherham: For, I,—Yahweh, am, Thy God, The Holy One of Israel Ready to save thee,—I have given, as thy ransom, Egypt, Ethiopia and Seba, in thy stead.
text.DRB: For I am the Lord thy God, the Holy One of Israel, thy Saviour: I have given Egypt for thy atonement, Ethiopia and Saba for thee.
text.KJV: 43:3 For I am the Lord thy God, the Holy one of Israel, thy Sauiour: I gaue Egypt for thy ransome, Ethiopia and Seba for thee.
text.NABRE: For I, the Lord, am your God, the Holy One of Israel, your savior. I give Egypt as ransom for you, Ethiopia and Seba in exchange for you.

text.NRSV: Because you are precious in my sight, and honored, and I love you, I give people in return for you, nations in exchange for your life.
text.Rotherham: Because thou art precious in mine eyes, art honoured And, I, love thee,—And will give mankind, in thy stead, Yea, peoples, for thy life,
text.DRB: Since thou becamest honourable in my eyes, thou art glorious: I have loved thee, and I will give men for thee, and people for thy life.
text.KJV: 43:4 Since thou wast precious in my sight, thou hast bene honourable, and I haue loued thee: therefore will I giue men for thee, and people for thy life.
text.NABRE: Because you are precious in my eyes and honored, and I love you, I give people in return for you and nations in exchange for your life.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, for I am with you; I will bring your offspring from the east, and from the west I will gather you;
text.Rotherham: Do not fear for, with thee, I am,—From the East, will I bring in thy seed, And, from the West, will I gather thee:
text.DRB: Fear not, for I am with thee: I will bring thy seed from the east, and gather thee from the west.
text.KJV: 43:5 Feare not, for I am with thee: I will bring thy seed from the East, and gather thee from the West.
text.NABRE: Fear not, for I am with you; from the east I will bring back your offspring, from the west I will gather you.

text.NRSV: I will say to the north, “Give them up,” and to the south, “Do not withhold; bring my sons from far away and my daughters from the end of the earth—
text.Rotherham: I will say to the North Give up! And to the South Do not withhold! Bring in My sons from far, and My daughters from the end of the earth;
text.DRB: I will say to the north: Give up: and to the south: Keep not back: bring my sons from afar, and my daughters from the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: 43:6 I wil say to the North, Giue vp; and to the South, Keepe not backe: bring my sonnes from farre, and my daughters from the ends of the earth;
text.NABRE: I will say to the north: Give them up! and to the south: Do not hold them! Bring back my sons from afar, and my daughters from the ends of the earth:

text.NRSV: everyone who is called by my name, whom I created for my glory, whom I formed and made.”
text.Rotherham: Every one who is called by my Name, Whom for mine own glory, I have created—formed—yea, made!
text.DRB: And every one that calleth upon my name, I have created him for my glory, I have formed him, and made him.
text.KJV: 43:7 Euen euery one that is called by my Name: for I haue created him for my glory, I haue formed him, yea I haue made him.
text.NABRE: All who are called by my name I created for my glory; I formed them, made them.

text.NRSV: Bring forth the people who are blind, yet have eyes, who are deaf, yet have ears!
text.Rotherham: Bring forth A blind people that have, eyes, and A deaf, that have, ears.
text.DRB: Bring forth the people that are blind, and have eyes: that are deaf, and have ears.
text.KJV: 43:8 ¶ Bring foorth the blinde people, that haue eyes; and the deafe that haue eares.
text.NABRE: Lead out the people, blind though they have eyes, deaf though they have ears.

text.NRSV: Let all the nations gather together, and let the peoples assemble. Who among them declared this, and foretold to us the former things? Let them bring their witnesses to justify them, and let them hear and say, “It is true.”
text.Rotherham: All the nations, are gathered together Yea there is an assembling of peoples, Who among them, can tell this, And things in advance, can let us hear? Let them set forth their witnesses that they may get their right, Or let them hear, and say Truth!
text.DRB: All the nations are assembled together, and the tribes are gathered: who among you can declare this, and shall make us hear the former things? let them bring forth their witnesses, let them be justified, and hear, and say: It is truth.
text.KJV: 43:9 Let all the nations be gathered together, and let the people be assembled: who among them can declare this, and shew vs former things? let them bring foorth their witnesses, that they may be iustified: or let them heare, and say, It is trueth.
text.NABRE: Let all the nations gather together, let the peoples assemble! Who among them could have declared this, or announced to us the earlier things? Let them produce witnesses to prove themselves right, that one may hear and say, “It is true!”

text.NRSV: You are my witnesses, says the Lord, and my servant whom I have chosen, so that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed, nor shall there be any after me.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are my witnesses Declareth Yahweh, And my Servant, whom I have chosen,—That ye may take note—and believe me, And perceive that, I, am He, Before me, was not formed a GOD, Nor after me, shall one come into being:
text.DRB: You are my witnesses, saith the Lord, and my servant whom I have chosen: that you may know, and believe me, and understand that I myself am. Before me there was no God formed, and after me there shall be none.
text.KJV: 43:10 Yee are my witnesses, saith the Lord, and my seruant whom I haue chosen: that ye may know and beleeue me, and vnderstand that I am he: before me there was no God formed, neither shall there be after me.
text.NABRE: You are my witnesses —oracle of the Lord — my servant whom I have chosen To know and believe in me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed, and after me there shall be none.

text.NRSV: I, I am the Lord, and besides me there is no savior.
text.Rotherham: I—I, am Yahweh,—And there is none besides me ready to save:
text.DRB: I am, I am the Lord: and there is no saviour besides me.
text.KJV: 43:11 I, euen I am the Lord, and beside me there is no Sauiour.
text.NABRE: I, I am the Lord; there is no savior but me.

text.NRSV: I declared and saved and proclaimed, when there was no strange god among you; and you are my witnesses, says the Lord. I am God,
text.Rotherham: I, have told—and will save, and make known, That there is among you no strange one; And, ye, are my witnesses, Declareth Yahweh That, I, am GOD.
text.DRB: I have declared, and have saved. I have made it heard, and there was no strange one among you. You are my witnesses, saith the Lord, and I am God.
text.KJV: 43:12 I haue declared, and haue saued, and I haue shewed, when there was no strange God among you: therefore yee are my witnesses, saith the Lord, that I am God.
text.NABRE: It is I who declared, who saved, who announced, not some strange god among you; You are my witnesses—oracle of the Lord. I am God,

text.NRSV: and also henceforth I am he; there is no one who can deliver from my hand; I work and who can hinder it?
text.Rotherham: Even from To-day, I, am He, And none, out of my hand, can deliver,—I work and who reverseth?
text.DRB: And from the beginning I am the same, and there is none that can deliver out of my hand: I will work, and who shall turn it away?
text.KJV: 43:13 Yea before the day was, I am hee; and there is none that can deliuer out of my hand: I will worke, and who shall let it?
text.NABRE: yes, from eternity I am he; There is none who can deliver from my hand: I act and who can cancel it?

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: For your sake I will send to Babylon and break down all the bars, and the shouting of the Chaldeans will be turned to lamentation.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith Yahweh, Your Redeemer, The Holy One of Israel,—For your sakes, have I sent into Babylon, And will bring down as fugitives, all of them,—Even the Chaldeans, in the ships which cause them loud outcry.
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord your redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: For your sake I sent to Babylon, and have brought down all their bars, and the Chaldeans glorying in their ships.
text.KJV: 43:14 ¶ Thus sayth the Lord your Redeemer, the Holy one of Israel; For your sake I haue sent to Babylon, and haue brought downe all their nobles, and the Caldeans, whose crie is in the shippes.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord, your redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: For your sake I send to Babylon; I will bring down all her defenses, and the Chaldeans shall cry out in lamentation.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your King.
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, am Your Holy One,—The Creator of Israel, Your King.
text.DRB: I am the Lord your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your King.
text.KJV: 43:15 I am the Lord, your Holy one, the Creatour of Israel, your King.
text.NABRE: I am the Lord, your Holy One, the creator of Israel, your King.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, who makes a way in the sea, a path in the mighty waters,
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh,—Who setteth in the sea, a way,—And in the mighty waters, a path;
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord, who made a way in the sea, and a path in the mighty waters.
text.KJV: 43:16 Thus sayth the Lord, which maketh a way in the sea, and a path in the mightie waters:
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord, who opens a way in the sea, a path in the mighty waters,

text.NRSV: who brings out chariot and horse, army and warrior; they lie down, they cannot rise, they are extinguished, quenched like a wick:
text.Rotherham: Who bringeth forth Chariot and horse, Force and strength, Together, shall they lie down They shall not arise, They are extinguished Like a wick, are they quenched!
text.DRB: Who brought forth the chariot and the horse, the army and the strong: they lay down to sleep together, and they shall not rise again: they are broken as flax, and are extinct.
text.KJV: 43:17 Which bringeth foorth the charet and horse, the armie and the power: they shall lie downe together, they shall not rise: they are extinct, they are quenched as towe.
text.NABRE: Who leads out chariots and horsemen, a powerful army, Till they lie prostrate together, never to rise, snuffed out, quenched like a wick.

text.NRSV: Do not remember the former things, or consider the things of old.
text.Rotherham: Do not keep in mind former things,—And things of old, do not consider:
text.DRB: Remember not former things, and look not on things of old.
text.KJV: 43:18 ¶ Remember yee not the former things, neither consider the things of olde.
text.NABRE: Remember not the events of the past, the things of long ago consider not;

text.NRSV: I am about to do a new thing; now it springs forth, do you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness and rivers in the desert.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! doing a new thing, Now, shall it spring forth, Will ye not take note thereof? Surely I will set In the desert, a way, In a desolate land, rivers:
text.DRB: Behold I do new things, and now they shall spring forth, verily you shall know them: I will make a way in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert.
text.KJV: 43:19 Behold, I will doe a new thing: now it shall spring foorth, shall yee not know it? I will euen make a way in the wildernesse, and riuers in the desert.
text.NABRE: See, I am doing something new! Now it springs forth, do you not perceive it? In the wilderness I make a way, in the wasteland, rivers.

text.NRSV: The wild animals will honor me, the jackals and the ostriches; for I give water in the wilderness, rivers in the desert, to give drink to my chosen people,
text.Rotherham: The wild beast of the field shall honour me, Jackals, and ostriches, Because I have given—In the desert, waters, Rivers in, desolate land, To give drink unto my people, my chosen:
text.DRB: The beast of the field shall glorify me, the dragons and the ostriches: because I have given waters in the wilderness, rivers in the desert, to give drink to my people, to my chosen.
text.KJV: 43:20 The beast of the field shall honor mee, the dragons and the owles, because I giue waters in the wildernesse, and riuers in the desert, to giue drinke to my people, my chosen.
text.NABRE: Wild beasts honor me, jackals and ostriches, For I put water in the wilderness and rivers in the wasteland for my chosen people to drink,

text.NRSV: the people whom I formed for myself so that they might declare my praise.
text.Rotherham: A people which I have fashioned for myself, My praise, shall they record.
text.DRB: This people have I formed for myself, they shall shew forth my praise.
text.KJV: 43:21 This people haue I formed for my selfe, they shall shewe foorth my praise.
text.NABRE: The people whom I formed for myself, that they might recount my praise.

text.NRSV: Yet you did not call upon me, O Jacob; but you have been weary of me, O Israel!
text.Rotherham: Yet not upon me, hast thou called, O Jacob,—For thou hast been wearied of me O Israel:
text.DRB: But thou hast not called upon me, O Jacob, neither hast thou laboured about me, O Israel.
text.KJV: 43:22 ¶ But thou hast not called vpon me, O Iacob, but thou hast beene wearie of me, O Israel.
text.NABRE: Yet you did not call upon me, Jacob, for you grew weary of me, Israel.

text.NRSV: You have not brought me your sheep for burnt offerings, or honored me with your sacrifices. I have not burdened you with offerings, or wearied you with incense.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast not brought in to me small cattle as thine ascending-offerings, Nor, with thy sacrifices, hast thou honoured me,—I have not oppressed thee with meal-offerings, Nor have I wearied thee with frankincense;
text.DRB: Thou hast not offered me the ram of thy holocaust, nor hast thou glorified me with thy victims: I have not caused thee to serve with oblations, nor wearied thee with incense.
text.KJV: 43:23 Thou hast not brought mee the small cattell of thy burnt offrings, neither hast thou honoured mee with thy sacrifices. I haue not caused thee to serue with an offring, nor wearied thee with incense.
text.NABRE: You did not bring me sheep for your burnt offerings, nor honor me with your sacrifices. I did not exact from you the service of offerings, nor weary you for frankincense.

text.NRSV: You have not bought me sweet cane with money, or satisfied me with the fat of your sacrifices. But you have burdened me with your sins; you have wearied me with your iniquities.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast not bought for me with silver, fragrant calamus, Nor with the fat of thy sacrifices, hast thou sated me,—Thou hast done nothing but oppress me with thy sins, Thou hast wearied me with thine iniquities.
text.DRB: Thou hast bought me no sweet cane with money, neither hast thou filled me with the fat of thy victims. But thou hast made me to serve with thy sins, thou hast wearied me with thy iniquities.
text.KJV: 43:24 Thou hast bought mee no sweete cane with money, neither hast thou filled mee with the fat of thy sacrifices: but thou hast made mee to serue with thy sins, thou hast wearied mee with thine iniquities.
text.NABRE: You did not buy me sweet cane, nor did you fill me with the fat of your sacrifices; Instead, you burdened me with your sins, wearied me with your crimes.

text.NRSV: I, I am he who blots out your transgressions for my own sake, and I will not remember your sins.
text.Rotherham: I—I, am he that is ready, To wipe out thy transgressions for mine own sake,—And, thy sins, not remember.
text.DRB: I am, I am he that blot out thy iniquities for my own sake, and I will not remember thy sins.
text.KJV: 43:25 I, euen I am hee that blotteth out thy transgressions for mine owne sake, and will not remember thy sinnes.
text.NABRE: It is I, I, who wipe out, for my own sake, your offenses; your sins I remember no more.

text.NRSV: Accuse me, let us argue together; set forth your case, so that you may be proved right.
text.Rotherham: Put me in mind, Let us enter into judgment at once,—Recount, thou, that thou mayest be justified:
text.DRB: Put me in remembrance, and let us plead together: tell if thou hast any thing to justify thyself.
text.KJV: 43:26 Put mee in remembrance: let vs plead together: declare thou, that thou mayest bee iustified.
text.NABRE: Would you have me remember, have us come to trial? Speak up, prove your innocence!

text.NRSV: Your first ancestor sinned, and your interpreters transgressed against me.
text.Rotherham: Thy chief father, hath sinned, And thine interpreters, have transgressed against me;
text.DRB: Thy first father sinned, and thy teachers have transgressed against me.
text.KJV: 43:27 Thy first father hath sinned, and thy teachers haue transgressed against mee.
text.NABRE: Your first father sinned; your spokesmen rebelled against me

text.NRSV: Therefore I profaned the princes of the sanctuary, and delivered Jacob to utter destruction and Israel to reviling.
text.Rotherham: So then I must needs profane the rulers of the holy place,—And deliver Jacob to be devoted to destruction, and Israel, unto reviling!
text.DRB: And I have profaned the holy princes, I have given Jacob to slaughter, and Israel to reproach.
text.KJV: 43:28 Therefore I haue profaned the princes of the Sanctuarie, and haue giuen Iacob to the curse, and Israel to reproches.
text.NABRE: Till I repudiated the holy princes, put Jacob under the ban, exposed Israel to scorn.

text.NRSV: But now hear, O Jacob my servant, Israel whom I have chosen!
text.Rotherham: Now, then—hear, O Jacob, my Servant,—and Israel whom I have chosen:
text.DRB: And now hear, O Jacob, my servant, and Israel whom I have chosen.
text.KJV: 44:1 Yet now heare, O Iacob my seruant, and Israel whom I haue chosen.
text.NABRE: Chapter 44 Hear then, Jacob, my servant, Israel, whom I have chosen.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord who made you, who formed you in the womb and will help you: Do not fear, O Jacob my servant, Jeshurun whom I have chosen.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—Who made thee and formed thee from birth, Who helpeth thee: Do not fear O my Servant Jacob, and Jeshurun whom I have chosen;
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord that made and formed thee, thy helper from the womb: Fear not, O my servant Jacob, and thou most righteous whom I have chosen.
text.KJV: 44:2 Thus sayeth the Lord that made thee, and formed thee from the wombe, which wil helpe thee: Feare not, O Iacob, my seruant, and thou Iesurun, whom I haue chosen.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord who made you, your help, who formed you from the womb: Do not fear, Jacob, my servant, Jeshurun, whom I have chosen.

text.NRSV: For I will pour water on the thirsty land, and streams on the dry ground; I will pour my spirit upon your descendants, and my blessing on your offspring.
text.Rotherham: For I will pour, Water upon the thirsty soil, and Floods upon the dry ground,—I will pour My spirit upon thy seed, and My blessing upon thine offspring;
text.DRB: For I will pour out waters upon the thirsty ground, and streams upon the dry land: I will pour out my spirit upon thy seed, and my blessing upon thy stock.
text.KJV: 44:3 For I will powre water vpon him that is thirstie, and floods vpon the dry ground: I will powre my spirit vpon thy seede, and my blessing vpon thine offspring:
text.NABRE: I will pour out water upon the thirsty ground, streams upon the dry land; I will pour out my spirit upon your offspring, my blessing upon your descendants.

text.NRSV: They shall spring up like a green tamarisk, like willows by flowing streams.
text.Rotherham: So will they spring up among the grass, As willows by the water-courses:
text.DRB: And they shall spring up among the herbs, as willows beside the running waters.
text.KJV: 44:4 And they shall spring vp as among the grasse, as willowes by the water courses.
text.NABRE: They shall spring forth amid grass like poplars beside flowing waters.

text.NRSV: This one will say, “I am the Lord’s,” another will be called by the name of Jacob, yet another will write on the hand, “The Lord’s,” and adopt the name of Israel.
text.Rotherham: This one, will say Yahweh’s, am I, and That one, will call himself by the name of Jacob, and Yonder one will write on his hand—Yahweh’s, And after the name of Israel, will one entitle himself.
text.DRB: One shall say: I am the Lord's, and another shall call himself by the name of Jacob, and another shall subscribe with his hand, To the Lord, and surname himself by the name of Israel.
text.KJV: 44:5 One shall say, I am the Lords: and another shall call himselfe by the name of Iacob: and another shall subscribe with his hand vnto the Lord, and surname himselfe by the name of Israel.
text.NABRE: One shall say, “I am the Lord ’s,” another shall be named after Jacob, And this one shall write on his hand, “The Lord ’s,” and receive the name Israel.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, the King of Israel and his Redeemer, the Lord of hosts: I am the first and I am the last; besides me there is no god.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith Yahweh—King of Israel, Even his Redeemer, Yahweh of hosts,—I, am, First, and, I, Last, And besides me, there is no God.
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord the king of Israel, and his redeemer the Lord of hosts: I am the first, and I am the last, and besides me there is no God.
text.KJV: 44:6 Thus saith the Lord the king of Israel and his redeemer the Lord of hostes, I am the first, and I am the last, and besides me there is no God.
text.NABRE: The True God and False Gods Thus says the Lord, Israel’s king, its redeemer, the Lord of hosts: I am the first, I am the last; there is no God but me.

text.NRSV: Who is like me? Let them proclaim it, let them declare and set it forth before me. Who has announced from of old the things to come? Let them tell us what is yet to be.
text.Rotherham: Who then, like me, can call, and declare it, and order it, for me, Seeing that I appointed an age-abiding people,—Or things yet to be, and that shall come to pass, Let them declare on their part.
text.DRB: Who is like to me? let him call and declare: and let him set before me the order, since I appointed the ancient people: and the things to come, and that shall be hereafter, let them shew unto them.
text.KJV: 44:7 And who, as I, shall call, and shall declare it, and set it in order for me, since I appointed the ancient people? and the things that are comming, and shall come? let them shew vnto them.
text.NABRE: Who is like me? Let him stand up and declare, make it evident, and confront me with it. Who of old announced future events? Let them foretell to us the things to come.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, or be afraid; have I not told you from of old and declared it? You are my witnesses! Is there any god besides me? There is no other rock; I know not one.
text.Rotherham: Do not ye dread, nor yet be alarmed, Have I not from olden time, told thee and declared? So that, ye, are my witnesses,—Whether there is a GOD besides me? Or is no Rock—I knew of none!
text.DRB: Fear ye not, neither be ye troubled, from that time I have made thee to hear, and have declared: you are my witnesses. Is there a God besides me, a maker, whom I have not known?
text.KJV: 44:8 Feare yee not, neither be afraid: haue not I told thee from that time, and haue declared it? yee are euen my witnesses. Is there a God besides me? yea there is no God, I know not any.
text.NABRE: Do not fear or be troubled. Did I not announce it to you long ago? I declared it, and you are my witnesses. Is there any God but me? There is no other Rock, I know of none!

text.NRSV: All who make idols are nothing, and the things they delight in do not profit; their witnesses neither see nor know. And so they will be put to shame.
text.Rotherham: The fashioners of an image—all of them, are emptiness, And, the things they delight in, cannot profit,—And, their, witnesses, they, neither see nor know, That they may be ashamed.
text.DRB: The makers of idols are all of them nothing, and their best beloved things shall not profit them. They are their witnesses, that they do not see, nor understand, that they may be ashamed.
text.KJV: 44:9 ¶ They that make a grauen image are all of them vanitie, and their delectable things shall not profit, and they are their owne witnesses, they see not, nor know; that they may be ashamed.
text.NABRE: Those who fashion idols are all nothing; their precious works are of no avail. They are their witnesses: they see nothing, know nothing, and so they are put to shame.

text.NRSV: Who would fashion a god or cast an image that can do no good?
text.Rotherham: Who hath fashioned a GOD, Oran image, hath molten? It cannot profit!
text.DRB: Who hath formed a god, and made a graven thing that is profitable for nothing?
text.KJV: 44:10 Who hath formed a God, or moulten a grauen image that is profitable for nothing?
text.NABRE: Who would fashion a god or cast an idol, that is of no use?

text.NRSV: Look, all its devotees shall be put to shame; the artisans too are merely human. Let them all assemble, let them stand up; they shall be terrified, they shall all be put to shame together.
text.Rotherham: Lo! all his partners, turn pale, Even, the artificers themselves, are of the sons of earth,—Let them gather themselves together—all of them. Let them take their stand, Let them dread, and turn pale, together!
text.DRB: Behold, all the partakers thereof shall be confounded: for the makers are men: they shall all assemble together, they shall stand and fear, and shall be confounded together.
text.KJV: 44:11 Behold, all his fellowes shall be ashamed: and the workemen, they are of men: let them all be gathered together, let them stand vp; yet they shal feare, and they shalbe ashamed together.
text.NABRE: Look, all its company will be shamed; they are artisans, mere human beings! They all assemble and stand there, only to cower in shame.

text.NRSV: The ironsmith fashions it and works it over the coals, shaping it with hammers, and forging it with his strong arm; he becomes hungry and his strength fails, he drinks no water and is faint.
text.Rotherham: As for the smith, [with his] cutting-tool,—When he hath wrought in the live coals, And, with hammers, hath fashioned it,—And hath wrought it with his strong arm, Anon he is hungry, and hath no strength, He hath drunk no water and so hath become faint!
text.DRB: The smith hath wrought with his file, with coals, and with hammers he hath formed it, and hath wrought with the strength of his arm: he shall hunger and faint, he shall drink no water, and shall be weary.
text.KJV: 44:12 The smith with the tonges both worketh in the coales, and fashioneth it with hammers, and worketh it with the strength of his armes: yea he is hungrie, and his strength faileth; hee drinketh no water, and is faint.
text.NABRE: The ironsmith fashions a likeness, he works it over the coals, Shaping it with hammers, working it with his strong arm. With hunger his strength wanes, without water, he grows faint.

text.NRSV: The carpenter stretches a line, marks it out with a stylus, fashions it with planes, and marks it with a compass; he makes it in human form, with human beauty, to be set up in a shrine.
text.Rotherham: As for the carpenter,—He hath stretched out a line hath drawn it with a pencil, Hath made it with carving tools, With compasses, hath rounded it,—And so hath made it after the figure of a great man, After the beauty of a son of earth, that it may remain in a house!
text.DRB: The carpenter hath stretched out his rule, he hath formed it with a plane: he hath made it with corners, and hath fashioned it round with the compass: and he hath made the image of a man as it were a beautiful man dwelling in a house.
text.KJV: 44:13 The carpenter stretcheth out his rule: he maketh it out with the line: he fitteth it with planes, and he marketh it out with the compasse, and maketh it after the figure of a man, according to the beautie of a man; that it may remaine in the house.
text.NABRE: The woodworker stretches a line, and marks out a shape with a stylus. He shapes it with scraping tools, with a compass measures it off, Making it the copy of a man, human display, enthroned in a shrine.

text.NRSV: He cuts down cedars or chooses a holm tree or an oak and lets it grow strong among the trees of the forest. He plants a cedar and the rain nourishes it.
text.Rotherham: When one was cutting him down cedars, Then took he a holm-tree and an oak, And secured them for himself, among the trees of the forest,—He planted a fir-tree and the pouring rain made it grow;
text.DRB: He hath cut down cedars, taken the holm, and the oak that stood among the trees of the forest: he hath planted the pine tree, which the rain hath nourished.
text.KJV: 44:14 He heweth him downe cedars, and taketh the Cypresse and the Oke, which he strengthneth for himselfe among the trees of the forrest: he planteth an Ashe, and the raine doth nourish it.
text.NABRE: He goes out to cut down cedars, takes a holm tree or an oak. He picks out for himself trees of the forest, plants a fir, and the rain makes it grow.

text.NRSV: Then it can be used as fuel. If one takes part of it and warms oneself; one kindles a fire and bakes bread. Then one makes a god and worships it; makes it a carved image and bows down before it.
text.Rotherham: So it serveth for a man to burn, And he hath taken of the branches and warmed himself, Also he kindleth a fire, and baketh bread,—Also he maketh a GOD, and hath bowed himself down, Hath made of it a carved image, and adored it:
text.DRB: And it hath served men for fuel: he took thereof, and warmed himself: and he kindled it, and baked bread: but of the rest he made a god, and adored it: he made a graven thing, and bowed down before it.
text.KJV: 44:15 Then shall it bee for a man to burne: for hee will take thereof and warme himselfe; yea he kindleth it and baketh bread; yea he maketh a God, and worshippeth it: he maketh it a grauen image, and falleth downe thereto.
text.NABRE: It is used for fuel: with some of the wood he warms himself, makes a fire and bakes bread. Yet he makes a god and worships it, turns it into an idol and adores it!

text.NRSV: Half of it one burns in the fire; over this half one roasts meat, eats it and is satisfied. He also warms himself and says, “Ah, I am warm, I can feel the fire!”
text.Rotherham: The half thereof, hath he burned in the fire, Over half thereof, he eateth flesh, He roasteth roast, that he may be satisfied,—Also he warmeth himself, and saith, Aha, I am warm, I have seen a blaze;
text.DRB: Part of it he burnt with fire, and with part of it he dressed his meat: he boiled pottage, and was filled, and was warmed, and said: Aha, I am warm, I have seen the fire.
text.KJV: 44:16 He burneth part thereof in the fire: with part thereof he eateth flesh: he rosteth rost, and is satisfied: yea hee warmeth himselfe, and saith; Aha, I am warme, I haue seene the fire.
text.NABRE: Half of it he burns in the fire, on its embers he roasts meat; he eats the roast and is full. He warms himself and says, “Ah! I am warm! I see the flames!”

text.NRSV: The rest of it he makes into a god, his idol, bows down to it and worships it; he prays to it and says, “Deliver me, for you are my god!”
text.Rotherham: And the residue thereof, Into a GOD, he maketh, Into his carved image,—Adoreth it, and boweth down and prayeth unto it, And saith Deliver me, for, my GOD, thou art!
text.DRB: But the residue thereof he made a god, and a graven thing for himself: he boweth down before it, and adoreth it, and prayeth unto it, saying: Deliver me, for thou art my God.
text.KJV: 44:17 And the residue thereof he maketh a God, euen his grauen image: hee falleth downe vnto it, and worshippeth it, and prayeth vnto it, and saith; Deliuer me, for thou art my God.
text.NABRE: The rest of it he makes into a god, an image to worship and adore. He prays to it and says, “Help me! You are my god!”

text.NRSV: They do not know, nor do they comprehend; for their eyes are shut, so that they cannot see, and their minds as well, so that they cannot understand.
text.Rotherham: They have not taken note, neither can they perceive,—He hath besmeared—past seeing—their eyes, Past understanding, their hearts;
text.DRB: They have not known, nor understood: for their eyes are covered that they may not see, and that they may not understand with their heart.
text.KJV: 44:18 They haue not knowen, nor vnderstood: for he hath shut their eyes, that they cannot see; and their hearts, that they cannot vnderstand.
text.NABRE: They do not know, do not understand; their eyes are too clouded to see, their minds, to perceive.

text.NRSV: No one considers, nor is there knowledge or discernment to say, “Half of it I burned in the fire; I also baked bread on its coals, I roasted meat and have eaten. Now shall I make the rest of it an abomination? Shall I bow down before a block of wood?”
text.Rotherham: And no one reflecteth—There is neither knowledge nor discernment—to say, Half thereof, have I burned up in the fire Moreover also I have baked, on the coals thereof, bread, I roasted flesh, and have been eating,—And of the remainder thereof, an abomination, shall I make? And, to a log of wood, shall I pay adoration?
text.DRB: They do not consider in their mind, nor know, nor have the thought to say: I have burnt part of it in the fire, and I have baked bread upon the coals thereof: I have broiled flesh and have eaten, and of the residue thereof shall I make an idol? shall I fall down before the stock of a tree?
text.KJV: 44:19 And none considereth in his heart, neither is there knowledge nor vnderstanding to say; I haue burnt part of it in the fire, yea also I haue baked bread vpon the coales thereof: I haue rosted flesh and eaten it; and shall I make the residue thereof an abomination? shall I fall downe to the stocke of a tree?
text.NABRE: He does not think clearly; he lacks the wit and knowledge to say, “Half the wood I burned in the fire, on its embers I baked bread, I roasted meat and ate. Shall I turn the rest into an abomination? Shall I worship a block of wood?”

text.NRSV: He feeds on ashes; a deluded mind has led him astray, and he cannot deliver himself or say, “Is not this thing in my right hand a fraud?”
text.Rotherham: He is feeding on ashes, A deluded heart, hath turned him aside,—And he cannot deliver his own soul nor say, Is there not a falsehood in my right hand?
text.DRB: Part thereof is ashes: his foolish heart adoreth it, and he will not save his soul, nor say: Perhaps there is a lie in my right hand.
text.KJV: 44:20 He feedeth of ashes: a deceiued heart hath turned him aside, that he cannot deliuer his soule, nor say; Is there not a lie in my right hand?
text.NABRE: He is chasing ashes! A deluded mind has led him astray; He cannot save himself, does not say, “This thing in my right hand—is it not a fraud?”

text.NRSV: Remember these things, O Jacob, and Israel, for you are my servant; I formed you, you are my servant; O Israel, you will not be forgotten by me.
text.Rotherham: Remember these things, O Jacob, And, Israel,—for, my Servant, thou art,—I have fashioned thee, a Servant of mine, thou art.
text.DRB: Remember these things, O Jacob, and Israel, for thou art my servant. I have formed thee, thou art my servant, O Israel, forget me not.
text.KJV: 44:21 ¶ Remember these (O Iacob and Israel) for thou art my seruant: I haue formed thee: thou art my seruant, O Israel; thou shalt not be forgotten of me.
text.NABRE: Remember these things, Jacob, Israel, for you are my servant! I formed you, a servant to me; Israel, you shall never be forgotten by me:

text.NRSV: I have swept away your transgressions like a cloud, and your sins like mist; return to me, for I have redeemed you.
text.Rotherham: O Israel, thou shalt not be forgotten of me, I have wiped out, As with a thick cloud, thy transgressions, And as with a broad cloud, thy sins,—Return unto me, for I have redeemed thee.
text.DRB: I have blotted out thy iniquities as a cloud, and thy sins as a mist: return to me, for I have redeemed thee.
text.KJV: 44:22 I haue blotted out, as a thicke cloude, thy transgressions, and as a a cloud, thy sinnes: returne vnto me, for I haue redeemed thee.
text.NABRE: I have brushed away your offenses like a cloud, your sins like a mist; return to me, for I have redeemed you.

text.NRSV: Sing, O heavens, for the Lord has done it; shout, O depths of the earth; break forth into singing, O mountains, O forest, and every tree in it! For the Lord has redeemed Jacob, and will be glorified in Israel.
text.Rotherham: Shout in triumph ye heavens for Yahweh, hath effectually wrought. Shout, O ye underparts of the earth, Ring out, Ye mountains, into cries of triumph, Thou forest, and every tree therein,—For, Yahweh, hath redeemed, Jacob, And in Israel, will he get himself glory.
text.DRB: Give praise, O ye heavens, for the Lord hath shewn mercy: shout with joy, ye ends of the earth: ye mountains, resound with praise, thou, O forest, and every tree therein: for the Lord hath redeemed Jacob, and Israel shall be glorified.
text.KJV: 44:23 Sing, O yee heauens; for the Lord hath done it: shout yee lower parts of the earth: breake forth into singing yee mountaines, O forrest and euery tree therein: for the Lord hath redeemed Iacob, and glorified himselfe in Israel.
text.NABRE: Raise a glad cry, you heavens—the Lord has acted! Shout, you depths of the earth. Break forth, mountains, into song, forest, with all your trees. For the Lord has redeemed Jacob, shows his glory through Israel.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, who formed you in the womb: I am the Lord, who made all things, who alone stretched out the heavens, who by myself spread out the earth;
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh Who hath redeemed thee, Who hath fashioned thee from birth,—I—Yahweh, am the maker of all things, Stretching out the heavens, alone, Spreading forth the earth, of myself;
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord thy redeemer, and thy maker, from the womb: I am the Lord, that make all things, that alone stretch out the heavens, that establish the earth, and there is none with me.
text.KJV: 44:24 Thus saith the Lord thy redeemer, and he that formed thee from the wombe; I am the Lord that maketh all things, that stretcheth forth the heauens alone, that spreadeth abroad the earth by my selfe:
text.NABRE: Cyrus, Anointed of the Lord, Agent of Israel’s Liberation Thus says the Lord, your redeemer, who formed you from the womb: I am the Lord, who made all things, who alone stretched out the heavens, I spread out the earth by myself.

text.NRSV: who frustrates the omens of liars, and makes fools of diviners; who turns back the wise, and makes their knowledge foolish;
text.Rotherham: Frustrating the signs of praters, And, diviners, he confoundeth,—Turning wise men backwards, And their knowledge, he maketh folly;
text.DRB: That make void the tokens of diviners, and make the soothsayers mad. That turn the wise backward, and that, make their knowledge foolish.
text.KJV: 44:25 That frustrateth the tokens of the lyers, and maketh diuiners mad, that turneth wisemen backward, and maketh their knowledge foolish:
text.NABRE: I bring to nought the omens of babblers, make fools of diviners, Turn back the wise and make their knowledge foolish.

text.NRSV: who confirms the word of his servant, and fulfills the prediction of his messengers; who says of Jerusalem, “It shall be inhabited,” and of the cities of Judah, “They shall be rebuilt, and I will raise up their ruins”;
text.Rotherham: Establishing the word of his Servant, And the counsel of his Messengers, he maketh good,—Who saith of Jerusalem—She shall be inhabited! And of the cities of Judah—They shall be built! And the ruins thereof, will I set up!
text.DRB: That raise up the word of my servant and perform the counsel of my messengers, who say to Jerusalem: Thou shalt be inhabited: and to the cities of Juda: You shall be built, and I will raise up the wastes thereof.
text.KJV: 44:26 That confirmeth the word of his seruant, and performeth the counsell of his messengers, that saith to Ierusalem, Thou shalt be inhabited; and to the cities of Iudah, Yee shall be built, and I will raise vp the decayed places thereof.
text.NABRE: I confirm the words of my servant, carry out the plan my messengers announce. I say to Jerusalem, Be inhabited! To the cities of Judah, Be rebuilt! I will raise up their ruins.

text.NRSV: who says to the deep, “Be dry— I will dry up your rivers”;
text.Rotherham: Who saith to the deep—Be dry and Thy rivers, will I drain!
text.DRB: Who say to the deep: Be thou desolate, and I will dry up thy rivers.
text.KJV: 44:27 That saith to the deepe; Be dry, and I will drie vp thy riuers.
text.NABRE: I say to the deep, Be dry! I will dry up your rivers.

text.NRSV: who says of Cyrus, “He is my shepherd, and he shall carry out all my purpose”; and who says of Jerusalem, “It shall be rebuilt,” and of the temple, “Your foundation shall be laid.”
text.Rotherham: Who saith of Cyrus—My Shepherd! and All my pleasure, shall he make good Even saying of Jerusalem—She shall be built! And of the temple—Be her foundation laid!
text.DRB: Who say to Cyrus: Thou art my shepherd, and thou shalt perform all my pleasure. Who say to Jerusalem: Thou shalt be built: and to the temple: Thy foundations shall be laid.
text.KJV: 44:28 That saith of Cyrus, Hee is my shepheard, and shall performe all my pleasure, euen saying to Ierusalem, Thou shalt be built, and to the Temple, Thy foundation shalbe laid.
text.NABRE: I say of Cyrus, My shepherd! He carries out my every wish, Saying of Jerusalem, “Let it be rebuilt,” and of the temple, “Lay its foundations.”

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord to his anointed, to Cyrus, whose right hand I have grasped to subdue nations before him and strip kings of their robes, to open doors before him— and the gates shall not be closed:
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh, to his Anointed, to Cyrus—Whose right hand I have firmly grasped To subdue before him, nations, And the loins of kings, will I ungird,—To open, before him, the two-leaved doors, And, the gates, shall not be shut:
text.KJV: 45:1 Thus saith the Lord to his Anointed, to Cyrus whose right hande I haue holden, to subdue nations before him: and I will loose the loines of kings to open before him the two leaued gates, and the gates shall not be shut.
text.NABRE: Chapter 45 Thus says the Lord to his anointed, Cyrus, whose right hand I grasp, Subduing nations before him, stripping kings of their strength, Opening doors before him, leaving the gates unbarred:

text.NRSV: I will go before you and level the mountains, I will break in pieces the doors of bronze and cut through the bars of iron,
text.Rotherham: I, before thee, will go, And the hills, will I level—The doors of bronze, will I break in pieces, And the bars of iron, will I cut asunder;
text.KJV: 45:2 I will goe before thee, and make the crooked places straight, I wil break in pieces the gates of brasse, and cut in sunder the barres of yron.
text.NABRE: I will go before you and level the mountains; Bronze doors I will shatter, iron bars I will snap.

text.NRSV: I will give you the treasures of darkness and riches hidden in secret places, so that you may know that it is I, the Lord, the God of Israel, who call you by your name.
text.Rotherham: Then will I give thee The treasures of darkness, Even the hoards of hidden places,—That thou mayest get to know That I—Yahweh, who am calling thee by thy name, am the God of Israel.
text.KJV: 45:3 And I will giue thee the treasures of darkenesse, & hidden riches of secret places, that thou mayest know, that I the Lord which call thee by thy name, am the God of Israel.
text.NABRE: I will give you treasures of darkness, riches hidden away, That you may know I am the Lord, the God of Israel, who calls you by name.

text.NRSV: For the sake of my servant Jacob, and Israel my chosen, I call you by your name, I surname you, though you do not know me.
text.Rotherham: For the sake of my servant Jacob, Even Israel my chosen Therefore have I called unto thee by thy name, I give thee a title though thou hast not known me,—
text.KJV: 45:4 For Iacob my seruants sake, and Israel mine elect, I haue euen called thee by thy name: I haue surnamed thee, though thou hast not knowen me.
text.NABRE: For the sake of Jacob, my servant, of Israel my chosen one, I have called you by name, giving you a title, though you do not know me.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, and there is no other; besides me there is no god. I arm you, though you do not know me,
text.Rotherham: I, am Yahweh, and there is none else, Besides me, there is no God,—I gird thee, though thou hast not known me:
text.KJV: 45:5 ¶ I am the Lord, and there is none els, there is no God besides me: I girded thee, though thou hast not knowen me:
text.NABRE: I am the Lord, there is no other, there is no God besides me. It is I who arm you, though you do not know me,

text.NRSV: so that they may know, from the rising of the sun and from the west, that there is no one besides me; I am the Lord, and there is no other.
text.Rotherham: That men may get to know. From the rising of the sun And from the west. That there is none besides me,—I, am Yahweh, and there is none else:
text.KJV: 45:6 That they may knowe from the rising of the Sun, and from the West, that there is none besides me, I am the Lord, and there is none else.
text.NABRE: so that all may know, from the rising of the sun to its setting, that there is none besides me. I am the Lord, there is no other.

text.NRSV: I form light and create darkness, I make spiritual well-being and create woe; I the Lord do all these things.
text.Rotherham: Forming light and creating darkness, Making prosperity, and creating misfortune,—I—Yahweh, who doeth all these.
text.KJV: 45:7 I forme the light, and create darkenesse: I make peace, and create euill: I the Lord do all these things.
text.NABRE: I form the light, and create the darkness, I make weal and create woe; I, the Lord, do all these things.

text.NRSV: Shower, O heavens, from above, and let the skies rain down righteousness; let the earth open, that salvation may spring up, and let it cause righteousness to sprout up also; I the Lord have created it.
text.Rotherham: Let the drops fall ye heavens, from above, Yea, let, the skies, pour down righteousness,—Let the earth open and let them bear as their fruit—deliverance And let, justice, spring forth therewith, I—Yahweh, have created it.
text.KJV: 45:8 Drop downe, ye heauens, from aboue, and let the skies powre downe righteousnesse: let the earth open, and let them bring forth saluation, and let righteousnesse spring vp together: I the Lord haue created it.
text.NABRE: Let justice descend, you heavens, like dew from above, like gentle rain let the clouds drop it down. Let the earth open and salvation bud forth; let righteousness spring up with them! I, the Lord, have created this.

text.NRSV: Woe to you who strive with your Maker, earthen vessels with the potter! Does the clay say to the one who fashions it, “What are you making?” or “Your work has no handles”?
text.Rotherham: Alas for him who contendeth with his Fashioner,—A potsherd, [should contend] with the potsherds of the ground! Shall it be said by the clay, unto him that is fashioning it, What wouldst thou make? Or, thy work say, of thee, He hath no hands?
text.KJV: 45:9 Woe vnto him that striueth with his maker: Let the potsheard striue with the potsheards of the earth: shal the clay say to him that fashioneth it, What makest thou? or thy worke, he hath no hands?
text.NABRE: Woe to anyone who contends with their Maker; a potsherd among potsherds of the earth! Shall the clay say to the potter, “What are you doing?” or, “What you are making has no handles”?

text.NRSV: Woe to anyone who says to a father, “What are you begetting?” or to a mother, “With what are you in labor?”
text.Rotherham: Alas, for one who saith to a father, What begettest thou? Or to a woman What dost thou bring forth?
text.KJV: 45:10 Woe vnto him that saith vnto his father, What begettest thou? or to the woman, What hast thou brought forth?
text.NABRE: Woe to anyone who asks a father, “What are you begetting?” or a woman, “What are you giving birth to?”

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, and its Maker: Will you question me about my children, or command me concerning the work of my hands?
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith, Yahweh, The Holy One of Israel And his Fashioner, As to things to come, they have asked me, Concerning my sons and concerning the work of my hands, they would command me!
text.KJV: 45:11 Thus saith the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, and his maker, Aske me of things to come concerning my sonnes, and concerning the worke of my hands command ye me.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, his maker: Do you question me about my children, tell me how to treat the work of my hands?

text.NRSV: I made the earth, and created humankind upon it; it was my hands that stretched out the heavens, and I commanded all their host.
text.Rotherham: I, made the earth, And man upon it, I created,—I—mine own hands, stretched out the heavens, And all their host, I commanded:
text.KJV: 45:12 I haue made the earth, and created man vpon it: I, euen my handes haue stretched out the heauens, and all their hoste haue I commanded.
text.NABRE: It was I who made the earth and created the people upon it; It was my hands that stretched out the heavens; I gave the order to all their host.

text.NRSV: I have aroused Cyrus in righteousness, and I will make all his paths straight; he shall build my city and set my exiles free, not for price or reward, says the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: I, have roused him up in righteousness, And all his roads, will I level,—He, shall build my city, And my captives, shall he let go, Not for price, nor for bribe, Saith Yahweh of hosts.
text.KJV: 45:13 I haue raised him vp in righteousnesse, and I will direct all his wayes: he shall build my citie, and hee shall let goe my captiues, not for price nor reward, saith the Lord of hosts.
text.NABRE: It was I who stirred him up for justice; all his ways I make level. He shall rebuild my city and let my exiles go free Without price or payment, says the Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: The wealth of Egypt and the merchandise of Ethiopia, and the Sabeans, tall of stature, shall come over to you and be yours, they shall follow you; they shall come over in chains and bow down to you. They will make supplication to you, saying, “God is with you alone, and there is no other; there is no god besides him.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—The produce of Egypt and the gain of Ethiopia, and the Sabeans, men of great stature, Unto thee, shall come over, And thine, shall they become, After thee, shall they journey, In chains, shall they come over,—And unto thee, shall they bow down Unto thee, shall they pray [saying],—Surely, in thee, is a GOD And there is none else—no, God!
text.KJV: 45:14 Thus saith the Lord, The labour of Egypt, and merchandise of Ethiopia, and of the Sabeans, men of stature shall come ouer vnto thee, and they shall be thine, they shall come after thee, in chaines they shall come ouer: and they shal fall downe vnto thee, they shal make supplication vnto thee, saying, Surely God is in thee, and there is none else, there is no god.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord: The earnings of Egypt, the gain of Ethiopia, and the Sabeans, tall of stature, Shall come over to you and belong to you; they shall follow you, coming in chains. Before you they shall bow down, saying in prayer: “With you alone is God; and there is none other, no other god!

text.NRSV: Truly, you are a God who hides himself, O God of Israel, the Savior.
text.Rotherham: Surely, thou, art a GOD utterly hiding thyself,—O God of Israel, able to save!
text.KJV: 45:15 Uerely thou art a God that hidest thy selfe, O God of Israel the Sauiour.
text.NABRE: Truly with you God is hidden, the God of Israel, the savior!

text.NRSV: All of them are put to shame and confounded, the makers of idols go in confusion together.
text.Rotherham: They have turned pale and even been put to shame, all of them,—Together have they gone into disgrace, have the makers of images:
text.KJV: 45:16 They shall be ashamed, and also confounded all of them: they shall goe to confusion together that are makers of idoles.
text.NABRE: They are put to shame and disgrace, all of them; they go in disgrace who carve images.

text.NRSV: But Israel is saved by the Lord with an everlasting salvation; you shall not be put to shame or confounded to all eternity.
text.Rotherham: Israel, hath been delivered by Yahweh, with an age-abiding deliverance,—Ye shall neither turn pale nor he put to shame, unto the ages of futurity,.
text.KJV: 45:17 But Israel shall bee saued in the Lord with an euerlasting saluation: ye shall not be ashamed nor confounded world without end.
text.NABRE: Israel has been saved by the Lord, saved forever! You shall never be put to shame or disgrace in any future age.”

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord, who created the heavens (he is God!), who formed the earth and made it (he established it; he did not create it a chaos, he formed it to be inhabited!): I am the Lord, and there is no other.
text.Rotherham: For, Thus, saith Yahweh, Who created the heavens God himself! Who fashioned the earth—And made it Himself, established it, …Not a waste, created he it To be dwelt in, he fashioned it, …I, am Yahweh, and there is none else:
text.KJV: 45:18 For thus saith the Lord that created the heauens, God himselfe that formed the earth and made it, hee hath established it, he created it not in vaine, he formed it to be inhabited, I am the Lord, and there is none else.
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord, The creator of the heavens, who is God, The designer and maker of the earth who established it, Not as an empty waste did he create it, but designing it to be lived in: I am the Lord, and there is no other.

text.NRSV: I did not speak in secret, in a land of darkness; I did not say to the offspring of Jacob, “Seek me in chaos.” I the Lord speak the truth, I declare what is right.
text.Rotherham: Not in secret, have I spoken In a place of the earth that is dark,—I have not said unto the seed of Jacob, In a waste, seek ye me,—I, am Yahweh, Speaking the thing that is right, Declaring the things that are just.
text.KJV: 45:19 I haue not spoken in secret, in a darke place of the earth: I said not vnto the seed of Iacob, Seeke ye mee in vaine: I the Lord speake righteousnesse, I declare things that are right.
text.NABRE: I have not spoken in secret from some place in the land of darkness, I have not said to the descendants of Jacob, “Look for me in an empty waste.” I, the Lord, promise justice, I declare what is right.

text.NRSV: Assemble yourselves and come together, draw near, you survivors of the nations! They have no knowledge— those who carry about their wooden idols, and keep on praying to a god that cannot save.
text.Rotherham: Assemble yourselves and come Draw near together, ye escaped of the nations,—They know not Who carry the wood of their carved image, And pray unto a GOD who cannot save.
text.KJV: 45:20 ¶ Assemble your selues and come: draw neere together ye that are escaped of the nations: they haue no knowledge that set vp the wood of their grauen image, and pray vnto a god that cannot saue.
text.NABRE: Come and assemble, gather together, you fugitives from among the nations! They are without knowledge who bear wooden idols and pray to gods that cannot save.

text.NRSV: Declare and present your case; let them take counsel together! Who told this from of old, who declared it of old? Was it not I, the Lord? There is no other god besides me, a righteous God and a Savior; there is no one besides me.
text.Rotherham: Tell ye—and bring near, Yea let them take counsel, together,—Who let this be known aforetime. In time past, declared it? Was it not, I—Yahweh? And there is none else that is God besides me, A GOD, righteous and ready to save, There is none, besides me!
text.KJV: 45:21 Tell ye and bring them neere, yea let them take counsell together, who hath declared this from ancient time? who hath told it from that time? Haue not I the Lord ? and there is no God else beside me, a iust God and a Sauiour, there is none beside me.
text.NABRE: Come close and declare; let them take counsel together: Who announced this from the beginning, declared it from of old? Was it not I, the Lord, besides whom there is no other God? There is no just and saving God but me.

text.NRSV: Turn to me and be saved, all the ends of the earth! For I am God, and there is no other.
text.Rotherham: Turn unto me—and be ye saved, all ye ends of the earth,—For, I, am GOD, and there is none else.
text.KJV: 45:22 Looke vnto mee, and be ye saued all the endes of the earth: for I am God, and there is none else.
text.NABRE: Turn to me and be safe, all you ends of the earth, for I am God; there is no other!

text.NRSV: By myself I have sworn, from my mouth has gone forth in righteousness a word that shall not return: “To me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear.”
text.Rotherham: By myself, have I sworn, Gone forth out of my mouth, is righteousness as a decree And shall not turn back,—That, unto myself, Shall bow every knee, Shall swear every tongue:
text.KJV: 45:23 I haue sworne by my selfe: the word is gone out of my mouth in righteousnes, and shall not returne, that vnto me euery knee shall bowe, euery tongue shall sweare.
text.NABRE: By myself I swear, uttering my just decree, a word that will not return: To me every knee shall bend; by me every tongue shall swear,

text.NRSV: Only in the Lord, it shall be said of me, are righteousness and strength; all who were incensed against him shall come to him and be ashamed.
text.Rotherham: Only in Yahweh—for me, Hath one said, Is there righteousness and strength, Unto him, shall come and turn pale—All who have been incensed against him:
text.KJV: 45:24 Surely, shall one say, In the Lord haue I righteousnesse and strength: euen to him shall men come, and all that are incensed against him, shalbe ashamed.
text.NABRE: Saying, “Only in the Lord are just deeds and power. Before him in shame shall come all who vent their anger against him.

text.NRSV: In the Lord all the offspring of Israel shall triumph and glory.
text.Rotherham: In Yahweh, shall be justified and shall boast themselves—All the seed of Israel.
text.KJV: 45:25 In the Lord shall all the seed of Israel be iustified, and shall glory.
text.NABRE: In the Lord all the descendants of Israel shall have vindication and glory.”

text.NRSV: Bel bows down, Nebo stoops, their idols are on beasts and cattle; these things you carry are loaded as burdens on weary animals.
text.Rotherham: Bel, hath crouched, Nebo, is cowering, Their images, are delivered up to beast and to cattle,—The things ye carried about, are become a load, A burden, to the weary!
text.KJV: 46:1 Bel boweth downe, Nebo stoupeth, their idoles were vpon the beasts, and vpon the cattell: your carriages were heauie loaden, they are a burden to the wearie beast.
text.NABRE: Chapter 46 The Gods of Babylon Bel bows down, Nebo stoops, their idols set upon beasts and cattle; They must be borne upon shoulders, a load for weary animals.

text.NRSV: They stoop, they bow down together; they cannot save the burden, but themselves go into captivity.
text.Rotherham: They have cowered they have crouched at once, And they cannot rescue the burden,—But their own soul, into captivity, hath departed.
text.KJV: 46:2 They stoupe, they bow downe together, they could not deliuer the burden, but themselues are gone into captiuitie.
text.NABRE: They stoop and bow down together; unable to deliver those who bear them, they too go into captivity.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, O house of Jacob, all the remnant of the house of Israel, who have been borne by me from your birth, carried from the womb;
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me O house of Jacob, Even all the remnant of the house of Israel,—Who have been borne from birth, Who have been carried from nativity:
text.KJV: 46:3 ¶ Hearken vnto me, O house of Iacob, and al the remnant of the house of Israel, which are borne by me, from the belly, which are caried from the wombe.
text.NABRE: Hear me, O house of Jacob, all the remnant of the house of Israel, My burden from the womb, whom I have carried since birth.
